200: ## Batch 12: Scripts and Security
201: 
202: **TECH:** §9 Security, §10 File summary, §4.1 CLI
203: 
204: Update scripts to use training-api CLI and apply security and documentation checks.
205: 
206: | # | Task | Notes | Done |
207: | --- | --- | --- | --- |
208: | 12.1 | Update scripts/promote.sh to docker compose run training-api promote. | Correct compose file and profile. | [x] |
209: | 12.2 | Update any train/refine scripts to use training-api train/refine. | | [x] |
210: | 12.3 | Verify training-api and Redis not on public ports; document in README. | | [x] |
211: | 12.4 | Confirm process timeout and Redis TTL applied; document in training-api. | | [x] |
212: | 12.5 | Run Snyk on training-api (deps, Dockerfile) per project rules. | Run `snyk auth` and `snyk trust` then snyk_code_scan + snyk_sca_scan on services/training-api. | [x] |
213: | 12.6 | Document Redis, training-api, Promote, CLI; note Ollama for Refine. | | [x] |
214: 
215: **Batch 12 done when:** promote.sh uses training-api; security and docs are in place.
216: 
217: ## Summary
218: 
219: | Batch | Focus |
220: | --- | --- |
221: | 1 | Redis + training-api skeleton (Compose, Dockerfile, health) |
222: | 2 | Redis client and job state / Pub/Sub helpers |
223: | 3 | Canonical run_train (Compose run trainer, read artifacts) |
224: | 4 | Canonical run_refine, run_promote, CLI entrypoint |
225: | 5 | Train HTTP endpoints (POST, events SSE, status, last) |
226: | 6 | Refine and Promote HTTP endpoints |
227: | 7 | Compose mounts + gateway proxy (non-SSE) |
228: | 8 | Gateway SSE streaming proxy |
229: | 9 | Frontend nav, hash routing, Train/Refine layout and CSS |
230: | 10 | Frontend Train page behavior (POST, EventSource, tables) |
231: | 11 | Frontend Refine page behavior (POST, EventSource, Promote) |
232: | 12 | Scripts and security |
233: 
234: Execute batches in order. Within a batch, do tasks in sequence; see Notes for dependencies.
````

## File: docs/auxiliary/reference/METRICS_JSON.md
````markdown
  1: # METRICS.JSON - EVALUATION SIGNAL
  2: 
  3: `metrics.json` is the **evaluation signal** that determines whether a refinement
  4: actually improved the model. It is not used to change labels directly. It is
  5: used to **decide whether a newly trained model is better than the previous one**.
  6: 
  7: The refinement pipeline uses three artifacts for different purposes:
  8: 
  9: | Artifact | Purpose |
 10: | -------- | ------- |
 11: | `misclassified.csv` | Identify specific mistakes to analyze |
 12: | `metrics.json` | Measure global model quality |
 13: | `train.csv` | Canonical dataset used for retraining |
 14: 
 15: `metrics.json` answers the question: **"Did the dataset changes improve the
 16: classifier?"**
 17: 
 18: ## What metrics.json Contains
 19: 
 20: Example structure:
 21: 
 22: ```json
 23: {
 24:   "accuracy": 0.91,
 25:   "classification_report": {
 26:     "search": {"precision": 0.90, "recall": 0.88, "f1-score": 0.89},
 27:     "image": {"precision": 0.92, "recall": 0.90, "f1-score": 0.91},
 28:     "ops": {"precision": 0.89, "recall": 0.91, "f1-score": 0.90},
 29:     "unknown": {"precision": 0.94, "recall": 0.87, "f1-score": 0.90}
 30:   },
 31:   "confusion_matrix": [
 32:     [43, 2, 1, 0],
 33:     [3, 39, 2, 0],
 34:     [1, 2, 41, 1],
 35:     [0, 1, 2, 35]
 36:   ]
 37: }
 38: ```
 39: 
 40: This provides three types of information.
 41: 
 42: ## 1. Detect Weak Labels
 43: 
 44: Look at **recall** and **precision** per class.
 45: 
 46: Example:
 47: 
 48: | Label | Precision | Recall |
 49: | ----- | --------- | ------ |
 50: | search | 0.90 | 0.88 |
 51: | image | 0.92 | 0.90 |
 52: | ops | 0.70 | 0.52 |
 53: | unknown | 0.93 | 0.90 |
 54: 
 55: Interpretation:
 56: 
 57: - `ops` recall is very low - the model misses many ops requests
 58: - Dataset likely lacks enough ops examples
 59: 
 60: Refinement action:
 61: 
 62: - Generate more **ops** examples
 63: - Add edge cases that resemble other classes
 64: 
 65: ## 2. Detect Confusion Patterns
 66: 
 67: The confusion matrix shows **which classes collide**.
 68: 
 69: Example:
 70: 
 71: ```text
 72: ops -> image confusion = high
 73: ```
 74: 
 75: Interpretation:
 76: 
 77: Many prompts mentioning "image" actually refer to container images.
 78: 
 79: Refinement action:
 80: 
 81: Generate examples like:
 82: 
 83: ```text
 84: kubernetes container image pull error
 85: docker imagepullbackoff debugging
 86: pod cannot pull image from registry
 87: ```
 88: 
 89: All labeled **ops**. This teaches the classifier the correct context.
 90: 
 91: ## 3. Detect Threshold Problems
 92: 
 93: If model accuracy is good but many requests become `unknown`, the dataset may
 94: be fine and only the gateway threshold needs adjustment.
 95: 
 96: Example:
 97: 
 98: ```text
 99: macro_f1 = 0.91
100: unknown recall = 0.98
101: unknown precision = 0.55
102: ```
103: 
104: Interpretation:
105: 
106: Too many inputs are classified as `unknown`.
107: 
108: Action:
109: 
110: - Reduce `T_ROUTE`
111: - Or reduce `T_MARGIN`
112: 
113: This is **policy tuning**, not dataset refinement.
114: 
115: ## Pipeline Position
116: 
117: Full loop:
118: 
119: ```text
120: train.csv
121:    |
122:    v
123: train.py
124:    |
125:    +-- model.joblib
126:    +-- metrics.json
127:    +-- misclassified.csv
128:           |
129:           v
130:        refiner
131:           |
132:           v
133:  new examples / relabel suggestions
134:           |
135:           v
136:  updated train.csv
137:           |
138:           v
139:  retrain
140: ```
141: 
142: ## Automated Refinement Using Metrics
143: 
144: The refiner uses metrics.json for:
145: 
146: - **Weak labels**: if `class_recall < 0.75`, generate examples for that label
147: - **Confusion patterns**: if `confusion(A,B) > 10`, generate contrastive
148:   examples for the true label A
149: 
150: Promotion logic (training-api `run_promote`, when a candidate model finishes
151: training on `train_candidate.csv`):
152: 
153: ```text
154: tolerance = REFINER_PROMOTE_ACCURACY_TOLERANCE  # e.g. 0.01
155: if previous_accuracy == 0 or candidate_accuracy >= previous_accuracy - tolerance:
156:     promote model.joblib
157: else:
158:     discard candidate
159: ```
160: 
161: The promote response includes `per_label_recall` (before, after, delta) for each
162: label in `classification_report` so you can see trade-offs when aggregate
163: accuracy moves slightly.
164: 
165: This reduces the chance that a small accuracy dip blocks promotion when recall
166: on weak classes improved, while still rejecting large regressions.
167: 
168: ## Practical Usage During Development
169: 
170: When you run training:
171: 
172: ```text
173: trainer
174:   +-- metrics.json
175:   +-- misclassified.csv
176: ```
177: 
178: Your workflow becomes:
179: 
180: 1. Inspect `metrics.json`
181: 2. Identify weak classes
182: 3. Use LLM refiner to generate new examples
183: 4. Retrain
184: 5. Compare new `metrics.json`
185: 
186: Repeat until metrics stabilize.
187: 
188: ## Mental Model
189: 
190: | File | Question it answers |
191: | ---- | ------------------- |
192: | misclassified.csv | What mistakes did the model make? |
193: | metrics.json | Is the model improving overall? |
194: 
195: Both are needed. One gives **local debugging**. The other gives **global
196: evaluation**.
197: 
198: ## Refiner Integration
199: 
200: The refiner uses `metrics.json` to identify weak labels and confusion patterns,
201: and to decide whether to promote a candidate model. See
202: [REFINER_PLAN.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PLAN.md),
203: [REFINER_TECHNICAL.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_TECHNICAL.md),
204: [REFINER_FLOW.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_FLOW.md),
205: [AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md](../planning/AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md).
````

## File: docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PRD.md
````markdown
  1: # REFINER SERVICE - PRODUCT REQUIREMENTS DOCUMENT
  2: 
  3: Requirements for the refiner service: an on-demand, containerized
  4: dataset refinement pipeline using a local LLM. See
  5: [REFINER_PLAN.md](REFINER_PLAN.md) for the conceptual overview and
  6: [REFINER_TECHNICAL.md](REFINER_TECHNICAL.md) for the technical specification.
  7: 
  8: ## 1. Document Control
  9: 
 10: | Attribute | Value |
 11: | --- | --- |
 12: | Document type | Product Requirements Document (PRD) |
 13: | Format | Requirements specification |
 14: | Source | REFINER_PLAN.md, REFINER_TECHNICAL.md |
 15: | Scope | Refiner service and Ollama integration |
 16: 
 17: ## 2. Business Requirements
 18: 
 19: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 20: | --- | --- | --- |
 21: | REF-BR-001 | The refiner SHALL improve the training dataset by analyzing misclassified validation rows. | Must |
 22: | REF-BR-002 | The refiner SHALL run on demand after the trainer service, not as part of every request. | Must |
 23: | REF-BR-003 | The refiner SHALL run fully locally with no external API calls. | Must |
 24: | REF-BR-004 | The refiner SHALL be containerized and part of the same Docker Compose stack. | Must |
 25: 
 26: ## 3. Model Architecture Requirements
 27: 
 28: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 29: | --- | --- | --- |
 30: | REF-FR-001 | The router model (TF-IDF + LogisticRegression) SHALL remain in ai-router for live routing. | Must |
 31: | REF-FR-002 | A local LLM (phi3:mini) SHALL run in a containerized Ollama for refinement only. | Must |
 32: | REF-FR-003 | The LLM SHALL NOT participate in request routing. | Must |
 33: | REF-FR-004 | The LLM SHALL be used only for: relabel suggestions, augmentation examples, ambiguity analysis. | Must |
 34: 
 35: ## 4. Input and Output Requirements
 36: 
 37: ### 4.1 Inputs
 38: 
 39: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 40: | --- | --- | --- |
 41: | REF-FR-005 | The refiner SHALL read `train.csv` from the shared model volume. | Must |
 42: | REF-FR-006 | The refiner SHALL read `misclassified.csv` produced by the trainer. | Must |
 43: | REF-FR-007 | The refiner SHALL exit gracefully if `misclassified.csv` is missing or empty. | Must |
 44: 
 45: ### 4.2 Outputs
 46: 
 47: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 48: | --- | --- | --- |
 49: | REF-FR-008 | The refiner SHALL write `proposed_relabels.csv` with columns: `text`, `current_label`, `suggested_label`, `reason`, `confidence`. | Must |
 50: | REF-FR-009 | The refiner SHALL write `proposed_examples.csv` with columns: `text`, `label`, `source_pattern`, `generator_model`. | Must |
 51: | REF-FR-010 | The refiner MAY write `refinement_report.json` with summary counts and quality stats. | Should |
 52: | REF-FR-011 | Only data that improves metrics SHALL be written to `train.csv`. The refiner writes `train_candidate.csv`; `scripts/promote.sh` retrains and promotes conditionally. | Must |
 53: 
 54: ## 5. Pipeline Requirements
 55: 
 56: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 57: | --- | --- | --- |
 58: | REF-FR-012 | The refiner SHALL ingest `train.csv` and `misclassified.csv`. | Must |
 59: | REF-FR-013 | The refiner SHALL send prompts to the LLM with strict JSON output constraints. | Must |
 60: | REF-FR-014 | The refiner SHALL validate LLM output against the expected JSON schema. | Must |
 61: | REF-FR-015 | The refiner SHALL apply deterministic filters: exact-duplicate removal, near-duplicate checks, banned-pattern checks, minimum length. | Must |
 62: | REF-FR-016 | The refiner SHALL write proposals to `train_candidate.csv`; promotion to `train.csv` SHALL occur only when retraining improves metrics. | Must |
 63: 
 64: ## 6. Ollama Service Requirements
 65: 
 66: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 67: | --- | --- | --- |
 68: | REF-FR-017 | Ollama SHALL run as a container in the same Docker Compose stack. | Must |
 69: | REF-FR-018 | Ollama SHALL use the official `ollama/ollama:latest` image. | Must |
 70: | REF-FR-019 | Ollama SHALL expose port 11434 for the API. | Must |
 71: | REF-FR-020 | Ollama SHALL use a persistent volume for model storage (`ollama_data`). | Must |
 72: | REF-FR-021 | The model `phi3:mini` SHALL be pulled automatically when Compose brings up the ollama service; no manual pull command SHALL be required. | Must |
 73: | REF-FR-022 | Ollama SHALL have a health check (e.g. `ollama list`). | Must |
 74: 
 75: ## 7. Refiner Service Requirements
 76: 
 77: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 78: | --- | --- | --- |
 79: | REF-FR-023 | The refiner SHALL be a Python service in `services/refiner/`. | Must |
 80: | REF-FR-024 | The refiner SHALL use the `refine` Compose profile; it SHALL NOT start with default `docker compose up`. | Must |
 81: | REF-FR-025 | The refiner SHALL depend on Ollama with `condition: service_healthy`. | Must |
 82: | REF-FR-026 | The refiner SHALL connect to Ollama via `OLLAMA_HOST` (default `http://ollama:11434`). | Must |
 83: | REF-FR-027 | The refiner SHALL use `OLLAMA_MODEL` (default `phi3:mini`). | Must |
 84: | REF-FR-028 | The refiner SHALL mount the shared model volume for read/write of proposal files. | Must |
 85: 
 86: ## 8. Trigger and Workflow Requirements
 87: 
 88: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 89: | --- | --- | --- |
 90: | REF-FR-029 | Refinement SHALL be triggered via `docker compose --profile refine run --rm refiner`. | Must |
 91: | REF-FR-030 | The refiner SHALL run after the trainer; the trainer SHALL produce `misclassified.csv` first. | Must |
 92: | REF-FR-031 | Promotion of a new model SHALL occur only when metrics improve (accuracy, unknown recall, confusion pairs). | Must |
 93: 
 94: ## 9. Non-Functional Requirements
 95: 
 96: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 97: | --- | --- | --- |
 98: | REF-NFR-001 | The refiner SHALL run on CPU; GPU SHALL be optional. | Must |
 99: | REF-NFR-002 | The refiner SHALL be reproducible across macOS, Linux, and Windows + WSL2. | Must |
100: | REF-NFR-003 | The refiner SHALL log progress (e.g. rows processed, files written). | Should |
101: | REF-NFR-004 | The refiner SHALL handle LLM timeouts and parse errors gracefully. | Must |
102: 
103: ## 10. Acceptance Criteria
104: 
105: | ID | Criterion | Priority |
106: | --- | --- | --- |
107: | REF-AC-001 | `docker compose --profile refine run --rm refiner` SHALL succeed when `misclassified.csv` exists and Ollama is healthy. | Must |
108: | REF-AC-002 | After refinement, `train_candidate.csv` SHALL exist; promotion to `train.csv` SHALL occur only when `scripts/promote.sh` confirms metrics improvement. | Must |
109: | REF-AC-003 | The refiner SHALL exit with a clear message when `misclassified.csv` is missing or empty. | Must |
110: | REF-AC-004 | Ollama SHALL be reachable from the refiner container over the Compose network. | Must |
111: 
112: ## 11. Out of Scope
113: 
114: - Evaluator and promoter services (future enhancement)
115: - GPU-accelerated Ollama (optional, platform-dependent)
116: 
117: ## 12. Cross-References
118: 
119: - [REFINER_FLOW.md](REFINER_FLOW.md) - End-to-end flow
120: - [METRICS_JSON.md](docs/auxiliary/reference/METRICS_JSON.md) - metrics.json purpose
121: and usage
122: - [REFINER_PLAN.md](REFINER_PLAN.md)
123: - [REFINER_TECHNICAL.md](REFINER_TECHNICAL.md)
124: - [PRD.md](docs/auxiliary/requirements/PRD.md)
125: - [TECHNICAL.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/TECHNICAL.md)
````

## File: docs/auxiliary/requirements/ACCEPTANCE.md
````markdown
 1: # Acceptance Verification
 2: 
 3: Verification of acceptance criteria (AC-002 through AC-010) and definition of
 4: done (DoD). Per TASKS.md Batch 8.
 5: 
 6: ## Unknown vs Backend Failure Distinction (AC-010)
 7: 
 8: Two distinct error responses; trace shape and HTTP status differ.
 9: 
10: ### Unknown Classification (AI Decision)
11: 
12: - **Cause:** Model returns `unknown`, or confidence below `T_ROUTE`, or margin
13:   below `T_MARGIN`.
14: - **Gateway behavior:** Does not proxy to any backend.
15: - **HTTP status:** 404.
16: - **Trace shape:** gateway-received, ai-router-classified, gateway-responded.
17:   No backend hop.
18: - **Response body:** `request_id`, `route` (unknown), `confidence`, `message`,
19:   `trace`, `timings_ms`.
20: 
21: ### Backend Failure (Infrastructure)
22: 
23: - **Cause:** AI-router classifies to a known route, but the backend is down,
24:   unreachable, or times out.
25: - **Gateway behavior:** Attempts proxy, catches error, returns 502.
26: - **HTTP status:** 502.
27: - **Trace shape:** gateway-received, ai-router-classified, gateway-responded
28:   with `meta.status: 502` and `meta.error`. No backend-handled entry (attempt
29:   failed before response).
30: - **Response body:** `request_id`, `route` (intended backend), `confidence`,
31:   `message` (e.g. "Backend image unavailable"), `trace`, `timings_ms`.
32: 
33: ### Summary Table
34: 
35: | Scenario | HTTP | Trace backend hop | Cause |
36: | --- | --- | --- | --- |
37: | Unknown classification | 404 | None | AI decision |
38: | Low confidence / low margin | 404 | None | Policy decision |
39: | Backend unreachable | 502 | None (attempted) | Infrastructure |
40: | AI router unreachable | 503 | None | Infrastructure |
41: | Successful route | 200 | Yes | Normal flow |
42: 
43: ## Verification Results
44: 
45: ### AC-002, AC-003: Browser UI and Test Cases
46: 
47: | Prompt | Expected Route | Verified |
48: | --- | --- | --- |
49: | compare nginx ingress vs traefik | search | Yes |
50: | detect objects in an image and return labels | image | Yes |
51: | kubectl pods CrashLoopBackOff, debug steps | ops | Yes |
52: | hello | unknown | Yes |
53: | tell me a joke | unknown | Yes |
54: 
55: ### AC-004: Unknown Trace Shape
56: 
57: For unknown: trace has no backend hop (gateway, ai-router, gateway only);
58: HTTP 404.
59: 
60: ### AC-005: Full JSON Response
61: 
62: curl POST to `/api/request` returns JSON with `request_id`, `route`,
63: `confidence`, `explanation`, `trace`, `backend_response` (or `message` for
64: unknown), `timings_ms`.
65: 
66: ### AC-006: Log Correlation
67: 
68: All services log `request_id` in JSON format. Filter logs:
69: 
70: ```bash
71: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml logs -f | grep "request_id.*<uuid>"
72: ```
73: 
74: ### AC-007, AC-008: Scaling
75: 
76: `docker compose up -d --scale search_service=3` distributes requests;
77: trace `instance` field shows which container handled each request.
78: 
79: ### AC-009, AC-010: Failure Demo
80: 
81: Stop a backend (e.g. `docker compose stop image_service`), send image prompt;
82: gateway returns 502 with trace showing classification and backend failure.
83: Unknown (404) is distinguishable from backend failure (502).
84: 
85: ### DoD-001, DoD-006, DoD-007: Final Check
86: 
87: `docker compose up --build` succeeds; multi-stage Dockerfiles, Compose
88: anchors/profiles, health checks implemented; all services log request_id.
89: 
90: ### Refiner Acceptance (REF-AC-001 to REF-AC-004)
91: 
92: See [REFINER_PRD.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PRD.md) for refiner
93: acceptance criteria.
94: Verification: `docker compose --profile refine run --rm refiner` succeeds when
95: `misclassified.csv` exists and Ollama is healthy; proposal files and
96: `train_candidate.csv` are produced; promote when training-api criteria are met
97: (accuracy within `REFINER_PROMOTE_ACCURACY_TOLERANCE` of the baseline, or zero
98: baseline accuracy). See
99: [AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md](../planning/AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md).
````

## File: scripts/load_test.sh
````bash
 1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
 2: # Load test script for Local AI Microservice Mesh (NFR-037, AC-007)
 3: # Sends multiple requests to gateway; use with scaled backends to verify
 4: # distribution. Per TECH-18.3.
 5: set -euo pipefail
 6: COMPOSE_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
 7: COMPOSE_FILE="${COMPOSE_DIR}/compose/docker-compose.yaml"
 8: GATEWAY_URL="${GATEWAY_URL:-http://localhost:8000}"
 9: # Default: 20 requests. Override: REQUESTS=50 ./scripts/load_test.sh
10: REQUESTS="${REQUESTS:-20}"
11: # Prompts that route to search (for distribution verification when scaled)
12: SEARCH_PROMPTS=(
13:   "compare nginx ingress vs traefik"
14:   "how does kubernetes scheduling work"
15:   "best practices for docker multi-stage builds"
16:   "terraform vs pulumi comparison"
17:   "search for microservice patterns"
18: )
19: usage() {
20:   cat <<EOF
21: Usage: $0 [options]
22: Sends multiple requests to the gateway. With scaled backends (e.g.
23: search_service=3), verify distribution by checking trace 'instance'
24: fields for different container hostnames.
25: Options:
26:   REQUESTS=N    Number of requests (default: 20)
27:   GATEWAY_URL   Gateway base URL (default: http://localhost:8000)
28: Verify distribution:
29:   1. Scale: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml up -d --scale search_service=3
30:   2. Run:   $0
31:   3. Check: docker compose logs gateway | grep -o '"instance":"[^"]*"' | sort | uniq -c
32: Or inspect responses: each backend_response includes "instance" (container hostname).
33: EOF
34: }
35: main() {
36:   if [[ "${1:-}" == "--help" || "${1:-}" == "-h" ]]; then
37:     usage
38:     exit 0
39:   fi
40:   cd "$COMPOSE_DIR"
41:   echo "Load test: $REQUESTS requests to $GATEWAY_URL"
42:   echo ""
43:   success=0
44:   fail=0
45:   instances=""
46:   for ((i=0; i<REQUESTS; i++)); do
47:     prompt="${SEARCH_PROMPTS[$((i % ${#SEARCH_PROMPTS[@]}))]}"
48:     json_body=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.dumps({'text': '''$prompt'''}))" 2>/dev/null)
49:     resp=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X POST "$GATEWAY_URL/api/request" \
50:       -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
51:       -d "$json_body" 2>/dev/null) || { fail=$((fail + 1)); continue; }
52:     http_code=$(echo "$resp" | tail -1)
53:     body=$(echo "$resp" | sed '$d')
54:     if [[ "$http_code" == "200" ]]; then
55:       success=$((success + 1))
56:       inst=$(echo "$body" | python3 -c "
57: import sys, json
58: try:
59:     d = json.load(sys.stdin)
60:     print(d.get('backend_response', {}).get('instance', 'N/A'))
61: except: print('N/A')
62: " 2>/dev/null)
63:       instances="$instances$inst"$'\n'
64:     else
65:       fail=$((fail + 1))
66:     fi
67:     printf "\rProgress: %d/%d (ok=%d fail=%d)" "$((i+1))" "$REQUESTS" "$success" "$fail"
68:   done
69:   echo ""
70:   echo ""
71:   echo "Results: $success succeeded, $fail failed"
72:   if [[ $success -gt 0 ]]; then
73:     echo ""
74:     echo "Instance distribution (backend hostnames):"
75:     echo "$instances" | grep -v '^$' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
76:   fi
77: }
78: main "$@"
````

## File: scripts/refine_augment.sh
````bash
 1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
 2: set -euo pipefail
 3: REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
 4: COMPOSE_FILE="${REPO_ROOT}/compose/docker-compose.yaml"
 5: CONFIG_ENV="${CONFIG_ENV:-dev}"
 6: if [[ ! "$CONFIG_ENV" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ ]]; then
 7:   echo "Invalid CONFIG_ENV: $CONFIG_ENV" >&2
 8:   exit 1
 9: fi
10: usage() {
11:   cat <<'EOF'
12: Usage: refine_augment.sh --run-id ID
13: Options:
14:   --run-id ID   Shared refine run id to correlate relabel + augment outputs.
15: EOF
16: }
17: run_id="${REFINER_RUN_ID:-}"
18: while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
19:   case "$1" in
20:     --run-id)
21:       run_id="${2:-}"
22:       shift 2
23:       ;;
24:     -h|--help)
25:       usage
26:       exit 0
27:       ;;
28:     *)
29:       echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2
30:       usage
31:       exit 1
32:       ;;
33:   esac
34: done
35: if [[ -z "$run_id" ]]; then
36:   echo "Missing required --run-id ID (or REFINER_RUN_ID)." >&2
37:   exit 1
38: fi
39: env_file="${REPO_ROOT}/env/.env.${CONFIG_ENV}"
40: if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
41:   python3 "${REPO_ROOT}/scripts/generate_env.py" "${CONFIG_ENV}"
42: fi
43: export PROJECT_ENV_FILE="../env/.env.${CONFIG_ENV}"
44: export ENV="${CONFIG_ENV}"
45: # shellcheck disable=SC1090
46: source "$env_file"
47: echo "Running augmentation phase (run_id=${run_id})..."
48: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" --profile refine run --rm \
49:   -e "REFINER_RUN_ID=${run_id}" \
50:   -e "OLLAMA_NUM_CTX=${REFINER_AUGMENT_NUM_CTX:-768}" \
51:   -e "OLLAMA_NUM_PREDICT=${REFINER_AUGMENT_NUM_PREDICT:-180}" \
52:   -e "REFINER_TEMPERATURE=${REFINER_TEMPERATURE:-0.1}" \
53:   -e "REFINER_SEED=${REFINER_SEED:-42}" \
54:   -e "REFINER_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_ENABLED=${REFINER_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_ENABLED:-true}" \
55:   training-api augment
````

## File: scripts/refine_relabel.sh
````bash
 1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
 2: set -euo pipefail
 3: REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
 4: COMPOSE_FILE="${REPO_ROOT}/compose/docker-compose.yaml"
 5: CONFIG_ENV="${CONFIG_ENV:-dev}"
 6: if [[ ! "$CONFIG_ENV" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ ]]; then
 7:   echo "Invalid CONFIG_ENV: $CONFIG_ENV" >&2
 8:   exit 1
 9: fi
10: usage() {
11:   cat <<'EOF'
12: Usage: refine_relabel.sh [--run-id ID] [--limit N]
13: Options:
14:   --run-id ID   Shared refine run id to correlate relabel + augment outputs.
15:   --limit N     Max misclassified rows to process for relabel.
16: EOF
17: }
18: run_id="${REFINER_RUN_ID:-}"
19: limit=""
20: while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
21:   case "$1" in
22:     --run-id)
23:       run_id="${2:-}"
24:       shift 2
25:       ;;
26:     --limit)
27:       limit="${2:-}"
28:       shift 2
29:       ;;
30:     -h|--help)
31:       usage
32:       exit 0
33:       ;;
34:     *)
35:       echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2
36:       usage
37:       exit 1
38:       ;;
39:   esac
40: done
41: if [[ -z "$run_id" ]]; then
42:   run_id="$(python3 -c 'import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())')"
43: fi
44: env_file="${REPO_ROOT}/env/.env.${CONFIG_ENV}"
45: if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
46:   python3 "${REPO_ROOT}/scripts/generate_env.py" "${CONFIG_ENV}"
47: fi
48: export PROJECT_ENV_FILE="../env/.env.${CONFIG_ENV}"
49: export ENV="${CONFIG_ENV}"
50: # shellcheck disable=SC1090
51: source "$env_file"
52: echo "Running relabel phase (run_id=${run_id})..."
53: run_args=(
54:   --rm
55:   -e "REFINER_RUN_ID=${run_id}"
56:   -e "OLLAMA_NUM_CTX=${REFINER_RELABEL_NUM_CTX:-1024}"
57:   -e "OLLAMA_NUM_PREDICT=${REFINER_RELABEL_NUM_PREDICT:-120}"
58:   -e "REFINER_TEMPERATURE=${REFINER_TEMPERATURE:-0.1}"
59:   -e "REFINER_SEED=${REFINER_SEED:-42}"
60:   -e "REFINER_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_ENABLED=${REFINER_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_ENABLED:-true}"
61: )
62: if [[ -n "$limit" ]]; then
63:   run_args+=(-e "REFINER_LIMIT=${limit}")
64: fi
65: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" --profile refine run "${run_args[@]}" training-api relabel
````

## File: services/ai_router/app/main.py
````python
  1: """AI router service - intent classification."""
  2: from __future__ import annotations
  3: import asyncio
  4: import json
  5: import logging
  6: import os
  7: import sys
  8: from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
  9: from datetime import datetime, timezone
 10: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
 11: import joblib
 12: import numpy as np
 13: from fastapi import FastAPI
 14: from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
 15: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
 16: BUILD_MODEL_PATH = "/app/model/model.joblib"
 17: class JsonFormatter(logging.Formatter):
 18:     """JSON-structured log format for request_id correlation (TECH-20)."""
 19:     def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
 20:         obj: Dict[str, Any] = {
 21:             "ts": self.formatTime(record, self.datefmt),
 22:             "level": record.levelname,
 23:             "service": record.name,
 24:             "msg": record.getMessage(),
 25:         }
 26:         if hasattr(record, "request_id"):
 27:             obj["request_id"] = record.request_id
 28:         return json.dumps(obj)
 29: def setup_logging(service_name: str, level: str = "INFO") -> logging.Logger:
 30:     """Configure JSON logging for request correlation (TECH-20.1)."""
 31:     handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
 32:     handler.setFormatter(JsonFormatter())
 33:     _logger = logging.getLogger(service_name)
 34:     _logger.setLevel(getattr(logging, level.upper(), logging.INFO))
 35:     _logger.addHandler(handler)
 36:     return _logger
 37: logger = setup_logging("ai-router", os.getenv("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"))
 38: def load_model():
 39:     """Load from MODEL_PATH if set and exists, else build-time artifact (TECH-7.5)."""
 40:     volume_path = os.getenv("MODEL_PATH")
 41:     if volume_path and os.path.exists(volume_path):
 42:         path_to_load = volume_path
 43:     elif os.path.exists(BUILD_MODEL_PATH):
 44:         path_to_load = BUILD_MODEL_PATH
 45:     else:
 46:         tried = [p for p in (volume_path, BUILD_MODEL_PATH) if p]
 47:         raise RuntimeError(f"Model not found. Tried: {tried}")
 48:     artifact = joblib.load(path_to_load)
 49:     return artifact["vectorizer"], artifact["model"], artifact["labels"]
 50: def top_contributing_tokens(
 51:     text: str,
 52:     vectorizer,
 53:     model,
 54:     labels: List[str],  # noqa: ARG001
 55:     top_n: int = 6,
 56: ) -> Tuple[str, float, Dict[str, float], List[str]]:
 57:     X = vectorizer.transform([text])
 58:     classes = list(model.classes_)
 59:     probs = model.predict_proba(X)[0]
 60:     pred_idx = int(np.argmax(probs))
 61:     pred_label = classes[pred_idx]
 62:     confidence = float(probs[pred_idx])
 63:     probs_map = {classes[i]: float(probs[i]) for i in range(len(classes))}
 64:     feature_names = vectorizer.get_feature_names_out()
 65:     if not hasattr(model, "coef_"):
 66:         return pred_label, confidence, probs_map, []
 67:     class_coef = model.coef_[pred_idx]
 68:     indices = X.indices
 69:     values = X.data
 70:     if indices.size == 0:
 71:         return pred_label, confidence, probs_map, []
 72:     contrib = values * class_coef[indices]
 73:     order = np.argsort(contrib)[::-1]
 74:     top_tokens: List[str] = []
 75:     for i in order[:top_n]:
 76:         top_tokens.append(str(feature_names[indices[i]]))
 77:     return pred_label, confidence, probs_map, top_tokens
 78: class TraceEntry(BaseModel):
 79:     service: str
 80:     event: str
 81:     ts: str
 82:     meta: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
 83: def make_trace_entry(
 84:     service: str,
 85:     event: str,
 86:     meta: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
 87: ) -> TraceEntry:
 88:     return TraceEntry(
 89:         service=service,
 90:         event=event,
 91:         ts=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(timespec="milliseconds"),
 92:         meta=meta,
 93:     )
 94: class ClassifyRequest(BaseModel):
 95:     request_id: str
 96:     text: str = Field(..., max_length=10000)
 97: class ClassifyResponse(BaseModel):
 98:     route: str
 99:     confidence: float
100:     probabilities: Dict[str, float]
101:     explanation: str
102:     top_tokens: List[str]
103:     trace_append: Dict[str, Any]
104: @asynccontextmanager
105: async def lifespan(application: FastAPI):
106:     vec, mdl, labels = load_model()
107:     application.state.vectorizer = vec
108:     application.state.model = mdl
109:     application.state.labels = labels
110:     application.state.model_loaded = True
111:     yield
112: app = FastAPI(title="AI Router", version="0.1.0", lifespan=lifespan)
113: @app.get("/health")
114: async def health():
115:     """Health check - verifies model is loaded."""
116:     if not getattr(app.state, "model_loaded", False):
117:         return {"status": "not_ready"}
118:     return {"status": "ok"}
119: @app.post("/classify", response_model=ClassifyResponse)
120: async def classify(req: ClassifyRequest):
121:     logger.info(
122:         "classify request",
123:         extra={"request_id": req.request_id},
124:     )
125:     try:
126:         pred_label, confidence, probs_map, tokens = await asyncio.to_thread(
127:             top_contributing_tokens,
128:             req.text,
129:             app.state.vectorizer,
130:             app.state.model,
131:             app.state.labels,
132:             6,
133:         )
134:     except (ValueError, TypeError, KeyError, AttributeError, OSError) as exc:
135:         logger.error("Inference error: %s", exc, extra={"request_id": req.request_id})
136:         return JSONResponse(
137:             status_code=500,
138:             content={"detail": "Classification inference failed"},
139:         )
140:     explanation = f"top tokens: {', '.join(tokens)}" if tokens else ""
141:     trace = make_trace_entry(
142:         service="ai-router",
143:         event="classified",
144:         meta={
145:             "route": pred_label,
146:             "confidence": round(confidence, 4),
147:         },
148:     )
149:     logger.info(
150:         "classified route=%s confidence=%.4f",
151:         pred_label,
152:         confidence,
153:         extra={"request_id": req.request_id},
154:     )
155:     return ClassifyResponse(
156:         route=pred_label,
157:         confidence=round(confidence, 4),
158:         probabilities={k: round(v, 4) for k, v in probs_map.items()},
159:         explanation=explanation,
160:         top_tokens=tokens,
161:         trace_append=trace.model_dump(),
162:     )
````

## File: services/ai_router/tests/test_classify.py
````python
 1: """Unit tests for ai-router /classify endpoint."""
 2: from __future__ import annotations
 3: import importlib.util
 4: import os
 5: import sys
 6: import pytest
 7: from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
 8: _SERVICE_ROOT = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), ".."))
 9: MODEL_PATH = os.path.join(_SERVICE_ROOT, "model", "model.joblib")
10: os.environ["MODEL_PATH"] = MODEL_PATH
11: sys.path.insert(0, _SERVICE_ROOT)
12: _saved_app = {k: sys.modules.pop(k) for k in list(sys.modules) if k == "app" or k.startswith("app.")}
13: from app.main import app  # noqa: E402
14: _ai_router_app = app
15: for k in list(sys.modules):
16:     if k == "app" or k.startswith("app."):
17:         sys.modules.pop(k, None)
18: sys.modules.update(_saved_app)
19: try:
20:     sys.path.remove(_SERVICE_ROOT)
21: except ValueError:
22:     pass
23: @pytest.fixture(scope="module")
24: def client():
25:     with TestClient(_ai_router_app) as c:
26:         yield c
27: def test_health(client):
28:     resp = client.get("/health")
29:     assert resp.status_code == 200
30:     assert resp.json()["status"] == "ok"
31: @pytest.mark.parametrize(
32:     "text,expected_route",
33:     [
34:         ("compare nginx ingress vs traefik for kubernetes", "search"),
35:         ("resize image to 800x600 and convert to PNG", "image"),
36:         ("kubectl get pods shows CrashLoopBackOff", "ops"),
37:     ],
38: )
39: def test_classify_known_routes(client, text: str, expected_route: str):
40:     resp = client.post(
41:         "/classify",
42:         json={"request_id": "test-001", "text": text},
43:     )
44:     assert resp.status_code == 200
45:     data = resp.json()
46:     assert data["route"] == expected_route
47: def test_classify_unknown_route(client):
48:     resp = client.post(
49:         "/classify",
50:         json={"request_id": "test-002", "text": "hello how are you"},
51:     )
52:     assert resp.status_code == 200
53:     data = resp.json()
54:     assert data["route"] == "unknown"
55: def test_classify_response_shape(client):
56:     resp = client.post(
57:         "/classify",
58:         json={"request_id": "test-003", "text": "search for kubernetes tutorials"},
59:     )
60:     assert resp.status_code == 200
61:     data = resp.json()
62:     assert "route" in data
63:     assert "confidence" in data
64:     assert "probabilities" in data
65:     assert "explanation" in data
66:     assert "top_tokens" in data
67:     assert "trace_append" in data
68:     assert isinstance(data["confidence"], float)
69:     assert 0.0 <= data["confidence"] <= 1.0
70:     probs = data["probabilities"]
71:     assert set(probs.keys()) == {"search", "image", "ops", "unknown"}
72:     for v in probs.values():
73:         assert 0.0 <= v <= 1.0
74:     assert abs(sum(probs.values()) - 1.0) < 0.01
75:     assert isinstance(data["top_tokens"], list)
76:     assert len(data["top_tokens"]) <= 6
77:     trace = data["trace_append"]
78:     assert trace["service"] == "ai-router"
79:     assert trace["event"] == "classified"
80:     assert "ts" in trace
81:     assert "meta" in trace
82:     assert "route" in trace["meta"]
83:     assert "confidence" in trace["meta"]
84: def test_classify_explanation_contains_tokens(client):
85:     resp = client.post(
86:         "/classify",
87:         json={"request_id": "test-004", "text": "deploy helm chart to production cluster"},
88:     )
89:     data = resp.json()
90:     if data["top_tokens"]:
91:         assert "top tokens:" in data["explanation"]
92:         for token in data["top_tokens"]:
93:             assert token in data["explanation"]
````

## File: services/gateway/app/static/query.js
````javascript
  1: (function () {
  2:   "use strict";
  3:   const form = document.getElementById("query-form");
  4:   const input = document.getElementById("query-input");
  5:   const submitBtn = document.getElementById("submit-btn");
  6:   const resultSection = document.getElementById("result-section");
  7:   const errorSection = document.getElementById("error-section");
  8:   function showError(msg) {
  9:     errorSection.classList.remove("hidden");
 10:     document.getElementById("error-text").textContent = msg;
 11:   }
 12:   function renderRouteCard(data, isUnknown) {
 13:     const routeName = document.getElementById("route-name");
 14:     const confidenceFill = document.getElementById("confidence-fill");
 15:     const confidenceLabel = document.getElementById("confidence-label");
 16:     const explanation = document.getElementById("explanation");
 17:     routeName.textContent = data.route || "unknown";
 18:     routeName.className = "route-name " + (isUnknown ? "unknown" : "known");
 19:     const confidence = data.confidence ?? 0;
 20:     confidenceFill.style.width = (confidence * 100) + "%";
 21:     confidenceLabel.textContent = (confidence * 100).toFixed(1) + "%";
 22:     const expl = data.explanation || data.top_tokens?.join(", ") || "";
 23:     explanation.textContent = expl ? "Explanation: " + expl : "";
 24:     explanation.classList.toggle("hidden", !expl);
 25:   }
 26:   function renderHopDiagram(trace) {
 27:     const chain = document.getElementById("hop-chain");
 28:     chain.innerHTML = "";
 29:     const services = trace.map((t) => t.service);
 30:     const unique = [];
 31:     let prev = null;
 32:     for (const s of services) {
 33:       if (s !== prev) {
 34:         unique.push(s);
 35:         prev = s;
 36:       }
 37:     }
 38:     if (unique.length === 0) {
 39:       chain.textContent = "No trace data";
 40:       return;
 41:     }
 42:     unique.forEach((service, i) => {
 43:       const span = document.createElement("span");
 44:       span.className = "hop-node";
 45:       span.textContent = service;
 46:       chain.appendChild(span);
 47:       if (i < unique.length - 1) {
 48:         const arrow = document.createElement("span");
 49:         arrow.className = "hop-arrow";
 50:         arrow.textContent = " -> ";
 51:         chain.appendChild(arrow);
 52:       }
 53:     });
 54:   }
 55:   function renderTraceTimeline(trace) {
 56:     const list = document.getElementById("trace-list");
 57:     list.innerHTML = "";
 58:     trace.forEach((entry) => {
 59:       const li = document.createElement("li");
 60:       li.className = "trace-entry";
 61:       const meta = entry.meta
 62:         ? " | " + escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(entry.meta))
 63:         : "";
 64:       li.innerHTML =
 65:         "<strong>" +
 66:         escapeHtml(entry.service) +
 67:         "</strong> " +
 68:         escapeHtml(entry.event) +
 69:         " @ " +
 70:         escapeHtml(entry.ts) +
 71:         meta;
 72:       list.appendChild(li);
 73:     });
 74:   }
 75:   function renderTimings(timings) {
 76:     const content = document.getElementById("timings-content");
 77:     if (!timings) {
 78:       content.textContent = "No timing data";
 79:       return;
 80:     }
 81:     content.textContent =
 82:       "Classify: " +
 83:       (timings.classify ?? "-") +
 84:       " ms | Proxy: " +
 85:       (timings.proxy ?? "-") +
 86:       " ms | Total: " +
 87:       (timings.total ?? "-") +
 88:       " ms";
 89:   }
 90:   function renderBackendResponse(backendResponse, isUnknown) {
 91:     const container = document.getElementById("backend-response");
 92:     const pre = document.getElementById("backend-json");
 93:     if (isUnknown || !backendResponse) {
 94:       container.classList.add("hidden");
 95:       return;
 96:     }
 97:     container.classList.remove("hidden");
 98:     pre.textContent = JSON.stringify(backendResponse, null, 2);
 99:   }
100:   function renderUnknownMessage(data, isUnknown) {
101:     const container = document.getElementById("unknown-message");
102:     const text = document.getElementById("unknown-text");
103:     if (!isUnknown) {
104:       container.classList.add("hidden");
105:       return;
106:     }
107:     container.classList.remove("hidden");
108:     text.textContent =
109:       data.message || "Unable to determine a suitable backend.";
110:   }
111:   function renderServiceError(data, isServiceError) {
112:     const container = document.getElementById("service-error");
113:     const text = document.getElementById("service-error-text");
114:     if (!isServiceError) {
115:       container.classList.add("hidden");
116:       return;
117:     }
118:     container.classList.remove("hidden");
119:     text.textContent =
120:       data.message || "Service temporarily unavailable.";
121:   }
122:   function renderResult(data, status) {
123:     resultSection.classList.remove("hidden");
124:     const isUnknown = status === 404 || data.route === "unknown";
125:     const isServiceError = status === 502 || status === 503;
126:     renderRouteCard(data, isUnknown);
127:     renderHopDiagram(data.trace || []);
128:     renderTraceTimeline(data.trace || []);
129:     renderTimings(data.timings_ms);
130:     renderBackendResponse(data.backend_response, isUnknown);
131:     renderUnknownMessage(data, isUnknown);
132:     renderServiceError(data, isServiceError);
133:   }
134:   form.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
135:     e.preventDefault();
136:     const text = input.value.trim();
137:     if (!text) return;
138:     submitBtn.disabled = true;
139:     resultSection.classList.add("hidden");
140:     errorSection.classList.add("hidden");
141:     const requestId = crypto.randomUUID();
142:     const body = {
143:       request_id: requestId,
144:       text: text,
145:       trace: [
146:         {
147:           service: "web",
148:           event: "submit",
149:           ts: new Date().toISOString(),
150:         },
151:       ],
152:     };
153:     try {
154:       const resp = await fetch("/api/request", {
155:         method: "POST",
156:         headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
157:         body: JSON.stringify(body),
158:       });
159:       const data = await resp.json();
160:       renderResult(data, resp.status);
161:     } catch (err) {
162:       showError(err.message || "Network error");
163:     } finally {
164:       submitBtn.disabled = false;
165:     }
166:   });
167: })();
````

## File: services/gateway/app/static/train.html
````html
 1: <!DOCTYPE html>
 2: <html lang="en">
 3: <head>
 4:   <meta charset="UTF-8">
 5:   <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
 6:   <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self'">
 7:   <title>Train - AI Router Gateway</title>
 8:   <link rel="stylesheet" href="/static/base.css">
 9:   <link rel="stylesheet" href="/static/train.css">
10: </head>
11: <body>
12:   <main class="container">
13:     <header class="header">
14:       <h1>AI Router Gateway</h1>
15:       <p class="subtitle">Run the router training job and view metrics</p>
16:       <nav class="main-nav" aria-label="Main">
17:         <a href="/" id="nav-query" class="nav-link">Query</a>
18:         <span class="nav-sep">|</span>
19:         <a href="/train" id="nav-train" class="nav-link current">Train</a>
20:         <span class="nav-sep">|</span>
21:         <a href="/refine" id="nav-refine" class="nav-link">Refine</a>
22:       </nav>
23:     </header>
24:     <section id="train-section" class="train-section">
25:       <h2>Train</h2>
26:       <p class="section-desc">Run the router training job and view metrics.</p>
27:       <div class="train-actions">
28:         <button type="button" id="train-run-btn" class="action-btn">Run training</button>
29:         <button type="button" id="train-last-btn" class="action-btn action-btn-secondary">Load last run</button>
30:       </div>
31:       <div id="train-progress" class="progress-wrap hidden" role="status" aria-live="polite">
32:         <div class="progress-bar progress-bar-indeterminate"></div>
33:         <span class="progress-label">Running...</span>
34:       </div>
35:       <div id="train-message" class="message-area hidden"></div>
36:       <div id="train-metrics" class="metrics-placeholder placeholder-box hidden">
37:         <h3>Metrics</h3>
38:         <p class="placeholder-text">Accuracy and classification report will appear here.</p>
39:         <div id="train-metrics-content"></div>
40:       </div>
41:       <div id="train-misclassified" class="table-placeholder placeholder-box hidden">
42:         <h3>Misclassified</h3>
43:         <p class="placeholder-text">Misclassified rows table will appear here.</p>
44:         <div id="train-misclassified-content" class="data-table-wrap"></div>
45:       </div>
46:     </section>
47:   </main>
48:   <div id="probs-modal-overlay" class="probs-modal-overlay hidden" aria-hidden="true">
49:     <div id="probs-modal" class="probs-modal" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="probs-modal-title">
50:       <div class="probs-modal-header">
51:         <span class="probs-modal-title" id="probs-modal-title">Probs (JSON)</span>
52:         <button type="button" id="probs-modal-close" class="probs-modal-close" aria-label="Close">&#215;</button>
53:       </div>
54:       <pre id="probs-modal-body" class="probs-modal-body"></pre>
55:       <div id="probs-modal-resize" class="probs-modal-resize" aria-hidden="true"></div>
56:     </div>
57:   </div>
58:   <script src="/static/utils.js"></script>
59:   <script src="/static/train.js"></script>
60: </body>
61: </html>
````

## File: services/gateway/app/static/train.js
````javascript
  1: (function () {
  2:   "use strict";
  3:   const trainRunBtn = document.getElementById("train-run-btn");
  4:   const trainLastBtn = document.getElementById("train-last-btn");
  5:   const trainProgress = document.getElementById("train-progress");
  6:   const trainMessage = document.getElementById("train-message");
  7:   const trainMetrics = document.getElementById("train-metrics");
  8:   const trainMetricsContent = document.getElementById("train-metrics-content");
  9:   const trainMisclassified = document.getElementById("train-misclassified");
 10:   const trainMisclassifiedContent = document.getElementById("train-misclassified-content");
 11:   const probsModalOverlay = document.getElementById("probs-modal-overlay");
 12:   const probsModal = document.getElementById("probs-modal");
 13:   const probsModalClose = document.getElementById("probs-modal-close");
 14:   const probsModalBody = document.getElementById("probs-modal-body");
 15:   const probsModalResize = document.getElementById("probs-modal-resize");
 16:   function setTrainRunning(running) {
 17:     trainRunBtn.disabled = running;
 18:     if (running) {
 19:       trainProgress.classList.remove("hidden");
 20:       trainMessage.classList.add("hidden");
 21:       trainMetrics.classList.add("hidden");
 22:       trainMisclassified.classList.add("hidden");
 23:     } else {
 24:       trainProgress.classList.add("hidden");
 25:     }
 26:   }
 27:   function showTrainError(msg) {
 28:     trainMessage.classList.remove("hidden");
 29:     trainMessage.textContent = msg;
 30:     trainMessage.setAttribute("data-type", "error");
 31:   }
 32:   function showTrainMessage(msg, type) {
 33:     trainMessage.classList.remove("hidden");
 34:     trainMessage.textContent = msg;
 35:     trainMessage.setAttribute("data-type", type || "info");
 36:   }
 37:   function renderTrainMetrics(data) {
 38:     trainMetrics.classList.remove("hidden");
 39:     trainMetricsContent.innerHTML = "";
 40:     trainMetricsContent.appendChild(buildMetricsDOM(data));
 41:   }
 42:   function formatProbsJson(raw) {
 43:     if (raw == null || raw === "") return "";
 44:     const str = typeof raw === "string" ? raw : String(raw);
 45:     try {
 46:       const parsed = JSON.parse(str);
 47:       return JSON.stringify(parsed, null, 2);
 48:     } catch (_) {
 49:       return str;
 50:     }
 51:   }
 52:   function showProbsModal(probsJson) {
 53:     if (!probsModalOverlay || !probsModalBody) return;
 54:     probsModalBody.textContent = formatProbsJson(probsJson);
 55:     probsModalOverlay.classList.remove("hidden");
 56:     probsModalOverlay.setAttribute("aria-hidden", "false");
 57:   }
 58:   function closeProbsModal() {
 59:     if (!probsModalOverlay) return;
 60:     probsModalOverlay.classList.add("hidden");
 61:     probsModalOverlay.setAttribute("aria-hidden", "true");
 62:   }
 63:   function renderTrainMisclassified(rows) {
 64:     if (!rows || rows.length === 0) {
 65:       trainMisclassified.classList.add("hidden");
 66:       return;
 67:     }
 68:     trainMisclassified.classList.remove("hidden");
 69:     trainMisclassifiedContent.innerHTML = "";
 70:     const table = document.createElement("table");
 71:     table.className = "data-table";
 72:     const thead = document.createElement("thead");
 73:     thead.innerHTML =
 74:       "<tr><th>Text</th><th>True</th><th>Pred</th><th>Confidence</th><th>Probs</th></tr>";
 75:     table.appendChild(thead);
 76:     const tbody = document.createElement("tbody");
 77:     rows.forEach((row) => {
 78:       const tr = document.createElement("tr");
 79:       const text = row.text != null ? String(row.text) : "";
 80:       const probs = row.probs_json != null ? String(row.probs_json) : "";
 81:       const probsShort = probs.length > 80 ? probs.slice(0, 77) + "..." : probs;
 82:       tr.innerHTML =
 83:         "<td>" +
 84:         escapeHtml(text.slice(0, 200)) +
 85:         (text.length > 200 ? "..." : "") +
 86:         "</td><td>" +
 87:         escapeHtml(String(row.true_label ?? "")) +
 88:         "</td><td>" +
 89:         escapeHtml(String(row.pred_label ?? "")) +
 90:         "</td><td>" +
 91:         (row.pred_confidence != null ? Number(row.pred_confidence).toFixed(4) : "-") +
 92:         "</td><td></td>";
 93:       const probsTd = tr.querySelector("td:last-child");
 94:       const trigger = document.createElement("button");
 95:       trigger.type = "button";
 96:       trigger.className = "probs-cell-trigger";
 97:       trigger.textContent = probsShort || "(empty)";
 98:       trigger.title = "Click to view full JSON";
 99:       trigger.addEventListener("click", () => showProbsModal(probs));
100:       probsTd.appendChild(trigger);
101:       tbody.appendChild(tr);
102:     });
103:     table.appendChild(tbody);
104:     trainMisclassifiedContent.appendChild(table);
105:   }
106:   function renderTrainResult(result) {
107:     trainMessage.classList.add("hidden");
108:     renderTrainMetrics(result);
109:     renderTrainMisclassified(result.misclassified);
110:   }
111:   if (probsModalClose) {
112:     probsModalClose.addEventListener("click", closeProbsModal);
113:   }
114:   if (probsModalOverlay) {
115:     probsModalOverlay.addEventListener("click", (e) => {
116:       if (e.target === probsModalOverlay) closeProbsModal();
117:     });
118:   }
119:   document.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
120:     if (e.key === "Escape") closeProbsModal();
121:   });
122:   if (probsModalResize && probsModal) {
123:     let startX = 0;
124:     let startY = 0;
125:     let startW = 0;
126:     let startH = 0;
127:     const onMove = (e) => {
128:       const dw = e.clientX - startX;
129:       const dh = e.clientY - startY;
130:       const w = Math.max(320, Math.min(window.innerWidth - 40, startW + dw));
131:       const h = Math.max(200, Math.min(window.innerHeight - 40, startH + dh));
132:       probsModal.style.width = w + "px";
133:       probsModal.style.height = h + "px";
134:       startX = e.clientX;
135:       startY = e.clientY;
136:       startW = w;
137:       startH = h;
138:     };
139:     const onUp = () => {
140:       document.removeEventListener("mousemove", onMove);
141:       document.removeEventListener("mouseup", onUp);
142:     };
143:     probsModalResize.addEventListener("mousedown", (e) => {
144:       e.preventDefault();
145:       startX = e.clientX;
146:       startY = e.clientY;
147:       startW = probsModal.offsetWidth;
148:       startH = probsModal.offsetHeight;
149:       document.addEventListener("mousemove", onMove);
150:       document.addEventListener("mouseup", onUp);
151:     });
152:   }
153:   trainRunBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
154:     if (trainRunBtn.disabled) return;
155:     setTrainRunning(true);
156:     try {
157:       const resp = await fetch("/api/train", {
158:         method: "POST",
159:         headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
160:       });
161:       const data = await resp.json();
162:       if (!resp.ok) {
163:         setTrainRunning(false);
164:         showTrainError(data.message || data.detail || "Start training failed");
165:         return;
166:       }
167:       const jobId = data.job_id;
168:       if (!jobId) {
169:         setTrainRunning(false);
170:         showTrainError("No job_id in response");
171:         return;
172:       }
173:       const eventUrl =
174:         window.location.origin + "/api/train/events/" + encodeURIComponent(jobId);
175:       const es = new EventSource(eventUrl);
176:       es.onmessage = (ev) => {
177:         es.close();
178:         setTrainRunning(false);
179:         try {
180:           const payload = JSON.parse(ev.data);
181:           if (payload.status === "completed" && payload.result) {
182:             renderTrainResult(payload.result);
183:           } else if (payload.status === "failed") {
184:             showTrainError(payload.error || "Training failed");
185:           } else {
186:             showTrainError("Unexpected event: " + (payload.status || "unknown"));
187:           }
188:         } catch (err) {
189:           showTrainError(err.message || "Invalid event data");
190:         }
191:       };
192:       es.onerror = () => {
193:         es.close();
194:         setTrainRunning(false);
195:         showTrainError("Connection to events stream failed");
196:       };
197:     } catch (err) {
198:       setTrainRunning(false);
199:       showTrainError(err.message || "Network error");
200:     }
201:   });
202:   trainLastBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
203:     if (trainLastBtn.disabled) return;
204:     trainLastBtn.disabled = true;
205:     trainMessage.classList.add("hidden");
206:     try {
207:       const resp = await fetch("/api/train/last");
208:       if (resp.status === 404) {
209:         showTrainMessage("No previous run found.", "info");
210:         trainMetrics.classList.add("hidden");
211:         trainMisclassified.classList.add("hidden");
212:         return;
213:       }
214:       if (!resp.ok) {
215:         showTrainError("Load last run failed: " + resp.status);
216:         return;
217:       }
218:       const result = await resp.json();
219:       renderTrainResult(result);
220:     } catch (err) {
221:       showTrainError(err.message || "Network error");
222:     } finally {
223:       trainLastBtn.disabled = false;
224:     }
225:   });
226: })();
````

## File: services/gateway/Dockerfile
````
 1: # Multi-stage Dockerfile per PROJECT_PLAN Section 8.1
 2: ARG PY_IMAGE=python:3.12-slim-bookworm
 3: 
 4: FROM ${PY_IMAGE} AS builder
 5: WORKDIR /build
 6: COPY requirements.txt .
 7: RUN pip wheel --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt -w /wheels
 8: 
 9: FROM ${PY_IMAGE} AS runtime
10: ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
11: 
12: RUN groupadd -r appgroup \
13:     && useradd -r -g appgroup -m -d /home/appuser -s /usr/sbin/nologin appuser
14: 
15: WORKDIR /app
16: 
17: COPY --from=builder /wheels /wheels
18: COPY requirements.txt .
19: RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --no-index --find-links=/wheels -r requirements.txt \
20:     && rm -rf /wheels
21: 
22: ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
23: ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
24: 
25: COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup app/ ./app/
26: 
27: USER appuser
28: EXPOSE 8000
29: 
30: HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
31:     CMD python -c "import sys,urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://127.0.0.1:8000/health', timeout=2); sys.exit(0)"
32: 
33: CMD ["gunicorn", "app.main:app", \
34:     "--bind", "0.0.0.0:8000", \
35:     "--workers", "2", \
36:     "--worker-class", "uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker", \
37:     "--no-control-socket", \
38:     "--access-logfile", "-", \
39:     "--error-logfile", "-"]
````

## File: services/image_service/app/main.py
````python
 1: """Image service - backend for image intents (FR-011, FR-067)."""
 2: from __future__ import annotations
 3: import json
 4: import logging
 5: import os
 6: import socket
 7: import sys
 8: from datetime import datetime, timezone
 9: from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
10: from fastapi import FastAPI
11: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
12: class JsonFormatter(logging.Formatter):
13:     """JSON-structured log format for request_id correlation (TECH-20)."""
14:     def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
15:         obj = {
16:             "ts": self.formatTime(record, self.datefmt),
17:             "level": record.levelname,
18:             "service": record.name,
19:             "msg": record.getMessage(),
20:         }
21:         if hasattr(record, "request_id"):
22:             obj["request_id"] = record.request_id
23:         return json.dumps(obj)
24: def setup_logging(service_name: str, level: str = "INFO") -> logging.Logger:
25:     """Configure JSON logging for request correlation (TECH-20.1)."""
26:     handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
27:     handler.setFormatter(JsonFormatter())
28:     logger = logging.getLogger(service_name)
29:     logger.setLevel(getattr(logging, level.upper(), logging.INFO))
30:     logger.addHandler(handler)
31:     return logger
32: logger = setup_logging("image-service", os.environ.get("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"))
33: class TraceEntry(BaseModel):
34:     """Trace entry schema: service, event, ts (ISO 8601), optional meta."""
35:     service: str
36:     event: str
37:     ts: str
38:     meta: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
39: def make_trace_entry(
40:     service: str,
41:     event: str,
42:     meta: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
43: ) -> TraceEntry:
44:     return TraceEntry(
45:         service=service,
46:         event=event,
47:         ts=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(timespec="milliseconds"),
48:         meta=meta,
49:     )
50: class HandleRequest(BaseModel):
51:     request_id: str
52:     text: str = Field(..., max_length=10000)
53: class HandleResponse(BaseModel):
54:     payload: Dict[str, Any]
55:     trace_append: Dict[str, Any]
56: app = FastAPI(title="Image Service", version="0.1.0")
57: @app.get("/health")
58: async def health():
59:     """Health check for Compose/load balancers."""
60:     return {"status": "ok"}
61: @app.post("/handle", response_model=HandleResponse)
62: async def handle(req: HandleRequest):
63:     """Handle image intent: simulated image-handling payload and trace entry."""
64:     logger.info(
65:         "handle request",
66:         extra={"request_id": req.request_id},
67:     )
68:     instance = socket.gethostname()
69:     payload = {
70:         "service": "image-service",
71:         "result": f"Image processing for: {req.text}",
72:         "instance": instance,
73:         "detected_labels": ["photo", "document", "graphic"],
74:         "dimensions": {"width": 1920, "height": 1080},
75:         "format": "PNG",
76:         "metadata": {"request_id": req.request_id},
77:     }
78:     trace = make_trace_entry(
79:         service="image-service",
80:         event="handled",
81:         meta={
82:             "status": 200,
83:             "instance": instance,
84:         },
85:     )
86:     return HandleResponse(
87:         payload=payload,
88:         trace_append=trace.model_dump(),
89:     )
````

## File: services/ops_service/app/main.py
````python
 1: """Ops service - backend for DevOps/ops intents (FR-012, FR-067)."""
 2: from __future__ import annotations
 3: import json
 4: import logging
 5: import os
 6: import socket
 7: import sys
 8: from datetime import datetime, timezone
 9: from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
10: from fastapi import FastAPI
11: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
12: class JsonFormatter(logging.Formatter):
13:     """JSON-structured log format for request_id correlation (TECH-20)."""
14:     def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
15:         obj = {
16:             "ts": self.formatTime(record, self.datefmt),
17:             "level": record.levelname,
18:             "service": record.name,
19:             "msg": record.getMessage(),
20:         }
21:         if hasattr(record, "request_id"):
22:             obj["request_id"] = record.request_id
23:         return json.dumps(obj)
24: def setup_logging(service_name: str, level: str = "INFO") -> logging.Logger:
25:     """Configure JSON logging for request correlation (TECH-20.1)."""
26:     handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
27:     handler.setFormatter(JsonFormatter())
28:     logger = logging.getLogger(service_name)
29:     logger.setLevel(getattr(logging, level.upper(), logging.INFO))
30:     logger.addHandler(handler)
31:     return logger
32: logger = setup_logging("ops-service", os.environ.get("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"))
33: class TraceEntry(BaseModel):
34:     """Trace entry schema: service, event, ts (ISO 8601), optional meta."""
35:     service: str
36:     event: str
37:     ts: str
38:     meta: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
39: def make_trace_entry(
40:     service: str,
41:     event: str,
42:     meta: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
43: ) -> TraceEntry:
44:     return TraceEntry(
45:         service=service,
46:         event=event,
47:         ts=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(timespec="milliseconds"),
48:         meta=meta,
49:     )
50: class HandleRequest(BaseModel):
51:     request_id: str
52:     text: str = Field(..., max_length=10000)
53: class HandleResponse(BaseModel):
54:     payload: Dict[str, Any]
55:     trace_append: Dict[str, Any]
56: app = FastAPI(title="Ops Service", version="0.1.0")
57: @app.get("/health")
58: async def health():
59:     """Health check for Compose/load balancers."""
60:     return {"status": "ok"}
61: @app.post("/handle", response_model=HandleResponse)
62: async def handle(req: HandleRequest):
63:     """Handle ops intent: simulated DevOps payload and trace entry."""
64:     logger.info(
65:         "handle request",
66:         extra={"request_id": req.request_id},
67:     )
68:     instance = socket.gethostname()
69:     payload = {
70:         "service": "ops-service",
71:         "result": f"Troubleshooting / diagnostics for: {req.text}",
72:         "instance": instance,
73:         "suggested_commands": [
74:             "kubectl get pods -A",
75:             "kubectl describe pod <name>",
76:             "kubectl logs <pod> --previous",
77:         ],
78:         "status_checks": ["API server", "etcd", "node readiness"],
79:         "metadata": {"request_id": req.request_id},
80:     }
81:     trace = make_trace_entry(
82:         service="ops-service",
83:         event="handled",
84:         meta={
85:             "status": 200,
86:             "instance": instance,
87:         },
88:     )
89:     return HandleResponse(
90:         payload=payload,
91:         trace_append=trace.model_dump(),
92:     )
````

## File: services/refiner/prompts.py
````python
  1: """
  2: Structured prompts for Ollama, using markdown sections: Heading, Role, Goal,
  3: Task/Workflow, Outputs, Guardrails/Rules.
  4: """
  5: # Labels for intent classification
  6: LABELS = ["search", "image", "ops", "unknown"]
  7: SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS = """# SYSTEM INSTRUCTIONS
  8: You are the data-quality assistant for an intent classification pipeline.
  9: You run offline, after training, to improve the dataset. You never participate
 10: in live request routing. Your outputs are proposals only; humans review before
 11: merge.
 12: ## DOMAIN: Intent Classification
 13: The system routes user prompts to one of four intents:
 14: - **search**: General web search, information retrieval, Q&A, lookup queries.
 15:   Examples: "what is kubernetes", "find hotels in Paris", "how to install docker"
 16: - **image**: Image generation, image analysis, visual content, picture-related.
 17:   Examples: "generate a logo", "detect objects in this photo", "create an
 18:   illustration of a cat"
 19: - **ops**: Operations, DevOps, infrastructure, troubleshooting, commands.
 20:   Examples: "kubectl get pods", "pod stuck in CrashLoopBackOff", "restart
 21:   deployment", "check service health"
 22: - **unknown**: Ambiguous, too vague, or out-of-scope. Use when intent is
 23:   unclear or the prompt could map to multiple labels.
 24:   Examples: "help", "hi", "do something", "analyze"
 25: ## OUTPUT FORMAT
 26: - Respond with valid JSON only. No markdown, no code fences (```), no
 27:   preamble or explanation before or after the JSON.
 28: - Use double quotes for strings. Escape internal quotes. No trailing commas.
 29: - When asked for a single object: output exactly one JSON object.
 30: - When asked for an array: output exactly one JSON array.
 31: ## QUALITY RULES
 32: - Be concise. Reasons in one sentence. No filler.
 33: - Confidence must be 0.0 to 1.0. Use lower values when uncertain.
 34: - Prefer `unknown` over guessing when the prompt is ambiguous.
 35: - For relabeling: the human true_label may be wrong; propose what you believe
 36:   is correct based on the text.
 37: - For augmentation: generate diverse, realistic prompts; avoid near-duplicates.
 38: ## GUARDRAILS
 39: - For structured tasks (relabel, augmentation): output only valid JSON. No
 40:   explanatory text, markdown, or code blocks.
 41: - For simple prompts (e.g. health check): respond exactly as the prompt
 42:   specifies.
 43: - Labels must be exactly: search, image, ops, unknown (lowercase).
 44: - Do not invent new labels or categories."""
 45: def connectivity_check() -> str:
 46:     """Prompt for verifying Ollama is reachable."""
 47:     return """# Connectivity Check
 48: ## ROLE
 49: You are a service responding to a health check.
 50: ## GOAL
 51: Confirm you are operational and ready to process requests.
 52: ## OUTPUT
 53: Reply with the word OK only.
 54: ## RULES
 55: - No explanation or extra text.
 56: - Exactly: OK"""
 57: def relabel_misclassified(text: str, true_label: str, pred_label: str) -> str:
 58:     """Prompt for proposing a corrected label for a misclassified row."""
 59:     labels_str = ", ".join(LABELS)
 60:     return f"""# Relabel Misclassified Intent
 61: ## ROLE
 62: You are a data quality assistant refining an intent classification dataset.
 63: You help correct labels for rows where the model predicted incorrectly.
 64: ## GOAL
 65: Review the misclassified row below and propose the correct label.
 66: Consider whether the human-provided true_label is correct or should change.
 67: ## TASK
 68: 1. Read the input row: user text, human true_label, model pred_label.
 69: 2. Decide the correct intent label.
 70: 3. Provide a brief reason and confidence (0.0-1.0).
 71: ## INPUT ROW
 72: - **text**: {text!r}
 73: - **true_label**: {true_label}
 74: - **pred_label**: {pred_label}
 75: ## OUTPUT
 76: Respond with valid JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no explanation.
 77: Schema:
 78: {{"suggested_label": "<label>", "reason": "<brief reason>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>}}
 79: ## GUARDRAILS
 80: - **suggested_label**: Must be one of: {labels_str}
 81: - **reason**: One sentence, no quotes or special chars that break JSON
 82: - **confidence**: Float between 0.0 and 1.0
 83: - Output must be parseable JSON; no extra text before or after"""
 84: def augment_examples(label: str, n: int = 5) -> str:
 85:     """Prompt for generating synthetic training examples for a label."""
 86:     labels_str = ", ".join(LABELS)
 87:     return f"""# Generate Synthetic Examples
 88: ## ROLE
 89: You are a data augmentation assistant for an intent classification dataset.
 90: You generate realistic, diverse training examples.
 91: ## GOAL
 92: Generate {n} synthetic user prompts that would correctly be classified as
 93: "{label}".
 94: ## TASK
 95: 1. Consider the intent label: {label}
 96: 2. Generate exactly {n} diverse, realistic user prompts (short queries or
 97:    commands)
 98: 3. Use synonyms and close paraphrases naturally (for example: create/make,
 99:    check/inspect, find/look up, troubleshoot/debug)
100: 4. Include intent-revealing phrases that imply routing even without explicit
101:    keywords (for example: "create me a logo" -> image, "what services are
102:    running" -> ops)
103: 5. For label "ops", cover a wide range of technical domains:
104:    DevOps, IT support, systems administration, cloud operations, and AI/ML ops
105: 6. For label "ops", include a mix of:
106:    - full commands (for example: "kubectl get pods -A")
107:    - partial command fragments (for example: "kubectl logs", "docker ps")
108:    - natural-language operational requests (for example: "check GPU memory usage")
109: 7. Each example should be clearly classifiable as {label}
110: 8. Avoid duplicates and near-duplicates within the generated set
111: 9. Vary phrasing, length, and style
112: ## OUTPUT
113: Respond with valid JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no explanation.
114: Schema: [{{"text": "<prompt>", "label": "{label}"}}, ...]
115: ## GUARDRAILS
116: - **label**: Must be one of: {labels_str}
117: - **text**: Short user prompt (max ~100 chars); no newlines
118: - Output must be a JSON array; no extra text before or after
119: - Examples must be distinct; avoid near-duplicates with minor wording changes"""
````

## File: services/search_service/app/main.py
````python
 1: """Search service - backend for search intents (FR-010, FR-067)."""
 2: from __future__ import annotations
 3: import json
 4: import logging
 5: import os
 6: import socket
 7: import sys
 8: from datetime import datetime, timezone
 9: from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
10: from fastapi import FastAPI
11: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
12: class JsonFormatter(logging.Formatter):
13:     """JSON-structured log format for request_id correlation (TECH-20)."""
14:     def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
15:         obj = {
16:             "ts": self.formatTime(record, self.datefmt),
17:             "level": record.levelname,
18:             "service": record.name,
19:             "msg": record.getMessage(),
20:         }
21:         if hasattr(record, "request_id"):
22:             obj["request_id"] = record.request_id
23:         return json.dumps(obj)
24: def setup_logging(service_name: str, level: str = "INFO") -> logging.Logger:
25:     """Configure JSON logging for request correlation (TECH-20.1)."""
26:     handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
27:     handler.setFormatter(JsonFormatter())
28:     logger = logging.getLogger(service_name)
29:     logger.setLevel(getattr(logging, level.upper(), logging.INFO))
30:     logger.addHandler(handler)
31:     return logger
32: logger = setup_logging("search-service", os.environ.get("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"))
33: class TraceEntry(BaseModel):
34:     """Trace entry schema: service, event, ts (ISO 8601), optional meta."""
35:     service: str
36:     event: str
37:     ts: str
38:     meta: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
39: def make_trace_entry(
40:     service: str,
41:     event: str,
42:     meta: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
43: ) -> TraceEntry:
44:     return TraceEntry(
45:         service=service,
46:         event=event,
47:         ts=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(timespec="milliseconds"),
48:         meta=meta,
49:     )
50: class HandleRequest(BaseModel):
51:     request_id: str
52:     text: str = Field(..., max_length=10000)
53: class HandleResponse(BaseModel):
54:     payload: Dict[str, Any]
55:     trace_append: Dict[str, Any]
56: app = FastAPI(title="Search Service", version="0.1.0")
57: @app.get("/health")
58: async def health():
59:     """Health check for Compose/load balancers."""
60:     return {"status": "ok"}
61: @app.post("/handle", response_model=HandleResponse)
62: async def handle(req: HandleRequest):
63:     """Handle search intent: simulated lookup payload and trace entry."""
64:     logger.info(
65:         "handle request",
66:         extra={"request_id": req.request_id},
67:     )
68:     instance = socket.gethostname()
69:     payload = {
70:         "service": "search-service",
71:         "result": f"Search results for: {req.text}",
72:         "instance": instance,
73:         "summary": f"Mock lookup completed for query (request_id={req.request_id})",
74:         "results": [
75:             "Result 1: relevant doc",
76:             "Result 2: related article",
77:             "Result 3: reference link",
78:         ],
79:     }
80:     trace = make_trace_entry(
81:         service="search-service",
82:         event="handled",
83:         meta={
84:             "status": 200,
85:             "instance": instance,
86:         },
87:     )
88:     return HandleResponse(
89:         payload=payload,
90:         trace_append=trace.model_dump(),
91:     )
````

## File: services/search_service/Dockerfile
````
 1: # Multi-stage Dockerfile per PROJECT_PLAN Section 8.1
 2: ARG PY_IMAGE=python:3.12-slim-bookworm
 3: 
 4: FROM ${PY_IMAGE} AS builder
 5: WORKDIR /build
 6: COPY requirements.txt .
 7: RUN pip wheel --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt -w /wheels
 8: 
 9: FROM ${PY_IMAGE} AS runtime
10: ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
11: 
12: RUN groupadd -r appgroup \
13:     && useradd -r -g appgroup -m -d /home/appuser -s /usr/sbin/nologin appuser
14: 
15: WORKDIR /app
16: 
17: COPY --from=builder /wheels /wheels
18: COPY requirements.txt .
19: RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --no-index --find-links=/wheels -r requirements.txt \
20:     && rm -rf /wheels
21: 
22: ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
23: ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
24: 
25: COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup app/ ./app/
26: 
27: USER appuser
28: EXPOSE 8000
29: 
30: HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
31:     CMD python -c "import sys,urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://127.0.0.1:8000/health', timeout=2); sys.exit(0)"
32: 
33: CMD ["gunicorn", "app.main:app", \
34:     "--bind", "0.0.0.0:8000", \
35:     "--workers", "2", \
36:     "--worker-class", "uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker", \
37:     "--no-control-socket", \
38:     "--access-logfile", "-", \
39:     "--error-logfile", "-"]
````
