339:                 etl = _handle_task_etl(cfg=cfg, pool=pool, task=task)
340:                 _write_text(
341:                     _batch_raw_output_path(cfg, run_id, task.batch_id),
342:                     etl["raw"],
343:                 )
344:                 _write_text(
345:                     _batch_prompt_output_path(cfg, run_id, task.batch_id),
346:                     etl["prompt"],
347:                 )
348:                 _write_json(
349:                     _batch_validation_output_path(cfg, run_id, task.batch_id),
350:                     etl["validation"],
351:                 )
352:                 rejected = etl["validation"].get("rejected_items") or []
353:                 if rejected:
354:                     _write_csv(
355:                         _batch_rejected_output_path(cfg, run_id, task.batch_id),
356:                         rejected,
357:                         fieldnames=[
358:                             "idx",
359:                             "error_code",
360:                             "text",
361:                             "suggested_label",
362:                             "reason",
363:                             "confidence",
364:                         ],
365:                     )
366:                 if etl.get("error"):
367:                     # Preserve legacy behavior: treat invalid JSON as a failed task
368:                     # so it can be retried. Artifacts are still written above.
369:                     raise ValueError(str(etl["error"]))
370:                 out_path = _batch_output_path(cfg, run_id, task.batch_id)
371:                 _write_csv(
372:                     out_path,
373:                     etl["accepted"],
374:                     fieldnames=["text", "suggested_label", "reason", "confidence"],
375:                 )
376:                 stream_ack(cfg.relabel_tasks_stream, group, entry_id)
377:                 with done_lock:
378:                     done_batches.add(task.batch_id)
379:                     completed = len(done_batches)
380:                 evt = {
381:                     "status": "progress",
382:                     "phase": "relabel",
383:                     "detail": f"Relabel batch {completed}/{expected_batches} complete",
384:                     "batch_id": task.batch_id,
385:                 }
386:                 if progress:
387:                     progress(evt)
388:                 publish_event(cfg.events_channel(run_id), evt)
389:             except (ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError, requests.RequestException, KeyError, TypeError) as e:
390:                 err_evt = {
391:                     "status": "progress",
392:                     "phase": "relabel",
393:                     "detail": f"Relabel batch failed: {e}",
394:                     "error": str(e),
395:                     "entry_id": entry_id,
396:                     "batch_id": fields.get("batch_id"),
397:                 }
398:                 if progress:
399:                     progress(err_evt)
400:                 publish_event(cfg.events_channel(run_id), err_evt)
401:                 # Decide whether to leave pending for retry or mark permanently failed.
402:                 deliveries = stream_get_delivery_count(
403:                     cfg.relabel_tasks_stream,
404:                     group,
405:                     entry_id,
406:                 )
407:                 if deliveries is not None and deliveries >= max_retries:
408:                     # Give up on this batch so the overall job can complete.
409:                     stream_ack(cfg.relabel_tasks_stream, group, entry_id)
410:                     with done_lock:
411:                         done_batches.add(str(fields.get("batch_id") or entry_id.replace("-", "_")))
412:     threads = []
413:     for i in range(cfg.relabel_max_parallel_batches):
414:         t = threading.Thread(target=_worker, args=(i,), daemon=True)
415:         threads.append(t)
416:         t.start()
417:     for t in threads:
418:         t.join()
419: def merge_relabel_outputs(
420:     cfg: RefineConfig,
421:     *,
422:     run_id: str,
423:     train_df: pd.DataFrame,
424: ) -> pd.DataFrame:
425:     relabel_dir = cfg.relabel_dir(run_id)
426:     relabel_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
427:     batches_dir = relabel_dir / "batches"
428:     relabel_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
429:     if batches_dir.exists():
430:         for p in sorted(batches_dir.glob("proposed_relabels.batch_*.csv")):
431:             with open(p, encoding="utf-8", newline="") as f:
432:                 relabel_rows.extend(list(csv.DictReader(f)))
433:     # Filter: valid labels, non-empty text, keep last suggestion per text
434:     latest: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
435:     rejected_count = 0
436:     overwrite_count = 0
437:     for r in relabel_rows:
438:         text = str(r.get("text") or "").strip()
439:         suggested = str(r.get("suggested_label") or "").strip()
440:         if not text or suggested not in LABELS:
441:             rejected_count += 1
442:             continue
443:         if text in latest:
444:             overwrite_count += 1
445:         latest[text] = r
446:     merged_rows = list(latest.values())
447:     # Confidence gating: only apply label changes when the LLM both
448:     # suggests a different label than the existing base/true label and
449:     # is sufficiently confident.
450:     base_by_text: dict[str, str] = {
451:         str(t).strip(): str(l).strip()
452:         for t, l in zip(
453:             train_df["text"].astype(str).str.strip(),
454:             train_df["label"].astype(str).str.strip(),
455:         )
456:     }
457:     min_conf = float(getattr(cfg, "relabel_min_confidence", 0.85))
458:     applied_count = 0
459:     skipped_same_as_base = 0
460:     skipped_low_confidence = 0
461:     skipped_invalid_confidence = 0
462:     proposed_map: dict[str, str] = {}
463:     for r in merged_rows:
464:         text = str(r.get("text") or "").strip()
465:         if text not in base_by_text:
466:             continue
467:         suggested = str(r.get("suggested_label") or "").strip()
468:         base_label = base_by_text[text]
469:         # Parse confidence for gating.
470:         try:
471:             conf = float(r.get("confidence") or 0.0)
472:         except (TypeError, ValueError):
473:             conf = -1.0
474:         if suggested == base_label:
475:             skipped_same_as_base += 1
476:             continue
477:         if conf < 0.0:
478:             skipped_invalid_confidence += 1
479:             continue
480:         if conf < min_conf:
481:             skipped_low_confidence += 1
482:             continue
483:         proposed_map[text] = suggested
484:         applied_count += 1
485:     _write_json(
486:         relabel_dir / "merge_relabel.validation.json",
487:         {
488:             "input_rows_count": int(len(relabel_rows)),
489:             "accepted_rows_count": int(len(merged_rows)),
490:             "rejected_rows_count": int(rejected_count),
491:             "overwrites_kept_last_count": int(overwrite_count),
492:             "min_confidence": float(min_conf),
493:             "proposed_rows_total": int(len(merged_rows)),
494:             "applied_rows_count": int(applied_count),
495:             "skipped_same_as_base_count": int(skipped_same_as_base),
496:             "skipped_low_confidence_count": int(skipped_low_confidence),
497:             "skipped_invalid_confidence_count": int(skipped_invalid_confidence),
498:         },
499:     )
500:     _write_csv(
501:         cfg.relabel_merged_csv(run_id),
502:         merged_rows,
503:         fieldnames=["text", "suggested_label", "reason", "confidence"],
504:     )
505:     result = train_df.copy()
506:     mask = result["text"].astype(str).str.strip().isin(proposed_map.keys())
507:     result.loc[mask, "label"] = result.loc[mask, "text"].astype(str).str.strip().map(proposed_map)
508:     result.to_csv(cfg.relabel_candidate_csv(run_id), index=False)
509:     return result
510: def run_relabel_phase(
511:     cfg: RefineConfig,
512:     *,
513:     model_artifacts_path: str,
514:     promote_target_path: str,
515:     run_id: str,
516:     progress: Callable[[dict[str, Any]], None] | None = None,
517: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
518:     cfg.ensure_run_dir(run_id)
519:     cfg.relabel_dir(run_id).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
520:     misclassified = _read_misclassified(model_artifacts_path)
521:     if not misclassified:
522:         raise RuntimeError("misclassified.csv is empty - nothing to relabel")
523:     metrics_before = _read_metrics(model_artifacts_path)
524:     cfg.metrics_before_path(run_id).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
525:     with open(cfg.metrics_before_path(run_id), "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
526:         json.dump(metrics_before, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
527:     train_df = _read_train_csv(promote_target_path)
528:     # Create the consumer group BEFORE enqueueing so workers don't miss entries.
529:     # stream_group_create handles BUSYGROUP silently, so the redundant call
530:     # inside run_relabel_workers is harmless.
531:     stream_group_create(
532:         cfg.relabel_tasks_stream, f"{cfg.relabel_consumer_group}:{run_id}"
533:     )
534:     total_batches = enqueue_relabel_batches(cfg, run_id=run_id, misclassified_rows=misclassified)
535:     start_evt = {
536:         "status": "progress",
537:         "phase": "relabel",
538:         "detail": f"Enqueued {total_batches} relabel batches",
539:         "batches": total_batches,
540:     }
541:     if progress:
542:         progress(start_evt)
543:     publish_event(cfg.events_channel(run_id), start_evt)
544:     run_relabel_workers(cfg, run_id=run_id, expected_batches=total_batches, progress=progress)
545:     candidate_df = merge_relabel_outputs(cfg, run_id=run_id, train_df=train_df)
546:     # Train candidate and persist metrics for UI comparison
547:     compose_working_dir = os.environ.get("COMPOSE_WORKING_DIR", ".")
548:     metrics_after = train_candidate(
549:         compose_working_dir=compose_working_dir,
550:         candidate_csv=cfg.relabel_candidate_csv(run_id),
551:         metrics_out=cfg.relabel_candidate_metrics_json(run_id),
552:         timeout_seconds=int(os.environ.get("RUN_TRAIN_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "3600")),
553:     )
554:     return {
555:         "run_id": run_id,
556:         "batches": total_batches,
557:         "train_relabel_candidate_rows": int(len(candidate_df)),
558:         "metrics_before": metrics_before,
559:         "metrics_after": metrics_after,
560:         "proposed_relabels": _read_csv_rows(cfg.relabel_merged_csv(run_id)),
561:     }
````

## File: services/training-api/app/redis_client.py
````python
  1: """Redis client and job state helpers for train/refine flows.
  2: Per TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH §3: job keys (job:train:{id},
  3: job:refine:{id}), Pub/Sub channels (job:train:events:{id}, etc.), TTL 24h.
  4: """
  5: from __future__ import annotations
  6: import json
  7: import logging
  8: import os
  9: import threading
 10: from typing import Any, Iterator
 11: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 12: JOB_STATE_TTL_SECONDS = 24 * 3600
 13: _client: Any = None
 14: _publish_client: Any = None
 15: _client_lock = threading.Lock()
 16: _publish_lock = threading.Lock()
 17: def _redis_url() -> str | None:
 18:     url = os.getenv("REDIS_URL")
 19:     if url:
 20:         return url
 21:     host = os.getenv("REDIS_HOST", "redis")
 22:     port = os.getenv("REDIS_PORT", "6379")
 23:     if host and port:
 24:         return f"redis://{host}:{port}/0"
 25:     return None
 26: def _make_redis_client():  # noqa: ANN201
 27:     url = _redis_url()
 28:     if not url:
 29:         raise RuntimeError(
 30:             "Redis not configured: set REDIS_URL or REDIS_HOST and REDIS_PORT"
 31:         )
 32:     import redis
 33:     return redis.from_url(url, decode_responses=True)
 34: def get_connection():  # noqa: ANN201
 35:     """Return a connected Redis client for reads and subscribe."""
 36:     global _client
 37:     if _client is not None:
 38:         return _client
 39:     with _client_lock:
 40:         if _client is not None:
 41:             return _client
 42:         _client = _make_redis_client()
 43:         return _client
 44: def get_publish_connection():  # noqa: ANN201
 45:     """Return a dedicated Redis connection for set/publish."""
 46:     global _publish_client
 47:     if _publish_client is not None:
 48:         return _publish_client
 49:     with _publish_lock:
 50:         if _publish_client is not None:
 51:             return _publish_client
 52:         _publish_client = _make_redis_client()
 53:         return _publish_client
 54: def set_job_state(key: str, payload: dict[str, Any], ttl: int = JOB_STATE_TTL_SECONDS) -> None:
 55:     """Store job state at key (e.g. job:train:{id}, job:refine:{id}). TTL in seconds."""
 56:     conn = get_publish_connection()
 57:     conn.set(key, json.dumps(payload), ex=ttl)
 58: def get_job_state(key: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
 59:     """Read job state; returns parsed JSON or None if missing/expired."""
 60:     conn = get_connection()
 61:     raw = conn.get(key)
 62:     if raw is None:
 63:         return None
 64:     try:
 65:         return json.loads(raw)
 66:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
 67:         return None
 68: def publish_job_event(channel: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
 69:     """Publish payload to channel (e.g. job:train:events:{id}). One message per job."""
 70:     conn = get_publish_connection()
 71:     conn.publish(channel, json.dumps(payload))
 72: def publish_event(channel: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
 73:     """Publish an event payload to an arbitrary channel."""
 74:     publish_job_event(channel, payload)
 75: def subscribe_to_job_channel(channel: str) -> Iterator[str]:
 76:     """Subscribe to channel and yield the first message. Used by SSE endpoints."""
 77:     conn = get_connection()
 78:     pubsub = conn.pubsub()
 79:     pubsub.subscribe(channel)
 80:     try:
 81:         for message in pubsub.listen():
 82:             if message["type"] == "message":
 83:                 yield message["data"]
 84:                 break
 85:     finally:
 86:         pubsub.close()
 87: def subscribe_to_job_channel_until_done(channel: str) -> Iterator[str]:
 88:     """Subscribe to channel and yield messages until a completed/failed status is received.
 89:     Used by refine SSE endpoint to stream progress events followed by a final result.
 90:     """
 91:     conn = get_connection()
 92:     pubsub = conn.pubsub()
 93:     pubsub.subscribe(channel)
 94:     try:
 95:         for message in pubsub.listen():
 96:             if message["type"] != "message":
 97:                 continue
 98:             data = message["data"]
 99:             yield data
100:             try:
101:                 parsed = json.loads(data)
102:                 if parsed.get("status") in ("completed", "failed"):
103:                     break
104:             except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
105:                 pass
106:     finally:
107:         pubsub.close()
108: def stream_add(stream: str, fields: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
109:     """XADD to a Redis Stream and return entry id."""
110:     conn = get_publish_connection()
111:     payload = {k: json.dumps(v) if isinstance(v, (dict, list)) else str(v) for k, v in fields.items()}
112:     return conn.xadd(stream, payload)
113: def stream_group_create(stream: str, group: str) -> None:
114:     """Create a consumer group if it does not exist (MKSTREAM)."""
115:     conn = get_publish_connection()
116:     try:
117:         conn.xgroup_create(name=stream, groupname=group, id="$", mkstream=True)
118:     except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
119:         import redis as _redis
120:         if isinstance(e, _redis.ResponseError) and "BUSYGROUP" in str(e):
121:             pass
122:         else:
123:             raise
124: def stream_read_group(
125:     stream: str,
126:     group: str,
127:     consumer: str,
128:     *,
129:     count: int = 1,
130:     block_ms: int = 1000,
131: ) -> list[tuple[str, dict[str, str]]]:
132:     """Read entries from a stream as part of a consumer group.
133:     Returns a list of (entry_id, fields) items.
134:     """
135:     conn = get_connection()
136:     resp = conn.xreadgroup(
137:         groupname=group,
138:         consumername=consumer,
139:         streams={stream: ">"},
140:         count=count,
141:         block=block_ms,
142:     )
143:     items: list[tuple[str, dict[str, str]]] = []
144:     for _stream_name, entries in resp:
145:         for entry_id, fields in entries:
146:             items.append((entry_id, fields))
147:     return items
148: def stream_ack(stream: str, group: str, entry_id: str) -> None:
149:     conn = get_publish_connection()
150:     conn.xack(stream, group, entry_id)
151: def stream_auto_claim_pending(
152:     stream: str,
153:     group: str,
154:     consumer: str,
155:     *,
156:     min_idle_ms: int = 300_000,
157:     count: int = 1,
158: ) -> list[tuple[str, dict[str, str]]]:
159:     """Claim idle pending entries for a consumer using XAUTOCLAIM.
160:     Returns a list of (entry_id, fields) items, similar to stream_read_group.
161:     """
162:     try:
163:         conn = get_connection()
164:     except RuntimeError:
165:         return []
166:     # xautoclaim returns (next_start_id, [ [entry_id, {field: value}], ... ])
167:     _next_id, entries = conn.xautoclaim(  # noqa: F841
168:         name=stream,
169:         groupname=group,
170:         consumername=consumer,
171:         min_idle_time=min_idle_ms,
172:         start_id="0-0",
173:         count=count,
174:     )
175:     items: list[tuple[str, dict[str, str]]] = []
176:     for entry_id, fields in entries:
177:         items.append((entry_id, fields))
178:     return items
179: def stream_get_delivery_count(stream: str, group: str, entry_id: str) -> int | None:
180:     """Return the delivery count for a specific pending entry, if available.
181:     Uses XPENDING RANGE to query a single entry; returns None if the entry is
182:     not pending or on error.
183:     """
184:     try:
185:         conn = get_connection()
186:     except RuntimeError:
187:         return None
188:     try:
189:         pending = conn.xpending_range(
190:             name=stream,
191:             groupname=group,
192:             min=entry_id,
193:             max=entry_id,
194:             count=1,
195:         )
196:     except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
197:         logger.debug("Failed to query pending entries for %s/%s", stream, entry_id, exc_info=True)
198:         return None
199:     if not pending:
200:         return None
201:     _id, _consumer, _idle, deliveries = pending[0]
202:     try:
203:         return int(deliveries)
204:     except (TypeError, ValueError):
205:         return None
206: def _verify() -> None:
207:     """Smoke test: set/get job state and pub/sub. Run with python -m app.redis_client."""
208:     import threading
209:     import time
210:     import uuid
211:     key = f"job:train:{uuid.uuid4()}"
212:     channel = f"job:train:events:{uuid.uuid4()}"
213:     payload = {"status": "completed", "result": {"accuracy": 0.9}}
214:     set_job_state(key, payload, ttl=60)
215:     got = get_job_state(key)
216:     assert got == payload, f"get_job_state: got {got}"
217:     received: list[str] = []
218:     def listen() -> None:
219:         first = next(subscribe_to_job_channel(channel), None)
220:         if first is not None:
221:             received.append(first)
222:     listener = threading.Thread(target=listen)
223:     listener.start()
224:     time.sleep(0.2)
225:     # publish_job_event uses get_publish_connection() so subscriber can hold default conn
226:     publish_job_event(channel, payload)
227:     listener.join(timeout=5.0)
228:     assert len(received) == 1, f"expected one message, got {len(received)}"
229:     assert json.loads(received[0]) == payload
230:     get_connection().delete(key)
231:     print("redis_client: set_job_state, get_job_state, publish_job_event, subscribe_to_job_channel OK")
232: if __name__ == "__main__":
233:     _verify()
````

## File: docs/auxiliary/architecture/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH.md
````markdown
  1: # Train and Refine GUI Pages: Technical Architecture
  2: 
  3: <!-- TASK-REF: B1..B12 = batches in TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TASKS.md -->
  4: 
  5: Technical architecture for the Train and Refine frontend pages, training-api
  6: service, Redis-backed job state, and event-driven completion via SSE. Intended
  7: for implementers and integration. For product requirements see
  8: [TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PRD.md](../requirements/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PRD.md);
  9: for planning see [TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PLAN.md](../planning/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PLAN.md).
 10: 
 11: ## Table of Contents
 12: 
 13: 1. [Technical Goals and Constraints](#1-technical-goals-and-constraints)
 14: 2. [Component Architecture](#2-component-architecture)
 15: 3. [Event-Driven Flow](#3-event-driven-flow)
 16: 4. [Training API](#4-training-api)
 17: 5. [Gateway Proxy](#5-gateway-proxy)
 18: 6. [Frontend Technical Design](#6-frontend-technical-design)
 19: 7. [Data Contracts](#7-data-contracts)
 20: 8. [Docker Compose and Volumes](#8-docker-compose-and-volumes)
 21: 9. [Security and Robustness](#9-security-and-robustness)
 22: 10. [File and Implementation Summary](#10-file-and-implementation-summary)
 23: 
 24: ## 1. Technical Goals and Constraints
 25: <!-- Tasks: B1 B3 B4 -->
 26: 
 27: ### 1.1 Goals
 28: 
 29: - **Event-driven completion:** No polling; job completion notified via Redis
 30:   PUBLISH and SSE stream to the client. No long HTTP timeouts or sleep loops.
 31: - **Single implementation:** Train, refine, and promote implemented once in
 32:   Python; triggered by (1) HTTP API (UI) and (2) CLI via Docker Compose
 33:   (scripts). No duplicated business logic.
 34: - **Platform alignment:** Same stack as main project: Docker Compose,
 35:   Python/FastAPI. Redis as a dedicated service in the same Compose file.
 36: 
 37: ### 1.2 Current State (Pre-requisites)
 38: 
 39: - **Frontend:** Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS in `services/gateway/app/static/`; single
 40:   page, no router; no tables today (only list-based trace timeline).
 41: - **Train/Refine:** One-shot containers via `docker compose --profile train run
 42:   --rm trainer` and `docker compose --profile refine run --rm refiner`. They
 43:   read/write the **model_artifacts** volume and host-mounted `train.csv`. The
 44:   gateway has no access to this volume (cannot read metrics, misclassified, or
 45:   refinement outputs).
 46: - **Trainer outputs:** `metrics.json`, `misclassified.csv` (columns: text,
 47:   true_label, pred_label, pred_confidence, probs_json).
 48: - **Refiner outputs:** `refinement_report.json`, `proposed_relabels.csv`,
 49:   `proposed_examples.csv`, `train_candidate.csv`, `metrics_before.json`.
 50:   Comparison "after" metrics require running the trainer on `train_candidate.csv`
 51:   to produce `metrics_candidate.json` (as in `scripts/promote.sh`).
 52: 
 53: ## 2. Component Architecture
 54: <!-- Tasks: B3 B4 B5 B6 B7 -->
 55: 
 56: ### 2.1 Components
 57: 
 58: | Component | Role |
 59: | --- | --- |
 60: | **Browser UI** | Query, Train, Refine; hash-based routing (`#`, `#train`, `#refine`) |
 61: | **Gateway** | Proxies POST/GET to training-api; streaming proxy for SSE; no direct UI |
 62: | **Training API** | FastAPI service; runs train/refine/promote in Python; Redis job state and Pub/Sub; reads/writes model_artifacts volume |
 63: | **Redis** | Dedicated service; job keys (TTL); Pub/Sub channels for completion |
 64: | **Trainer / Refiner** | Existing one-shot containers; invoked by training-api via Docker Compose |
 65: 
 66: ### 2.2 Data Flow
 67: 
 68: - UI sends POST to gateway; gateway proxies to training-api. Training-api
 69:   creates job, stores state in Redis, spawns background task, returns
 70:   `job_id` immediately.
 71: - Background task runs trainer/refiner via Docker Compose; on completion reads
 72:   artifacts from model_artifacts volume, SETs Redis key, PUBLISHes to Redis
 73:   channel.
 74: - UI opens EventSource to gateway; gateway streams SSE from training-api. Training-api
 75:   SUBSCRIBEs to Redis channel; on first message sends SSE event to client and
 76:   closes stream. No polling.
 77: 
 78: ### 2.3 Architecture Diagram
 79: 
 80: ```mermaid
 81: flowchart LR
 82:   subgraph browser [Browser]
 83:     UI[Query / Train / Refine]
 84:   end
 85:   subgraph gateway [Gateway]
 86:     G[FastAPI]
 87:   end
 88:   subgraph new [Training API]
 89:     API[FastAPI + Docker]
 90:     Vol[model_artifacts volume]
 91:   end
 92:   subgraph existing [Existing]
 93:     Trainer[trainer container]
 94:     Refiner[refiner container]
 95:   end
 96:   subgraph redis_svc [Redis]
 97:     Redis[Redis]
 98:   end
 99:   UI -->|POST start job| G
100:   UI -->|SSE stream| G
101:   G -->|proxy POST, proxy SSE| API
102:   API -->|SET key, PUBLISH| Redis
103:   API -->|SUBSCRIBE, stream SSE| Redis
104:   API -->|docker compose run| Trainer
105:   API -->|docker compose run| Refiner
106:   Trainer -->|writes| Vol
107:   Refiner -->|reads/writes| Vol
108:   API -->|reads| Vol
109: ```
110: 
111: ## 3. Event-Driven Flow
112: <!-- Tasks: B2 B5 B6 B8 -->
113: 
114: ### 3.1 Redis Keys
115: 
116: - **Key pattern:** `job:train:{job_id}` and `job:refine:{job_id}`.
117: - **Value:** JSON: `{ "status", "result" (when completed), "error" (when failed),
118:   "created_at" }`.
119: - **TTL:** 24h (86400s) to limit storage.
120: 
121: ### 3.2 Redis Pub/Sub
122: 
123: - **Channels:** `job:train:events:{job_id}` and `job:refine:events:{job_id}`.
124: - **Message:** On job completion, training-api PUBLISHes final payload (e.g.
125:   `{"status":"completed","result":{...}}` or `{"status":"failed","error":"..."}`).
126:   One message per job; client receives it once via SSE.
127: 
128: ### 3.3 SSE Endpoints
129: 
130: - **Training-api:** GET `/train/events/{job_id}` and GET `/refine/events/{job_id}`.
131:   Each SUBSCRIBEs to the Redis channel; on first message, sends it as an SSE
132:   event to the client and closes the stream.
133: - **Gateway:** Proxies GET `/api/train/events/{job_id}` and
134:   GET `/api/refine/events/{job_id}` as streaming responses so that
135:   `EventSource` can connect to the gateway origin. No buffering; stream
136:   response body from training-api to client.
137: 
138: ### 3.4 Client Flow
139: 
140: 1. POST to start job; receive `{ "job_id": "..." }`.
141: 2. Open `EventSource(url)` to `/api/train/events/{job_id}` (or refine).
142: 3. Show indeterminate progress until one SSE event received.
143: 4. On event: close EventSource, hide progress, render result or error.
144: 
145: ## 4. Training API
146: <!-- Tasks: B1 B3 B4 B5 B6 B7 -->
147: 
148: ### 4.1 Location and Modes
149: 
150: - **Location:** `services/training-api/`.
151: - **HTTP server (default CMD):** Serves FastAPI app for UI; healthcheck and
152:   REDIS_URL/REDIS_HOST/REDIS_PORT configuration.
153: - **CLI mode:** Override for `docker compose run training-api promote` (or
154:   `train`, `refine` subcommands). Same Python code as HTTP handlers; entrypoint
155:   e.g. `python -m app.cli promote`.
156: 
157: ### 4.2 Volume and Artifacts
158: 
159: - **Volume layout:** Unchanged. Trainer outputs at `/model/` (same volume as
160:   refiner's `/data/`); training-api mounts model_artifacts at one path to read
161:   both. When the background task finishes, it reads artifact files and puts
162:   parsed result into Redis so status endpoint can return it without re-reading
163:   the volume.
164: - **Promotion:** Read-write mount of host `train.csv` at e.g.
165:   `/promote_target/train.csv` so POST /refine/promote can write the promoted
166:   dataset.
167: 
168: ### 4.3 Endpoints (Training API)
169: 
170: | Method | Path | Behavior |
171: | --- | --- | --- |
172: | POST | /train | Create job; store "pending" in Redis; spawn background run_train(); return `{ "job_id" }` |
173: | GET | /train/events/{job_id} | SSE: SUBSCRIBE to job:train:events:{job_id}; stream first message; close |
174: | GET | /train/status/{job_id} | Read Redis key; return job_id, status, result, error; 404 if unknown/expired |
175: | GET | /train/last | Read last run from volume; same shape as result; 404 if not present |
176: | POST | /refine/relabel | Create job; background relabel run; return `{ "job_id", "run_id" }` |
177: | GET | /refine/relabel/events/{job_id} | SSE: SUBSCRIBE to job:refine:relabel:events:{job_id}; stream messages until completed/failed; close |
178: | POST | /refine/augment | Create job; background augment run; return `{ "job_id", "run_id" }` |
179: | GET | /refine/augment/events/{job_id} | SSE: SUBSCRIBE to job:refine:augment:events:{job_id}; stream messages until completed/failed; close |
180: | POST | /refine/promote | Synchronous run_promote(); body `{ "run_id": "..." }` optional. Return `{ "promoted", "message", "acc_before", "acc_after", "promote_accuracy_tolerance", "used_tolerance", "per_label_recall" }`; 400 if candidate missing; 200 with promoted: false if accuracy is below `acc_before - tolerance` (unless baseline accuracy was zero) |
181: 
182: ### 4.4 Background Job Behavior
183: 
184: - On success: read artifacts (metrics.json, misclassified.csv or refinement
185:   outputs), SET Redis key with status "completed" and result payload, PUBLISH
186:   to events channel, then exit.
187: - On failure: SET Redis key with status "failed" and error, PUBLISH to events
188:   channel. Apply process timeout (train 1h/3600s, refine 10 min/600s) so a
189:   stuck run does not leak resources.
190: 
191: ## 5. Gateway Proxy
192: <!-- Tasks: B7 B8 -->
193: 
194: ### 5.1 Configuration
195: 
196: - **Env:** `TRAINING_API_URL` (e.g. `http://training-api:8000`). UI talks only
197:   to gateway; gateway proxies to training-api on internal network.
198: 
199: ### 5.2 Route Mapping
200: 
201: | Gateway Route | Training-API Target | Notes |
202: | --- | --- | --- |
203: | POST /api/train | POST /train | Normal timeout |
204: | GET /api/train/events/{job_id} | GET /train/events/{job_id} | Streaming proxy (SSE) |
205: | GET /api/train/status/{job_id} | GET /train/status/{job_id} | |
206: | GET /api/train/last | GET /train/last | |
207: | POST /api/refine/relabel | POST /refine/relabel | Normal timeout |
208: | GET /api/refine/relabel/events/{job_id} | GET /refine/relabel/events/{job_id} | Streaming proxy (SSE) |
209: | POST /api/refine/augment | POST /refine/augment | Normal timeout |
210: | GET /api/refine/augment/events/{job_id} | GET /refine/augment/events/{job_id} | Streaming proxy (SSE) |
211: | POST /api/refine/promote | POST /refine/promote | Longer timeout (5 min / 300s) |
212: 
213: ### 5.3 Streaming Proxy
214: 
215: - For GET .../events/{job_id}, gateway must stream the response body from
216:   training-api to the client (no full buffering). Use streaming HTTP client
217:   and streaming response so EventSource on gateway origin receives the SSE
218:   event. Normal proxy timeouts do not apply for the duration of the SSE
219:   stream (long-lived until one event delivered).
220: 
221: ## 6. Frontend Technical Design
222: <!-- Tasks: B9 B10 B11 -->
223: 
224: ### 6.1 Navigation
225: 
226: - Nav links: "Query | Train | Refine" setting `location.hash` to `#`,
227:   `#train`, `#refine`.
228: - On `load` and `hashchange`: show section for current hash; hide others.
229:   Default view remains existing query form and result section.
230: 
231: ### 6.2 Train Page
232: 
233: - **Run training:** Button POSTs to /api/train; on response parse job_id; show
234:   indeterminate progress bar; open EventSource to
235:   `/api/train/events/{job_id}`. On one SSE event (completed or failed), close
236:   EventSource, hide progress, render result or error.
237: - **Load last run (optional):** GET /api/train/last when no run triggered this
238:   session; render same metrics and misclassified tables.
239: - **Metrics:** One row/summary for accuracy; table for classification_report
240:   (rows = labels, columns = precision, recall, f1-score, support); table for
241:   confusion_matrix with row/column labels.
242: - **Misclassified:** `<table>` with columns text, true_label, pred_label,
243:   pred_confidence, probs_json (or truncated). Use thead/tbody.
244: 
245: ### 6.3 Refine Page
246: 
247: - **Run relabeling:** POST /api/refine/relabel; progress bar; EventSource
248:   /api/refine/relabel/events/{job_id}; on event render comparison and tables.
249: - **Run augmentation:** POST /api/refine/augment; progress bar; EventSource
250:   /api/refine/augment/events/{job_id}; on event render comparison and tables.
251: - **Report:** Summary from refinement_report (rows_processed, relabels_proposed,
252:   examples_proposed, rows_skipped, errors) as small table or definition list.
253: - **Comparison:** Metrics before and metrics after in same structure as Train
254:   page; side-by-side or one table with Before/After columns.
255: - **Promote:** Button POSTs to /api/refine/promote; show loading (promotion may
256:   take several minutes). On success show "Promoted" with acc_before/acc_after or
257:   a tolerance message when `used_tolerance` is true; or "Metrics did not
258:   improve; candidate discarded" when below threshold; optionally remind to
259:   restart ai_router. On error (e.g. train_candidate missing) show message.
260: - **Tabulated data:** Proposed relabels table, proposed examples table, train
261:   candidate table (sample or full with pagination if large).
262: 
263: ### 6.4 Styling and UX
264: 
265: - Reuse `services/gateway/app/static/styles.css` (CSS variables, layout).
266: - Add `.data-table` (borders, alternating rows), `.train-section`,
267:   `.refine-section`. Indeterminate progress bar animation.
268: - Disable trigger buttons and show "Running..." while EventSource open or
269:   promote in flight; errors in message area.
270: 
271: ## 7. Data Contracts
272: <!-- Tasks: B3 B4 B10 B11 -->
273: 
274: ### 7.1 Metrics (from trainer)
275: 
276: ```json
277: {
278:   "accuracy": 0.92,
279:   "classification_report": {
280:     "<label>": {
281:       "precision": 0.9,
282:       "recall": 0.88,
283:       "f1-score": 0.89,
284:       "support": 50
285:     }
286:   },
287:   "confusion_matrix": [[...]]
288: }
289: ```
290: 
291: - Flatten classification_report to table rows; confusion_matrix row/col headers
292:   from label order.
293: 
294: ### 7.2 Misclassified
295: 
296: - Array of objects: `{ "text", "true_label", "pred_label", "pred_confidence",
297:   "probs_json" }`.
298: 
299: ### 7.3 Refine Result
300: 
301: - **report:** Object (rows_processed, relabels_proposed, examples_proposed,
302:   rows_skipped, errors).
303: - **metrics_before**, **metrics_after:** Same shape as metrics above.
304: - **proposed_relabels**, **proposed_examples**, **train_candidate_sample:**
305:   Arrays of objects; keys as table headers.
306: 
307: ### 7.4 Promote Response
308: 
309: ```json
310: {
311:   "promoted": true,
312:   "message": "...",
313:   "acc_before": 0.88,
314:   "acc_after": 0.92,
315:   "promote_accuracy_tolerance": 0.01,
316:   "used_tolerance": false,
317:   "per_label_recall": {
318:     "search": {
319:       "recall_before": 0.88,
320:       "recall_after": 0.89,
321:       "delta": 0.01
322:     }
323:   }
324: }
325: ```
326: 
327: - 400 if train_candidate missing; 200 with promoted: false if candidate
328:   accuracy is below `acc_before - promote_accuracy_tolerance` (unless baseline
329:   accuracy was zero).
330: 
331: ## 8. Docker Compose and Volumes
332: <!-- Tasks: B1 B7 -->
333: 
334: ### 8.1 Redis Service
335: 
336: - **Service name:** e.g. `redis`. Image: `redis:7-alpine`. No public ports
337:   required; internal network only. Training-api connects via
338:   `redis:6379` or REDIS_URL.
339: 
340: ### 8.2 Training-API Service
341: 
342: - **Depends on:** redis.
343: - **Mounts:**
344:   - model_artifacts volume (read/write for reading artifacts)
345:   - Docker socket (to run docker compose for trainer/refiner)
346:   - Compose project directory (to run compose from correct context)
347:   - Host `services/trainer/train.csv` at `/promote_target/train.csv`
348:     (read-write for promotion)
349: - **Env:** configuration is provided via env vars such as `REDIS_URL`,
350:   `MODEL_ARTIFACTS_PATH`, `PROMOTE_TARGET_PATH`, `OLLAMA_URLS`, `OLLAMA_MODEL`,
351:   and `REFINER_*`. These are populated from `config/PROJECT_CONFIG.yaml` by
352:   generating env files (for example `env/.env.<env>`) with
353:   `scripts/generate_env.py` and referencing them via Docker Compose `env_file`.
354:   Per-environment overrides should be done in `PROJECT_CONFIG.yaml`, not by
355:   editing Compose directly.
356: - **Profile:** e.g. `ops` or `refine` so one deployment deploys all; gateway may
357:   show "Training API not available" when profile inactive.
358: - **CMD:** Default HTTP server; override for CLI: `docker compose run
359:   training-api promote` (or train, refine).
360: 
361: ### 8.3 Single Stack
362: 
363: - One Compose stack deploys gateway, Redis, training-api, ai_router, backends,
364:   etc. No separate Redis installation. For Refine, Ollama must be up (e.g.
365:   `docker compose --profile refine up -d ollama` first).
366: 
367: ## 9. Security and Robustness
368: <!-- Tasks: B12 -->
369: 
370: ### 9.1 Network
371: 
372: - Training-api and Redis on internal network only; do not expose Docker socket
373:   or Redis publicly. Gateway proxies to training-api on internal network only.
374: 
375: ### 9.2 Job Execution
376: 
377: - Process timeout: 1h for train jobs; 10 min for refine jobs
378:   (`RUN_REFINE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=600`).
379: - Redis TTL on job keys is 24h (`JOB_STATE_TTL_SECONDS=86400`) to limit storage.
380: 
381: ### 9.3 Gateway
382: 
383: - Normal proxy timeouts (`REQUEST_TIMEOUT=30`) except POST /api/refine/promote
384:   (`PROMOTE_TIMEOUT=300`, 5 min).
385: - Consider response size limits for status payload when result is large.
386: 
387: ### 9.4 Validation
388: 
389: - After adding new Python deps (e.g. redis) and Dockerfile, run Snyk or project
390:   security check per project rules. Document Redis and training-api in stack;
391:   document Promote button and CLI trigger.
392: 
393: ## 10. File and Implementation Summary
394: <!-- Tasks: B12 (all batches) -->
395: 
396: | Area | Files to Add/Modify |
397: | --- | --- |
398: | New service | `services/training-api/` (main app, Dockerfile, requirements.txt; canonical Python train/refine/promote; HTTP server + CLI entrypoint; Redis client, background job runner) |
399: | Scripts | `scripts/promote.sh` (and any train/refine scripts) updated to `docker compose run training-api promote` etc. |
400: | Compose | `compose/docker-compose.yaml` (Redis service, training-api service, volumes, profiles) |
401: | Gateway | `services/gateway/app/main.py` (TRAINING_API_URL, proxy routes for train, refine, events, status, refine/promote; streaming proxy for SSE; longer timeout for promote) |
402: | Frontend | `services/gateway/app/static/index.html` (nav, Train/Refine sections, progress bar, tables, Promote button), `app.js` (hash routing, EventSource, POST /api/refine/promote, result display), `styles.css` (tables, progress bar) |
403: 
404: ## References
405: 
406: - [TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PLAN.md](../planning/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PLAN.md)
407: - [TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PRD.md](../requirements/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PRD.md)
408: - PROJECT_PLAN.md Section 2.1 (trainer, refiner), Section 9.3
409: - Refiner: REFINER_PLAN.md, REFINER_TECHNICAL.md, REFINER_FLOW.md in
410:   docs/auxiliary/refiner/
````

## File: docs/auxiliary/demo/DEMO.md
````markdown
  1: # Demo Guide
  2: 
  3: This guide covers native and containerized Ollama workflows, configuration
  4: management, and the demo scripts for train/relabel/augment/promote.
  5: 
  6: ## Table Of Contents
  7: 
  8: - [Runtime Modes](#runtime-modes)
  9: - [Native Ollama Setup (macOS)](#native-ollama-setup-macos)
 10: - [Native Ollama Setup (Ubuntu)](#native-ollama-setup-ubuntu)
 11: - [Configuration Reference](#configuration-reference)
 12: - [Running The Demo](#running-the-demo)
 13: - [Demo Prompt Examples](#demo-prompt-examples)
 14: - [Using The Frontend](#using-the-frontend)
 15: - [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
 16: - [See Also](#see-also)
 17: 
 18: ## Runtime Modes
 19: 
 20: Exactly one Ollama backend mode must be active:
 21: 
 22: - `OLLAMA_MODE=native`
 23:   - Use host-native Ollama.
 24:   - Containerized Ollama must not run.
 25: - `OLLAMA_MODE=container`
 26:   - Use Compose-managed `ollama` service.
 27:   - Host-native endpoint must not be used by the services.
 28: 
 29: `scripts/demo.sh` enforces this when
 30: `OLLAMA_BACKEND_ENFORCE_EXCLUSIVE=true`.
 31: 
 32: ## Native Ollama Setup (macOS)
 33: 
 34: ### Prerequisites
 35: 
 36: - macOS on Apple Silicon (recommended for this project).
 37: - Docker Desktop and Docker Compose.
 38: 
 39: ### Install
 40: 
 41: 1. Install Ollama from [Ollama Download](https://ollama.com/download).
 42: 2. Start Ollama once from Applications.
 43: 3. Verify:
 44: 
 45: ```bash
 46: ollama --version
 47: ollama list
 48: ```
 49: 
 50: ### Pull Model
 51: 
 52: ```bash
 53: ollama pull phi3:mini
 54: ```
 55: 
 56: ### Smoke Test
 57: 
 58: ```bash
 59: curl -s http://localhost:11434/api/tags | python3 -m json.tool
 60: ```
 61: 
 62: ### Use Native Mode In This Project
 63: 
 64: Set in `config/PROJECT_CONFIG.yaml` (or `env/.env.<env>`):
 65: 
 66: - `OLLAMA_MODE=native`
 67: - `OLLAMA_HOST=http://host.docker.internal:11434`
 68: - `OLLAMA_BACKEND_ENFORCE_EXCLUSIVE=true`
 69: 
 70: Then run:
 71: 
 72: ```bash
 73: CONFIG_ENV=dev ./scripts/demo.sh run
 74: ```
 75: 
 76: ## Native Ollama Setup (Ubuntu)
 77: 
 78: ### Prerequisites
 79: 
 80: - Ubuntu 22.04+.
 81: - Docker and Docker Compose plugin.
 82: 
 83: ### Install
 84: 
 85: ```bash
 86: curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh
 87: ```
 88: 
 89: Start service and enable at boot:
 90: 
 91: ```bash
 92: sudo systemctl enable ollama
 93: sudo systemctl start ollama
 94: ```
 95: 
 96: Verify:
 97: 
 98: ```bash
 99: ollama --version
100: curl -s http://localhost:11434/api/tags | python3 -m json.tool
101: ```
102: 
103: ### Pull Model
104: 
105: ```bash
106: ollama pull phi3:mini
107: ```
108: 
109: ### Use Native Mode In This Project
110: 
111: Set:
112: 
113: - `OLLAMA_MODE=native`
114: - `OLLAMA_HOST=http://host.docker.internal:11434` (Docker Desktop or host bridge)
115: 
116: If your host networking differs, set `OLLAMA_HOST` to the reachable host address.
117: 
118: ## Configuration Reference
119: 
120: All values are defined in `config/PROJECT_CONFIG.yaml` and rendered to
121: `env/.env.<env>` by `scripts/generate_env.py`.
122: 
123: > [!NOTE]
124: > Use `CONFIG_ENV=dev` (or `prod`) with `scripts/demo.sh` to select env.
125: 
126: | Variable | Default | Meaning |
127: | --- | --- | --- |
128: | `OLLAMA_MODE` | `native` | Backend mode (`native` or `container`) |
129: | `OLLAMA_BACKEND_ENFORCE_EXCLUSIVE` | `true` | Fail if both backends are active |
130: | `OLLAMA_CONTAINER_SERVICE` | `ollama` | Compose service name for container backend |
131: | `OLLAMA_HOST` | `http://host.docker.internal:11434` | Active endpoint used by services |
132: | `OLLAMA_MODEL` | `phi3:mini` | Model tag used by refiner/training-api |
133: | `OLLAMA_URLS` | one URL | Comma list for pool (single URL recommended) |
134: | `OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` | `300` | Request timeout to Ollama |
135: | `OLLAMA_MAX_INFLIGHT_PER_INSTANCE` | `1` | Inflight cap per backend instance |
136: | `OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS` | `1` | Max loaded models for container Ollama |
137: | `OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL` | `1` | Parallel requests per loaded model |
138: | `OLLAMA_MAX_QUEUE` | `512` | Queue limit before overload |
139: | `OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE` | `30m` | Keep model in memory between requests |
140: | `OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION` | `0` | Optional memory/throughput experiment |
141: | `OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_TYPE` | `f16` | KV cache precision (`q8_0`, `q4_0`, etc.) |
142: | `REFINER_RELABEL_NUM_CTX` | `1024` | Relabel context budget |
143: | `REFINER_AUGMENT_NUM_CTX` | `768` | Augment context budget |
144: | `REFINER_RELABEL_NUM_PREDICT` | `120` | Relabel output token cap |
145: | `REFINER_AUGMENT_NUM_PREDICT` | `180` | Augment output token cap |
146: | `REFINER_TEMPERATURE` | `0.1` | Generation determinism |
147: | `REFINER_SEED` | `42` | Reproducibility seed |
148: | `REFINER_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_ENABLED` | `true` | Force structured JSON output |
149: | `REFINER_RELABEL_BATCH_SIZE` | `25` | Relabel batch size |
150: | `REFINER_RELABEL_MAX_PARALLEL_BATCHES` | `1` | Relabel worker parallelism |
151: | `REFINER_AUGMENT_N_PER_LABEL` | `3` | Aug examples per selected label |
152: | `REFINER_AUGMENT_MAX_PARALLEL_LABELS` | `1` | Augment label parallelism |
153: | `REFINER_RELABEL_MAX_RETRIES` | `3` | Max retries per relabel batch |
154: | `REFINER_AUGMENT_MAX_RETRIES` | `3` | Max retries per augment label |
155: | `REFINER_LIMIT` | `5` | Max misclassified rows for relabel phase |
156: | `DEMO_START_BACKEND` | `true` | Let `demo.sh` start selected backend |
157: | `DEMO_RUN_RELABEL` | `true` | Enable relabel phase in `demo.sh refine` |
158: | `DEMO_RUN_AUGMENT` | `true` | Enable augment phase in `demo.sh refine` |
159: | `BENCH_PROFILE` | `mac_performance` | Benchmark profile label |
160: | `BENCH_EXPERIMENT_ID` | `baseline` | Benchmark experiment id |
161: | `BENCH_METRICS_INTERVAL_SEC` | `5` | Metrics sampling cadence |
162: | `BENCH_MAX_RETRIES` | `3` | Retry cap during benchmark runs |
163: 
164: ### Cross References
165: 
166: - Configuration architecture:
167:   [CONFIGURATION.md](../architecture/CONFIGURATION.md)
168: - Refiner flow:
169:   [REFINER_FLOW.md](../refiner/REFINER_FLOW.md)
170: - Performance plan:
171:   [PERFORMANCE_IMPROVEMENTS.md](../planning/PERFORMANCE_IMPROVEMENTS.md)
172: 
173: ## Running The Demo
174: 
175: ### Build
176: 
177: ```bash
178: ./scripts/demo.sh build
179: ```
180: 
181: ### Start Stack
182: 
183: ```bash
184: ./scripts/demo.sh run
185: ```
186: 
187: ### Start With Dev Overlay
188: 
189: ```bash
190: ./scripts/demo.sh run --dev
191: ```
192: 
193: ### Train
194: 
195: ```bash
196: ./scripts/demo.sh train
197: ```
198: 
199: ### Relabel Only
200: 
201: ```bash
202: ./scripts/demo.sh relabel --limit 5
203: ```
204: 
205: ### Augment Only
206: 
207: ```bash
208: ./scripts/demo.sh augment --run-id <run-id-from-relabel>
209: ```
210: 
211: ### Full Split Refine (Relabel + Augment)
212: 
213: ```bash
214: ./scripts/demo.sh refine --limit 5
215: ```
216: 
217: ### Promote
218: 
219: ```bash
220: ./scripts/demo.sh promote
221: ```
222: 
223: ### Stop / Restart / Delete / Reset
224: 
225: ```bash
226: ./scripts/demo.sh stop
227: ./scripts/demo.sh restart
228: ./scripts/demo.sh restart --dev
229: ./scripts/demo.sh delete
230: ./scripts/demo.sh reset
231: ```
232: 
233: - `stop` - stop the stack (containers remain).
234: - `restart` - stop + start. Accepts the same flags as `run`
235:   (`--dev`, `--scale N`).
236: - `delete` - remove containers, networks, and volumes.
237: - `reset` - stop + delete + start (full teardown and rebuild).
238:   Accepts the same flags as `run`.
239: 
240: ## Demo Prompt Examples
241: 
242: Paste these `text` values into the Query tab to demo routing across labels.
243: 
244: ### search
245: 
246: 1. Compare nginx ingress vs traefik
247: 2. How does kubernetes scheduling work
248: 3. Best practices for docker multi-stage builds
249: 4. Terraform vs pulumi comparison
250: 5. Search for microservice patterns for observability
251: 6. What is an SLO and how do you measure it
252: 7. How to troubleshoot a 502 Bad Gateway in nginx
253: 8. Latest guidance on docker compose profiles
254: 9. Explain k8s liveness vs readiness probes
255: 10. Find resources for learning vector databases
256: 
257: ### image
258: 
259: 1. Generate a logo for a coffee roaster, minimalist style
260: 2. Create an illustration of a cat wearing a space helmet
261: 3. Detect objects in this image and return labels
262: 4. Turn this photo into a watercolor painting
263: 5. Generate a realistic product mockup of a smartwatch on a dark background
264: 6. Create an icon set for a mobile app: settings, search, notifications
265: 7. Generate a poster design for "Summer Sale" with bold typography
266: 8. Analyze this screenshot and describe what UI elements are present
267: 9. Create a simple illustration diagram of a home network
268: 10. Generate a thumbnail image for a YouTube video about kubernetes
269: 
270: ### ops
271: 
272: 1. Kubectl get pods in namespace default
273: 2. Restart the deployment `my-service`
274: 3. Pod stuck in CrashLoopBackOff debug steps
275: 4. Check service health for gateway and ai-router
276: 5. Docker compose logs for `search_service` with tail 200
277: 6. Terraform plan looks risky, how do I review safely
278: 7. Rate limit requests on nginx ingress
279: 8. How to resize an EBS volume and extend the filesystem
280: 9. Fix permissions for a mounted volume in docker compose
281: 10. Troubleshoot why a container cannot reach host.docker.internal
282: 
283: ### unknown
284: 
285: 1. Help
286: 2. Hi
287: 3. Do something
288: 4. Analyze
289: 5. I need assistance
290: 6. Something is broken
291: 7. It depends
292: 8. Explain that
293: 9. Test request
294: 10. Can you do the thing again
295: 
296: ## Using The Frontend
297: 
298: The gateway UI is available at <http://localhost:8000>.
299: 
300: - **Query** tab routes requests.
301: - **Train** tab triggers training and displays metrics.
302: - **Refine** tab runs relabel and augment phases and allows promotion.
303: 
304: Train/Refine tabs require `training-api` in the running stack.
305: 
306: ## Troubleshooting
307: 
308: ### Both Native And Container Backends Running
309: 
310: Set `OLLAMA_BACKEND_ENFORCE_EXCLUSIVE=true` and run with `demo.sh`.
311: The script will fail fast or stop conflicting backend processes.
312: 
313: ### Native Ollama Unreachable
314: 
315: - Verify local service:
316:   `curl -s http://localhost:11434/api/tags`
317: - Verify project endpoint:
318:   `echo $OLLAMA_HOST`
319: 
320: ### Container Ollama Unreachable
321: 
322: - Confirm mode:
323:   `OLLAMA_MODE=container`
324: - Confirm host:
325:   `OLLAMA_HOST=http://ollama:11434`
326: - Check service:
327:   `docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml --profile refine-container ps`
328: 
329: ### Missing Model
330: 
331: Pull the selected model in active backend:
332: 
333: ```bash
334: ollama pull phi3:mini
335: ```
336: 
337: ### Performance Regressions
338: 
339: - Check `OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL`, `OLLAMA_NUM_CTX`, and `OLLAMA_NUM_PREDICT`.
340: - Reduce relabel/augment parallelism first.
341: - Re-run the benchmark protocol in
342:   [PERFORMANCE_IMPROVEMENTS.md](../planning/PERFORMANCE_IMPROVEMENTS.md).
343: 
344: ## See Also
345: 
346: - [AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md](../planning/AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md)
347: - [PERFORMANCE_IMPROVEMENTS.md](../planning/PERFORMANCE_IMPROVEMENTS.md)
348: - [CONFIGURATION.md](../architecture/CONFIGURATION.md)
349: - [REFINER_FLOW.md](../refiner/REFINER_FLOW.md)
350: - [TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH.md](../architecture/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH.md)
````

## File: docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_FLOW.md
````markdown
  1: # REFINER FLOW
  2: 
  3: End-to-end flow for the refinement pipeline, based on REFINER_PLAN,
  4: REFINER_TECHNICAL, REFINER_PRD, and METRICS_JSON specifications.
  5: 
  6: ## 1. Artifacts and Their Roles
  7: 
  8: | Artifact | Purpose |
  9: | -------- | ------- |
 10: | `train.csv` | Canonical dataset used for retraining; only updated when metrics improve |
 11: | `misclassified.csv` | Identify specific mistakes to analyze (local debugging) |
 12: | `metrics.json` | Measure global model quality; evaluation signal (global evaluation) |
 13: 
 14: `metrics.json` answers: **"Did the dataset changes improve the classifier?"**
 15: `misclassified.csv` answers: **"What mistakes did the model make?"**
 16: 
 17: ## 2. High-Level Flow (Updated: Two-Phase Refine)
 18: 
 19: ```ascii
 20:                     train.csv (host)
 21:                            |
 22:                            v
 23:                     +-------------+
 24:                     |   trainer   |
 25:                     +-------------+
 26:                            |
 27:          +-----------------+-----------------+
 28:          |                 |                 |
 29:          v                 v                 v
 30:    model.joblib      metrics.json    misclassified.csv
 31:          |                 |                 |
 32:          |                 +--------+--------+
 33:          |                          |
 34:          v                          v
 35:    ai_router (runtime)        training-api (offline jobs)
 36:                                   |
 37:                                   +--> relabel phase (Redis Streams tasks)
 38:                                   |       |
 39:                                   |       v
 40:                                   |   ollama pool (multi-instance)
 41:                                   |       |
 42:                                   |       v
 43:                                   |   refine_runs/<run_id>/relabel/
 44:                                   |     proposed_relabels.csv
 45:                                   |     train_relabel_candidate.csv
 46:                                   |     metrics_relabel_candidate.json
 47:                                   |
 48:                                   +--> augment phase (Redis Streams tasks)
 49:                                           |
 50:                                           v
 51:                                       ollama pool (multi-instance)
 52:                                           |
 53:                                           v
 54:                                       refine_runs/<run_id>/augment/
 55:                                         proposed_examples.csv
 56:                                         train_augment_candidate.csv
 57:                                         metrics_augment_candidate.json
 58:                                   |
 59:                                   v
 60:                              promote (optional)
 61:                                   |
 62:                     +-------------+-------------+
 63:                     |                           |
 64:         accuracy improved or within      below threshold
 65:         tolerance (see below)              (discard)
 66:                     |                           |
 67:                     v                           v
 68:              train.csv updated            discard candidate
 69:              model.joblib promoted        train.csv unchanged
 70:                     |
 71:                     v
 72:             restart ai_router
 73: ```
 74: 
 75: ## 3. Detailed Step-by-Step Flow
 76: 
 77: ### Phase 1: Train
 78: 
 79: 1. **Input**: `train.csv` (mounted from host at `services/trainer/train.csv`)
 80: 2. **Action**: `docker compose --profile train run --rm trainer`
 81: 3. **Output** (to `model_artifacts` volume):
 82:    - `model.joblib` - classifier for ai_router
 83:    - `metrics.json` - accuracy, classification_report, confusion_matrix
 84:    - `misclassified.csv` - rows where pred_label != true_label
 85: 
 86: ### Phase 2: Refine (Relabel + Augment)
 87: 
 88: 1. **Input**:
 89:    - `train.csv` (read-only)
 90:    - `misclassified.csv` (from volume)
 91:    - `metrics.json` (from volume)
 92: 
 93: 2. **Action**: Use the UI or call the training-api endpoints:
 94: 
 95:    - `POST /refine/relabel` (plus SSE at `GET /refine/relabel/events/{job_id}`)
 96:    - `POST /refine/augment` (plus SSE at `GET /refine/augment/events/{job_id}`)
 97: 
 98: 3. **Refine sub-steps**:
 99:    - Ingest inputs (`train.csv`, `misclassified.csv`, `metrics.json`)
100:    - Use Redis Streams to enqueue work units:
101:      - Relabel: tasks are batches of misclassified rows
102:      - Augment: tasks are one label per task
103:    - Process tasks in parallel with an Ollama pool (multi-instance)
104:    - Write conflict-free per-task outputs under `refine_runs/<run_id>/...`
105:    - Merge deterministically into a candidate dataset CSV per phase
106:    - Retrain on the candidate and write phase-specific metrics JSON for a
107:      before/after comparison
108: 
109: 4. **Output** (to `model_artifacts` volume):
110:    - `refine_runs/<run_id>/metrics_before.json`
111:    - Relabel:
112:      - `refine_runs/<run_id>/relabel/proposed_relabels.csv`
113:      - `refine_runs/<run_id>/relabel/train_relabel_candidate.csv`
114:      - `refine_runs/<run_id>/relabel/metrics_relabel_candidate.json`
115:    - Augment:
116:      - `refine_runs/<run_id>/augment/proposed_examples.csv`
117:      - `refine_runs/<run_id>/augment/train_augment_candidate.csv`
118:      - `refine_runs/<run_id>/augment/metrics_augment_candidate.json`
119:      - Per-label outputs under `refine_runs/<run_id>/augment/labels/` (raw LLM
120:        output, prompts, validation JSON, optional rejected rows)
121: 
122: ### Phase 3: Promote
123: 
124: 1. **Input**: A run_id candidate dataset (augment preferred, else relabel) plus
125:    its `metrics_before.json`.
126: 
127: 2. **Action**: Use the UI Promote button (sends the most recent `run_id`) or
128:    call `POST /refine/promote` with body `{ "run_id": "..." }`.
129: 
130: 3. **Promote sub-steps**:
131:    - Retrain with train_candidate.csv (produces model_candidate.joblib,
132:      metrics_candidate.json)
133:    - Compare accuracy: metrics_candidate vs metrics_before using
134:      `REFINER_PROMOTE_ACCURACY_TOLERANCE` (promote when
135:      `acc_after >= acc_before - tolerance`, or when `acc_before` is zero)
136:    - **If promoted**: copy train_candidate.csv to train.csv (host), copy
137:      model_candidate.joblib to model.joblib, update metrics.json. The JSON
138:      response includes per-label recall deltas for debugging.
139:    - **If not promoted**: discard candidate; train.csv and model.joblib
140:      unchanged
141: 
142: 4. **Post-promote**: Restart ai_router to load new model (if promoted)
143: 
144: ## 4. Commands Summary
145: 
146: ```bash
147: # 1. Train
148: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml --profile train run --rm trainer
149: 
150: # 2. Refine (via training-api endpoints; uses multi Ollama instances)
151: # Use the UI at /refine or call:
152: # - POST /refine/relabel
153: # - POST /refine/augment
154: 
155: # 3. Promote when accuracy improves or stays within tolerance
156: ./scripts/promote.sh
157: 
158: # 4. If promoted, restart ai_router
159: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml restart ai_router
160: ```
161: 
162: Before running refine-related commands in a new environment, generate the env
163: file from the centralized configuration:
164: 
165: ```bash
166: python scripts/generate_env.py
167: ```
168: 
169: This reads `config/PROJECT_CONFIG.yaml`, selects the environment from
170: `default.ENV` (fallback `dev`) unless overridden, and writes `env/.env.<env>`.
171: Docker Compose then consumes that file via `env_file` so training-api and the
172: refine pipeline receive consistent settings
173: (`REDIS_URL`, `MODEL_ARTIFACTS_PATH`, `PROMOTE_TARGET_PATH`, `OLLAMA_URLS`,
174: `OLLAMA_MODEL`, and `REFINER_*` values).
175: 
176: Or via demo.sh:
177: 
178: ```bash
179: ./scripts/demo.sh train
180: ./scripts/demo.sh refine
181: ./scripts/demo.sh promote
182: ```
183: 
184: ## 5. Decision Points
185: 
186: | Decision | Condition | Action |
187: | -------- | --------- | ------ |
188: | Labels to augment | Weak labels from metrics (recall < 0.75 or all labels if metrics missing) | Per-label N: base `REFINER_AUGMENT_N_PER_LABEL`, scaled up for rarer classes (capped at 3x base) |
189: | Promote candidate | `acc_after >= acc_before - REFINER_PROMOTE_ACCURACY_TOLERANCE` (or `acc_before == 0`) | Copy to train.csv, promote model |
190: | Discard candidate | candidate accuracy below threshold | Keep train.csv and model.joblib |
191: 
192: Augmentation uses seed examples from `train.csv`, optional verification
193: (`REFINER_AUGMENT_VERIFY_LABELS`), length limits, and fuzzy dedupe at merge.
194: See [AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md](../planning/AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md).
195: 
196: ## 6. Final train.csv Contents (When Promoted)
197: 
198: 1. **Post-refined original content**: Relabels applied to misclassified rows
199: 2. **Additional synthetic examples**: Generated per weak label with quality
200:    gates (seeded prompts, verification, dedupe). The legacy standalone refiner
201:    used a 150-row skip threshold; **training-api** augment does not apply that
202:    rule and instead uses class-weighted counts and filters above.
203: 
204: ## 7. Cross-References
205: 
206: - [REFINER_PLAN.md](REFINER_PLAN.md) - Conceptual overview
207: - [REFINER_TECHNICAL.md](REFINER_TECHNICAL.md) - Technical specification
208: - [REFINER_PRD.md](REFINER_PRD.md) - Requirements
209: - [AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md](../planning/AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md)
210: Training-api augment and promote tuning
211: - [METRICS_JSON.md](docs/auxiliary/reference/METRICS_JSON.md) - metrics.json
212: purpose and usage
````

## File: docs/auxiliary/requirements/PRD.md
````markdown
  1: # Product Requirements Document: Local AI Microservice Mesh
  2: 
  3: Structured requirements for the locally runnable, multi-container microservice
  4: system with AI-driven request routing and application-level tracing.
  5: 
  6: ## 1. Document Control
  7: 
  8: | Attribute | Value |
  9: | --- | --- |
 10: | Document type | Product Requirements Document (PRD) |
 11: | Format | Requirements specification |
 12: | Source | PROJECT_PLAN.md |
 13: | Scope | Full project |
 14: 
 15: ## 2. Business and Project Requirements
 16: 
 17: ### 2.1 Goals
 18: 
 19: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 20: | --- | --- | --- |
 21: | BR-001 | The system SHALL provide a locally runnable multi-container microservice mesh. | Must |
 22: | BR-002 | An AI classifier SHALL select the backend service for each incoming request. | Must |
 23: | BR-003 | The system SHALL demonstrate Docker Compose advanced features and AI-driven routing. | Must |
 24: | BR-004 | The browser UI SHALL display the exact path taken (application-level tracing) per action. | Must |
 25: 
 26: ### 2.2 Demo and Observability
 27: 
 28: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 29: | --- | --- | --- |
 30: | BR-005 | The project SHALL be demonstrable via a browser UI as the primary interface. | Must |
 31: | BR-006 | The project SHALL be demonstrable via terminal commands (e.g., curl-based API testing) with trace visibility. | Must |
 32: | BR-007 | The project SHALL support scaling scenarios that demonstrate load distribution across scaled services. | Must |
 33: | BR-008 | The project SHALL support failure scenarios that demonstrate fallback or error behavior when backends fail. | Must |
 34: 
 35: ## 3. Platform and Technical Constraints
 36: 
 37: ### 3.1 Platform
 38: 
 39: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 40: | --- | --- | --- |
 41: | NFR-001 | The system SHALL run cross-platform on Linux, Mac, and Windows. | Must |
 42: | NFR-002 | All services SHALL be implemented in Python using FastAPI. | Must |
 43: | NFR-003 | Tracing SHALL be application-level only; no external observability stack SHALL be required. | Must |
 44: | NFR-004 | Docker SHALL be used with multi-stage Dockerfiles; model training SHALL occur at build time where applicable. | Must |
 45: | NFR-005 | Docker Compose SHALL use advanced features: fragments, anchors, profiles, and health checks. | Must |
 46: 
 47: ### 3.2 AI and Model
 48: 
 49: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 50: | --- | --- | --- |
 51: | NFR-006 | The AI classifier SHALL use scikit-learn (TF-IDF + Logistic Regression) as specified in the project plan. | Must |
 52: | NFR-007 | The classifier SHALL run on CPU; no GPU SHALL be required. | Must |
 53: | NFR-008 | The model SHALL be deterministic and reproducible (fixed random_state, build-time training). | Must |
 54: 
 55: ## 4. System Architecture Requirements
 56: 
 57: ### 4.1 Services
 58: 
 59: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 60: | --- | --- | --- |
 61: | FR-001 | A **gateway** service (FastAPI) SHALL serve the static UI at `/`. | Must |
 62: | FR-002 | The gateway SHALL expose the main API entrypoint `POST /api/request`. | Must |
 63: | FR-003 | The gateway SHALL call the ai-router to classify each request. | Must |
 64: | FR-004 | The gateway SHALL proxy the request to the selected backend service. | Must |
 65: | FR-005 | The gateway SHALL aggregate and return the full request trace in the response. | Must |
 66: | FR-006 | The gateway SHALL implement confidence thresholding and fallback logic per routing policy. | Must |
 67: | FR-007 | An **ai-router** service (FastAPI) SHALL expose `POST /classify`. | Must |
 68: | FR-008 | The ai-router SHALL run a lightweight text classifier and return route, confidence score, and explanation. | Must |
 69: | FR-009 | The ai-router SHALL ship a pre-built model artifact (`model.joblib`) in its Docker image. | Must |
 70: | FR-010 | A **search-service** (FastAPI) SHALL expose `POST /handle` and simulate information lookup/research responses. | Must |
 71: | FR-011 | An **image-service** (FastAPI) SHALL expose `POST /handle` and simulate image-related request handling. | Must |
 72: | FR-012 | An **ops-service** (FastAPI) SHALL expose `POST /handle` and simulate DevOps/infrastructure troubleshooting responses. | Must |
 73: | FR-080 | A **trainer** service SHALL be a one-shot container that runs `train.py`, writes `model.joblib` to a shared volume, and exits. | Must |
 74: | FR-081 | The trainer service SHALL use `train.py` (baked into the image) and `train.csv` (mounted from `services/trainer/` at runtime); both SHALL live in `services/trainer/`. | Must |
 75: | FR-082 | The ai-router SHALL support loading model artifacts from a shared volume path specified by `MODEL_PATH` env var, falling back to the build-time artifact. | Must |
 76: | FR-083 | The ai-router MAY expose `POST /reload-model` for hot-reload of the model without container restart. | Should |
 77: | FR-084 | A **refiner** service SHALL improve the training dataset by analyzing misclassified rows via a local LLM (Ollama). | Must |
 78: | FR-085 | The refiner SHALL run on demand via `docker compose --profile refine run --rm refiner`; it SHALL NOT start with default `docker compose up`. | Must |
 79: | FR-086 | The refiner SHALL produce `train_candidate.csv`; promotion to `train.csv` SHALL occur when retraining meets the training-api promotion policy (including `REFINER_PROMOTE_ACCURACY_TOLERANCE`; via `scripts/promote.sh` or UI). | Must |
 80: 
 81: See [REFINER_PRD.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PRD.md) for full refiner
 82: requirements.
 83: 
 84: ### 4.2 Request Flow
 85: 
 86: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
 87: | --- | --- | --- |
 88: | FR-013 | User requests SHALL be submitted via UI or curl to `gateway /api/request`. | Must |
 89: | FR-014 | The gateway SHALL call `ai-router /classify` with the request text. | Must |
 90: | FR-015 | The ai-router SHALL return `{route, confidence, explanation, trace_append}`. | Must |
 91: | FR-016 | The gateway SHALL evaluate a configurable confidence threshold (`T_route`; runtime default value 0.60 from generated env config, code fallback 0.55). | Must |
 92: | FR-017 | If route is `unknown` or confidence is below threshold, the gateway SHALL return a 404-equivalent response without proxying. | Must |
 93: | FR-018 | Otherwise, the gateway SHALL proxy to the selected backend `POST /handle`. | Must |
 94: | FR-019 | The gateway SHALL aggregate all trace entries and return the complete response with routing metadata. | Must |
 95: 
 96: ## 5. Routing Model Requirements
 97: 
 98: ### 5.1 Routes and Labels
 99: 
100: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
101: | --- | --- | --- |
102: | FR-020 | The model SHALL support exactly four labels: `search`, `image`, `ops`, `unknown`. | Must |
103: | FR-021 | `search` SHALL be used for lookup, explanation, comparison queries. | Must |
104: | FR-022 | `image` SHALL be used for image-related intents (detection, processing, etc.). | Must |
105: | FR-023 | `ops` SHALL be used for DevOps, infrastructure, and troubleshooting queries. | Must |
106: | FR-024 | `unknown` SHALL be used for out-of-scope, unclear, or irrelevant requests and SHALL result in a 404-equivalent response. | Must |
107: 
108: ### 5.2 Routing Policy
109: 
110: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
111: | --- | --- | --- |
112: | FR-025 | The model SHALL produce probabilities for all four labels. | Must |
113: | FR-026 | The gateway SHALL use a configurable confidence threshold (`T_route`; runtime default value 0.60 from generated env config, code fallback 0.55) for non-unknown routes. | Must |
114: | FR-027 | The system MAY support an optional margin check: if the difference between the top two routes is below a threshold, treat as unknown. | Should |
115: | FR-028 | If route is `unknown` or confidence is too low, the gateway SHALL return 404 without proxying to any backend. | Must |
116: 
117: ## 6. Tracing Requirements
118: 
119: ### 6.1 Trace Contract
120: 
121: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
122: | --- | --- | --- |
123: | FR-029 | A `request_id` (UUID) SHALL be generated by the gateway if not provided by the client. | Must |
124: | FR-030 | `request_id` SHALL be propagated end-to-end via headers and/or body. | Must |
125: | FR-031 | Each service SHALL append exactly one trace entry per request. | Must |
126: | FR-032 | The gateway SHALL aggregate all trace entries into a single `trace` array. | Must |
127: | FR-033 | The complete trace SHALL be returned to the UI/client in the gateway response. | Must |
128: 
129: ### 6.2 Trace Entry Schema
130: 
131: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
132: | --- | --- | --- |
133: | FR-034 | Each trace entry SHALL include: `service`, `event`, `ts` (ISO 8601). | Must |
134: | FR-035 | Trace entries MAY include an optional `meta` object (e.g., `detail`, `route`, `confidence`, `status`). | Must |
135: 
136: ### 6.3 Gateway Response Schema
137: 
138: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
139: | --- | --- | --- |
140: | FR-036 | Successful gateway response SHALL include: `request_id`, `route`, `confidence`, `explanation`, `trace`, `backend_response`, `timings_ms`. | Must |
141: | FR-037 | Unknown-route (404-equivalent) response SHALL include: `request_id`, `route` (unknown), `confidence`, `message`, `trace` (no backend hop). | Must |
142: | FR-038 | `timings_ms` SHALL include at least: `classify`, `proxy`, `total`. | Must |
143: 
144: ## 7. Repository and Structure Requirements
145: 
146: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
147: | --- | --- | --- |
148: | NFR-009 | The repository SHALL follow the structure defined in the project plan (compose/, services/, scripts/, docs/auxiliary/). | Must |
149: | NFR-010 | Each service SHALL have its own directory under `services/` with Dockerfile, requirements.txt, app/, and tests/ where specified. | Must |
150: | NFR-011 | Compose files SHALL reside in `compose/` (docker-compose.yaml base, docker-compose.dev.yaml for dev profile). | Must |
151: | NFR-012 | Demo and load-test scripts SHALL reside in `scripts/` (demo.sh, load_test.sh). | Must |
152: | NFR-013 | Documentation SHALL include architecture and demo instructions in `docs/auxiliary/`. | Must |
153: 
154: ## 8. Training Data Requirements
155: 
156: ### 8.1 Data Strategy
157: 
158: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
159: | --- | --- | --- |
160: | FR-039 | Training data SHALL be synthetic and hand-written (curated examples). | Must |
161: | FR-040 | Minimum SHALL be 50-100 examples per label; recommended 150-300 per label. | Must |
162: | FR-041 | Data SHALL be balanced across all four labels (search, image, ops, unknown). | Must |
163: | FR-042 | Training data SHALL use `text,label` format (e.g., train.csv). | Must |
164: 
165: ### 8.2 Data Content Guidelines
166: 
167: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
168: | --- | --- | --- |
169: | FR-043 | search-service examples SHALL include patterns such as "what is...", "compare...", "explain...", "find...", "best way to...". | Should |
170: | FR-044 | image-service examples SHALL include image nouns, vision verbs, and file references. | Should |
171: | FR-045 | ops-service examples SHALL include tooling names, error words, and imperative debugging tone. | Should |
172: | FR-046 | unknown examples SHALL include chit-chat, vague questions, nonsense, and generic conversation. | Should |
173: 
174: ## 9. AI Model Implementation Requirements
175: 
176: ### 9.1 Algorithm
177: 
178: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
179: | --- | --- | --- |
180: | FR-047 | Vectorization SHALL use TfidfVectorizer with ngram_range=(1,2) and min_df=2. | Must |
181: | FR-048 | Classification SHALL use LogisticRegression with max_iter=2000, solver="lbfgs", multi_class="multinomial". | Must |
182: | FR-049 | The model SHALL provide probability outputs (predict_proba) for confidence scoring. | Must |
183: | FR-050 | The model SHALL support explainability via top weighted features (e.g., 3-8 tokens/phrases). | Must |
184: 
185: ### 9.2 Training Pipeline
186: 
187: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
188: | --- | --- | --- |
189: | FR-051 | Training code and data SHALL be located in `services/trainer/` (train.py, train.csv). The trained artifact (`model.joblib`) SHALL be placed in `services/ai_router/model/` for inclusion in the ai-router image. | Must |
190: | FR-052 | Training SHALL use a fixed random_state for reproducibility. | Must |
191: | FR-053 | The training script SHALL fit TfidfVectorizer and LogisticRegression, evaluate accuracy/confusion matrix, and save vectorizer, model, and label mapping in the artifact. | Must |
192: | FR-054 | The model artifact (`model.joblib`) SHALL be pre-built by the trainer service and included in the ai-router Docker image at build time. | Must |
193: 
194: ### 9.3 Inference
195: 
196: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
197: | --- | --- | --- |
198: | FR-055 | The ai-router SHALL load model.joblib at service startup. | Must |
199: | FR-056 | Inference SHALL vectorize input text, compute probabilities, select route via argmax, and set confidence to max(probs). | Must |
200: | FR-057 | The ai-router SHALL return explanation (top features) along with route, confidence, and trace_append. | Must |
201: 
202: ## 10. API Endpoint Requirements
203: 
204: ### 10.1 Gateway
205: 
206: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
207: | --- | --- | --- |
208: | FR-058 | Gateway SHALL expose `GET /` to serve static UI files. | Must |
209: | FR-059 | Gateway SHALL expose `POST /api/request` accepting body: `request_id` (optional), `text`, `trace` (optional). | Must |
210: | FR-060 | Gateway SHALL expose `GET /health` returning 200 when healthy. | Must |
211: | FR-061 | Gateway SHALL expose `GET /routes` returning list of available routes and backend URLs. | Must |
212: 
213: ### 10.2 AI Router
214: 
215: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
216: | --- | --- | --- |
217: | FR-062 | AI router SHALL expose `POST /classify` accepting `request_id`, `text`. | Must |
218: | FR-063 | AI router SHALL expose `GET /health` returning 200 when healthy. | Must |
219: | FR-064 | Classify response SHALL include `route`, `confidence`, `explanation`, `trace_append`. | Must |
220: 
221: ### 10.3 Backend Services
222: 
223: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
224: | --- | --- | --- |
225: | FR-065 | Each backend (search, image, ops) SHALL expose `POST /handle` accepting `request_id`, `text`. | Must |
226: | FR-066 | Handle response SHALL include `payload` and `trace_append`. | Must |
227: | FR-067 | Each backend SHALL expose `GET /health` returning 200 when healthy. | Must |
228: 
229: ## 11. Web UI Requirements
230: 
231: ### 11.1 Components
232: 
233: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
234: | --- | --- | --- |
235: | FR-068 | The UI SHALL provide a text input field for user queries and a submit button. | Must |
236: | FR-069 | The UI SHALL display selected route name, confidence score (0.0-1.0), and explanation (top tokens/phrases). | Must |
237: | FR-070 | The UI SHALL display a trace visualization: timeline list of each hop. | Must |
238: | FR-071 | The UI SHALL display a hop diagram (e.g., web -> gateway -> ai-router -> gateway -> backend -> gateway -> web). | Must |
239: | FR-072 | The UI SHALL display the backend response (formatted JSON payload) when route is not unknown. | Must |
240: | FR-073 | The UI SHALL display timings: classification time, proxy time, total time. | Must |
241: 
242: ### 11.2 Behavior
243: 
244: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
245: | --- | --- | --- |
246: | FR-074 | The UI SHALL generate request_id when the user clicks Send. | Must |
247: | FR-075 | The UI SHALL POST to gateway `/api/request` with `request_id`, `text`, and initial trace entry (e.g., service: "web", event: "submit"). | Must |
248: | FR-076 | The UI SHALL render the complete response including full trace. | Must |
249: | FR-077 | The UI SHALL handle unknown-route (404-equivalent) responses with appropriate messaging. | Must |
250: 
251: ### 11.3 Serving
252: 
253: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
254: | --- | --- | --- |
255: | FR-078 | The UI SHALL be served from the gateway as static files; no separate web container SHALL be required. | Must |
256: | FR-079 | Gateway SHALL expose `/` for UI and `/api/request` for API. | Must |
257: 
258: ## 12. Docker Requirements
259: 
260: ### 12.1 Multi-Stage Dockerfiles
261: 
262: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
263: | --- | --- | --- |
264: | NFR-014 | All Python services (gateway, backends) SHALL use a multi-stage Dockerfile: builder (wheels) and runtime. | Must |
265: | NFR-015 | Base image SHALL be Python 3.12-slim (e.g., bookworm). | Must |
266: | NFR-016 | The runtime stage SHALL create a non-root user (e.g., appuser/appgroup) and run the application as that user. | Must |
267: | NFR-017 | The runtime stage SHALL set PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 and PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1. | Must |
268: | NFR-018 | Production CMD SHALL use gunicorn with uvicorn workers, bind 0.0.0.0:8000, with access and error log to stdout. | Must |
269: | NFR-019 | Each service Dockerfile SHALL include a HEALTHCHECK that hits the service /health endpoint. | Must |
270: 
271: ### 12.2 AI Router Dockerfile
272: 
273: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
274: | --- | --- | --- |
275: | NFR-020 | The ai-router Dockerfile SHALL copy a pre-built `model.joblib` artifact into the image; training is handled by the separate trainer service. | Must |
276: | NFR-021 | The runtime stage SHALL copy `model.joblib` from the build context (`services/ai_router/model/`). | Must |
277: | NFR-022 | Dependencies SHALL include gunicorn and uvicorn[standard] for production. | Must |
278: 
279: ### 12.3 Trainer Dockerfile
280: 
281: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
282: | --- | --- | --- |
283: | NFR-041 | The trainer SHALL have its own directory `services/trainer/` with Dockerfile and requirements.txt. | Must |
284: | NFR-042 | The trainer Dockerfile SHALL be single-stage (no runtime server); only training dependencies (scikit-learn, numpy, joblib). | Must |
