285: | NFR-043 | The trainer SHALL include `train.py` in its Docker image at build time; `train.csv` SHALL be mounted from the host at runtime. | Must |
286: 
287: ### 12.4 Dev Target
288: 
289: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
290: | --- | --- | --- |
291: | NFR-023 | An optional dev target MAY override CMD to use uvicorn with --reload. | Should |
292: 
293: ## 13. Docker Compose Requirements
294: 
295: ### 13.1 File Structure
296: 
297: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
298: | --- | --- | --- |
299: | NFR-024 | Base configuration SHALL be in `compose/docker-compose.yaml`. | Must |
300: | NFR-025 | Dev overrides SHALL be in `compose/docker-compose.dev.yaml` with profile usage. | Must |
301: 
302: ### 13.2 Advanced Features
303: 
304: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
305: | --- | --- | --- |
306: | NFR-026 | Compose SHALL use anchors (e.g., x-common-env, x-common-healthcheck, x-common-logging) for shared configuration. | Must |
307: | NFR-027 | Compose SHALL define profiles: core (default) and dev. | Must |
308: | NFR-028 | Each service SHALL have a health check defined (e.g., `curl -f http://localhost:8000/health`). | Must |
309: | NFR-029 | Gateway SHALL have depends_on with condition: service_healthy for ai-router and all backends. | Must |
310: | NFR-030 | Services SHALL support horizontal scaling (e.g., docker compose up --scale search-service=3). | Must |
311: | NFR-031 | Gateway SHALL distribute requests across scaled backend replicas (e.g., round-robin at DNS or load balancer). | Must |
312: 
313: ### 13.3 Refiner Profile
314: 
315: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
316: | --- | --- | --- |
317: | NFR-048 | Compose SHALL define a `refine` profile containing the refiner and ollama services; they SHALL NOT start with default `docker compose up`. | Must |
318: | NFR-049 | The refiner SHALL depend on Ollama with `condition: service_healthy` and mount the shared `model_artifacts` volume. | Must |
319: 
320: See [REFINER_PRD.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PRD.md) and
321: [REFINER_TECHNICAL.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_TECHNICAL.md).
322: 
323: ### 13.4 Trainer Profile and Shared Volume
324: 
325: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
326: | --- | --- | --- |
327: | NFR-044 | Compose SHALL define a `train` profile containing the trainer service; the trainer SHALL NOT start with default `docker compose up`. | Must |
328: | NFR-045 | Compose SHALL define a named volume `model_artifacts` shared between the trainer (read-write) and ai-router (read-only). | Must |
329: | NFR-046 | The trainer SHALL use `train.py` from its build context and `train.csv` via a bind mount from `services/trainer/`; edits to `train.csv` take effect without rebuilding. | Must |
330: | NFR-047 | Training SHALL be triggered via `docker compose --profile train run --rm trainer`; ai-router SHALL reload the new model after `docker compose restart ai_router`. | Must |
331: 
332: ### 13.5 Dev Profile
333: 
334: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
335: | --- | --- | --- |
336: | NFR-032 | Dev profile SHALL support bind mounts for hot reload where applicable. | Must |
337: | NFR-033 | Dev profile MAY expose additional debug ports. | Should |
338: 
339: ## 14. Testing and Demonstration Requirements
340: 
341: ### 14.1 Browser Demo
342: 
343: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
344: | --- | --- | --- |
345: | AC-001 | `docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml up --build` SHALL start all services successfully. | Must |
346: | AC-002 | Opening the gateway URL in a browser SHALL show the UI and allow submitting queries. | Must |
347: | AC-003 | Test cases SHALL verify: "compare nginx ingress vs traefik" -> search-service; image intent -> image-service; ops intent -> ops-service; "hello"/"tell me a joke" -> unknown. | Must |
348: | AC-004 | For unknown, trace SHALL show no backend hop and 404 status. | Must |
349: 
350: ### 14.2 Terminal Demo
351: 
352: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
353: | --- | --- | --- |
354: | AC-005 | curl POST to /api/request SHALL return JSON with route, confidence, explanation, trace, backend_response (or message for unknown). | Must |
355: | AC-006 | Logs SHALL be correlatable by request_id (e.g., docker compose logs filtered by request_id). | Must |
356: 
357: ### 14.3 Scaling Demo
358: 
359: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
360: | --- | --- | --- |
361: | AC-007 | Scaling a backend (e.g., search-service=3) and running a load test SHALL show requests distributed across replicas. | Must |
362: | AC-008 | Trace SHALL indicate which backend instance handled the request where visible. | Should |
363: 
364: ### 14.4 Failure Demo
365: 
366: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
367: | --- | --- | --- |
368: | AC-009 | When a backend is stopped, gateway SHALL attempt proxy, fail, and return an error response with trace showing classification and backend failure. | Must |
369: | AC-010 | Unknown (AI decision) SHALL be distinguishable from backend failure: unknown has no backend hop and 404 message; failure has backend hop attempted and service unavailability message. | Must |
370: 
371: ## 15. Documentation and Deliverables
372: 
373: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
374: | --- | --- | --- |
375: | NFR-034 | `docs/auxiliary/demo/DEMO.md` SHALL provide copy-paste commands for browser, curl, scaling, and failure demos. | Must |
376: | NFR-035 | `docs/auxiliary/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md` SHALL describe system design and request flow. | Must |
377: | NFR-036 | `scripts/demo.sh` SHALL implement the full demo sequence as specified in the project plan. | Must |
378: | NFR-037 | `scripts/load_test.sh` SHALL implement load testing for scaling verification. | Must |
379: 
380: ## 16. Quality and Definition of Done
381: 
382: ### 16.1 Unit and Integration Tests
383: 
384: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
385: | --- | --- | --- |
386: | NFR-038 | Unit tests SHALL exist for ai-router classification logic. | Must |
387: | NFR-039 | Unit tests SHALL exist for gateway routing logic with mocked dependencies. | Must |
388: | NFR-040 | Integration tests SHALL run against the full compose stack where feasible. | Should |
389: 
390: ### 16.2 Definition of Done (Summary)
391: 
392: | ID | Requirement | Priority |
393: | --- | --- | --- |
394: | DoD-001 | docker compose up --build SHALL succeed on Linux, Mac, and Windows. | Must |
395: | DoD-002 | Browser UI SHALL display correct routing for all four route types and full trace visualization. | Must |
396: | DoD-003 | Curl demos SHALL produce deterministic, traceable outputs. | Must |
397: | DoD-004 | Scaling demo SHALL distribute load across replicas. | Must |
398: | DoD-005 | Failure demo SHALL show distinct behavior from unknown classification. | Must |
399: | DoD-006 | Multi-stage Dockerfiles, build-time model training, compose anchors/fragments/profiles, and health checks SHALL be implemented. | Must |
400: | DoD-007 | All services SHALL log request_id for correlation. | Must |
401: | DoD-008 | Unknown route SHALL return 404-equivalent without backend call; confidence thresholding SHALL prevent low-confidence routing. | Must |
402: 
403: ## 17. Requirements Traceability Summary
404: 
405: | Category | Count | IDs |
406: | --- | --- | --- |
407: | Business | 8 | BR-001 to BR-008 |
408: | Functional | 86 | FR-001 to FR-086 |
409: | Non-Functional | 49 | NFR-001 to NFR-049 |
410: | Acceptance / DoD | 18 | AC-001 to AC-010, DoD-001 to DoD-008 |
411: 
412: ## 18. Refiner and Dataset Improvement
413: 
414: The refiner service automates dataset improvement using a local LLM. See:
415: 
416: - [REFINER_PLAN.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PLAN.md) - Conceptual overview
417: and workflow
418: - [REFINER_PRD.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PRD.md) - Requirements
419: - [REFINER_TECHNICAL.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_TECHNICAL.md) - Technical
420: specification
421: - [REFINER_FLOW.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_FLOW.md) - End-to-end flow
422: - [REFINER_TASKS.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_TASKS.md) - Implementation tasks
423: 
424: ## 19. Optional / Future Enhancements
425: 
426: The following are out of scope for the current release but may be considered later:
427: 
428: - Enhanced explanations (top n-grams with weights)
429: - Request replay endpoint for debugging
430: - Metrics endpoint (request rate, latency per route)
431: - Additional backend services
432: - More sophisticated load balancing in gateway
433: - External training data sources (e.g., Stack Overflow, intent datasets)
434:   as optional augmentation
435: - On-demand Ollama model selection: UI dropdown to choose or pull LLM models
436:   for the refiner (via Ollama `/api/tags` and `/api/pull`), with download
437:   progress feedback
438: - Reduce `AUGMENT_MIN_PER_LABEL` further (e.g., from 5 to 3) to cut
439:   augmentation Ollama calls and speed up refinement
440: - Two-phase refinement: split into a fast "relabel-only" pass (< 2 min)
441:   that returns immediately, and a separate "augment" pass that runs
442:   asynchronously and streams results as they arrive
````

## File: docs/auxiliary/troubleshooting/DEBUG.md
````markdown
  1: # DEBUG RUNBOOK
  2: 
  3: This runbook contains copy-paste commands to debug and validate every stage
  4: of the pipeline:
  5: 
  6: - `train`
  7: - `refine` (split into `relabel` + `augment`)
  8: - `relabel` only
  9: - `augment` only
 10: - `promote`
 11: 
 12: ## Common Setup
 13: 
 14: Run from the repo root.
 15: 
 16: ```bash
 17: cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
 18: 
 19: COMPOSE_FILE="compose/docker-compose.yaml"
 20: GATEWAY_URL="http://localhost:8000"
 21: AI_ROUTER_URL="http://localhost:8001"
 22: ```
 23: 
 24: ## Start and Stop the Stack
 25: 
 26: Start with the Refine profile (includes `training-api`, `redis`, and `refiner`).
 27: 
 28: ```bash
 29: ./scripts/demo.sh run
 30: ```
 31: 
 32: Stop containers (keeps volumes).
 33: 
 34: ```bash
 35: ./scripts/demo.sh stop
 36: ```
 37: 
 38: Restart the stack (stop + start, accepts `--dev` and `--scale N`).
 39: 
 40: ```bash
 41: ./scripts/demo.sh restart
 42: ```
 43: 
 44: Delete everything (containers, networks, volumes).
 45: 
 46: ```bash
 47: ./scripts/demo.sh delete
 48: ```
 49: 
 50: Full teardown and rebuild (stop + delete + start,
 51: accepts same flags as `run`).
 52: 
 53: ```bash
 54: ./scripts/demo.sh reset
 55: ```
 56: 
 57: ## Sanity Checks
 58: 
 59: Health endpoint.
 60: 
 61: ```bash
 62: curl -s "$GATEWAY_URL/health" | python3 -m json.tool
 63: ```
 64: 
 65: Route map (shows backend URLs).
 66: 
 67: ```bash
 68: curl -s "$GATEWAY_URL/routes" | python3 -m json.tool
 69: ```
 70: 
 71: Direct ai-router classify (bypass gateway; returns probabilities and top
 72: explanation tokens).
 73: 
 74: ```bash
 75: curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:8001/classify" \
 76:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
 77:   -d '{"request_id":"debug-1","text":"Kubectl get pods in namespace default"}' \
 78:   | python3 -m json.tool
 79: ```
 80: 
 81: Quick comparison: direct `ai_router` classify vs gateway `/api/request`.
 82: 
 83: ```bash
 84: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8001/classify \
 85:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
 86:   -d '{"request_id":"cmp-1","text":"Kubectl get pods in namespace default"}' | jq
 87: 
 88: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/request \
 89:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
 90:   -d '{"request_id":"cmp-1","text":"Kubectl get pods in namespace default","trace":[]}' | jq
 91: ```
 92: 
 93: Tail logs (add any service names you need).
 94: 
 95: ```bash
 96: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" logs -f gateway ai_router training-api refiner trainer redis ollama
 97: ```
 98: 
 99: ## AI Router Debugging
100: 
101: Check that `ai_router` is running and port `8001` is published.
102: 
103: ```bash
104: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" ps ai_router
105: ```
106: 
107: Direct health check for `ai_router` (bypass gateway).
108: 
109: ```bash
110: curl -s "$AI_ROUTER_URL/health" | python3 -m json.tool
111: ```
112: 
113: Direct classify with full response fields.
114: 
115: ```bash
116: curl -s -X POST "$AI_ROUTER_URL/classify" \
117:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
118:   -d '{"request_id":"debug-ai-router-1","text":"kubectl get pods in namespace default"}' \
119:   | python3 -m json.tool
120: ```
121: 
122: Show headers and status for `/classify` (useful for 4xx/5xx triage).
123: 
124: ```bash
125: curl -sS -i -X POST "$AI_ROUTER_URL/classify" \
126:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
127:   -d '{"request_id":"debug-ai-router-2","text":"show current deployment health"}'
128: ```
129: 
130: Validate input contract (missing `text` should return 422).
131: 
132: ```bash
133: curl -sS -i -X POST "$AI_ROUTER_URL/classify" \
134:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
135:   -d '{"request_id":"debug-ai-router-3"}'
136: ```
137: 
138: Tail only `ai_router` logs.
139: 
140: ```bash
141: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" logs -f ai_router
142: ```
143: 
144: Inspect `MODEL_PATH` and model files inside `ai_router`.
145: 
146: ```bash
147: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T ai_router sh -lc '
148: echo "MODEL_PATH=$MODEL_PATH"
149: ls -la /model
150: ls -la /app/model
151: '
152: ```
153: 
154: Probe internal service-to-service access from `gateway` to `ai_router`.
155: 
156: ```bash
157: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T gateway python - <<'PY'
158: import urllib.request
159: print(urllib.request.urlopen("http://ai_router:8000/health", timeout=5).read().decode())
160: PY
161: ```
162: 
163: ## Inspect Model Artifacts (Most Important)
164: 
165: These paths live on the shared `model_artifacts` volume, mounted at `/model`
166: inside the `training-api` container.
167: 
168: List top-level artifact dirs.
169: 
170: ```bash
171: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc '
172: ls -la /model
173: ls -la /model/refine_runs 2>/dev/null || true
174: '
175: ```
176: 
177: List latest relabel/augment runs.
178: 
179: ```bash
180: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc '
181: ls -1 /model/refine_runs 2>/dev/null | tail -n 20 || true
182: '
183: ```
184: 
185: ## Train (Intent Router Model)
186: 
187: ### Option A: CLI
188: 
189: ```bash
190: ./scripts/demo.sh train
191: ```
192: 
193: ### Option B: API (Gateway)
194: 
195: Create job.
196: 
197: ```bash
198: curl -s -X POST "$GATEWAY_URL/api/train" | python3 -m json.tool
199: ```
200: 
201: Let `JOB_ID` be the returned `job_id`.
202: 
203: Check status until completed.
204: 
205: ```bash
206: JOB_ID="PASTE_JOB_ID"
207: curl -s "$GATEWAY_URL/api/train/status/$JOB_ID" | python3 -m json.tool
208: ```
209: 
210: Stream progress events.
211: 
212: ```bash
213: JOB_ID="PASTE_JOB_ID"
214: curl -sN "$GATEWAY_URL/api/train/events/$JOB_ID"
215: ```
216: 
217: Fetch last successful train result (from the shared artifacts volume).
218: 
219: ```bash
220: curl -s "$GATEWAY_URL/api/train/last" | python3 -m json.tool
221: ```
222: 
223: ### Verify Artifacts
224: 
225: ```bash
226: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc '
227: echo "--- /model/metrics.json ---"
228: cat /model/metrics.json
229: 
230: echo "--- /model/misclassified.csv (head) ---"
231: if [ -f /model/misclassified.csv ]; then
232:   head -n 15 /model/misclassified.csv
233: else
234:   echo "misclassified.csv missing"
235: fi
236: '
237: ```
238: 
239: ## Capturing JOB_ID and RUN_ID
240: 
241: Many relabel and augment failures look the same at the UI level (for example,
242: identical before/after metrics). The fastest path to the correct artifacts
243: is to capture `JOB_ID`/`RUN_ID` from the API responses and then map them to
244: the `refine_runs/<run_id>/...` files.
245: 
246: ### Expected JSON fields by endpoint
247: 
248: Train:
249: 
250: - `POST /api/train` returns: `{"job_id": "<uuid>"}`.
251: - `GET /api/train/status/{job_id}` returns:
252:   `{"job_id": "<uuid>", "status": "...", "result": {...}, "error": "..."}`
253:   (some keys may be `null`/missing depending on state).
254: - `GET /api/train/last` returns the last model artifacts summary (includes
255:   metrics and `misclassified` if present).
256: 
257: Relabel:
258: 
259: - `POST /api/refine/relabel` returns: `{"job_id": "<uuid>", "run_id": "<uuid>"}`.
260: - `GET /api/refine/relabel/events/{job_id}` is an SSE stream.
261:   - Progress events look like: `{"status":"progress","detail":"...","phase":"relabel",...}`
262:     (exact fields can vary).
263:   - Completion event looks like:
264:     `{"status":"completed","result":{...}}` or `{"status":"failed","error":"..."}`.
265:   - Final `result` (from the relabel phase) includes keys:
266:     `run_id`, `batches`, `train_relabel_candidate_rows`, `metrics_before`,
267:     `metrics_after`, `proposed_relabels`.
268: 
269: Augment:
270: 
271: - `POST /api/refine/augment` returns: `{"job_id": "<uuid>", "run_id": "<uuid>"}`.
272: - `GET /api/refine/augment/events/{job_id}` is an SSE stream.
273:   - Completion event looks like: `{"status":"completed","result":{...}}`.
274:   - Final `result` includes keys:
275:     `run_id`, `labels`, `train_augment_candidate_rows`, `metrics_before`,
276:     `metrics_after`, `proposed_examples`, and often `label_counts` (per-label
277:     synthetic row targets for the run).
278: 
279: Promote:
280: 
281: - `POST /api/refine/promote` returns at least:
282:   `{"promoted": true|false, "message": "...",`
283:   `"acc_before": <float>, "acc_after": <float>,`
284:   `"promote_accuracy_tolerance": <float>,`
285:   `"used_tolerance": <bool>,`
286:   `"per_label_recall": {"<label>": {"recall_before", "recall_after", "delta"}}}`
287:   (exact shape can vary on errors; tolerance fields support debugging).
288: 
289: ### Relabel: API start response
290: 
291: ```bash
292: RESP="$(curl -s -X POST "$GATEWAY_URL/api/refine/relabel" \
293:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
294:   -d '{}' )"
295: 
296: echo "$RESP" | python3 -m json.tool
297: 
298: JOB_ID="$(echo "$RESP" | python3 - <<'PY'
299: import sys, json
300: d=json.load(sys.stdin)
301: print(d.get("job_id",""))
302: PY
303: )"
304: 
305: RUN_ID="$(echo "$RESP" | python3 - <<'PY'
306: import sys, json
307: d=json.load(sys.stdin)
308: print(d.get("run_id",""))
309: PY
310: )"
311: 
312: echo "JOB_ID=$JOB_ID"
313: echo "RUN_ID=$RUN_ID"
314: ```
315: 
316: If `RUN_ID` is missing or empty, use the Redis job-state commands in the
317: `Redis Job Debugging` section at the end of this runbook.
318: 
319: ### Relabel: stream events (job completion only)
320: 
321: ```bash
322: JOB_ID="PASTE_JOB_ID"
323: 
324: curl -sN "$GATEWAY_URL/api/refine/relabel/events/$JOB_ID"
325: ```
326: 
327: ### Augment: API start response
328: 
329: ```bash
330: RESP="$(curl -s -X POST "$GATEWAY_URL/api/refine/augment" \
331:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
332:   -d '{}' )"
333: 
334: echo "$RESP" | python3 -m json.tool
335: 
336: JOB_ID="$(echo "$RESP" | python3 -c 'import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get("job_id",""))' <<<"$RESP")"
337: RUN_ID="$(echo "$RESP" | python3 -c 'import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get("run_id",""))' <<<"$RESP")"
338: 
339: echo "JOB_ID=$JOB_ID"
340: echo "RUN_ID=$RUN_ID"
341: ```
342: 
343: ### Artifact correlation: where "before" and "after" live
344: 
345: These are the canonical paths used by the relabel and augment phases.
346: 
347: Relabel:
348: 
349: ```bash
350: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
351: BEFORE="/model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/metrics_before.json"
352: AFTER="/model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/relabel/metrics_relabel_candidate.json"
353: echo "$BEFORE"
354: echo "$AFTER"
355: ```
356: 
357: Augment:
358: 
359: ```bash
360: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
361: BEFORE="/model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/metrics_before.json"
362: AFTER="/model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/augment/metrics_augment_candidate.json"
363: echo "$BEFORE"
364: echo "$AFTER"
365: ```
366: 
367: ### Optional: extract RUN_ID from the SSE stream (robust)
368: 
369: If you only have `JOB_ID` and the SSE parsing is brittle in your terminal,
370: use this regex-based approach to pull the first UUID-like run id from the
371: stream.
372: 
373: ```bash
374: JOB_ID="PASTE_JOB_ID"
375: URL="$GATEWAY_URL/api/refine/relabel/events/$JOB_ID"
376: 
377: curl -sN "$URL" | python3 - <<'PY'
378: import sys,re
379: txt=sys.stdin.read()
380: m=re.search(r'"run_id"\s*:\s*"([0-9a-fA-F-]{36})"', txt)
381: print(m.group(1) if m else "")
382: PY
383: ```
384: 
385: ## Refine (Full Pipeline: Relabel + Augment)
386: 
387: The "full refine" flow uses the CLI and runs split phases with a shared
388: `run_id`.
389: 
390: ### CLI (recommended for end-to-end testing)
391: 
392: ```bash
393: ./scripts/demo.sh refine --limit 5
394: ```
395: 
396: If you want explicit split control, use `scripts/demo.sh relabel` and
397: `scripts/demo.sh augment` with a shared `run_id`:
398: 
399: ```bash
400: RUN_ID="$(python3 -c 'import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())')"
401: 
402: ./scripts/demo.sh relabel --limit 5
403: # If relabel prints a run id, use that. Otherwise, rerun with:
404: # REFINER_RUN_ID="$RUN_ID" ./scripts/demo.sh relabel --limit 5
405: 
406: # Use the run id produced by relabel:
407: ./scripts/demo.sh augment --run-id "$RUN_ID"
408: ```
409: 
410: Then promote.
411: 
412: ```bash
413: ./scripts/demo.sh promote
414: ```
415: 
416: ## Relabel Only (Debugging the Data ETL)
417: 
418: ### Option A: CLI
419: 
420: ```bash
421: ./scripts/demo.sh relabel --limit 5
422: ```
423: 
424: This prints a `Run id: ...` value. Capture it as `RUN_ID`.
425: 
426: ### Option B: API (Gateway)
427: 
428: Start relabel job.
429: 
430: ```bash
431: RESP="$(curl -s -X POST "$GATEWAY_URL/api/refine/relabel" -H "Content-Type: application/json")"
432: echo "$RESP"
433: echo "$RESP" | python3 -m json.tool
434: ```
435: 
436: From the response, capture:
437: 
438: - `JOB_ID`
439: - `RUN_ID`
440: 
441: Stream job events (until completed/failed).
442: 
443: ```bash
444: JOB_ID="PASTE_JOB_ID"
445: curl -sN "$GATEWAY_URL/api/refine/relabel/events/$JOB_ID"
446: ```
447: 
448: ### Verify Candidate vs Base (Before/After Metrics)
449: 
450: ```bash
451: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
452: 
453: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc "
454: set -e
455: BEFORE='/model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/metrics_before.json'
456: AFTER='/model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/relabel/metrics_relabel_candidate.json'
457: 
458: echo \"Before: \$BEFORE\"
459: echo \"After:  \$AFTER\"
460: 
461: if cmp -s \"\$BEFORE\" \"\$AFTER\"; then
462:   echo 'metrics_before.json and metrics_relabel_candidate.json are byte-identical'
463: else
464:   echo 'metrics differ:'
465:   diff -u \"\$BEFORE\" \"\$AFTER\" | head -n 200
466: fi
467: "
468: ```
469: 
470: ### Confirm Whether Labels Actually Changed
471: 
472: ```bash
473: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
474: 
475: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc "
476: python3 - <<'PY'
477: import pandas as pd
478: 
479: rid='$RUN_ID'
480: cand_path=f'/model/refine_runs/{rid}/relabel/train_relabel_candidate.csv'
481: base_path='/promote_target/train.csv'
482: 
483: cand=pd.read_csv(cand_path)
484: base=pd.read_csv(base_path)
485: 
486: m=cand.merge(base[['text','label']], on='text', how='inner',
487:               suffixes=('_cand','_base'))
488: diff=(m['label_cand']!=m['label_base'])
489: 
490: print('cand_rows:', len(cand))
491: print('overlap_rows:', len(m))
492: print('label_changes:', int(diff.sum()))
493: PY
494: "
495: ```
496: 
497: ### ETL Artifacts Created Per Batch
498: 
499: List batch files.
500: 
501: ```bash
502: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
503: 
504: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc "
505: ls -1 /model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/relabel/batches
506: "
507: ```
508: 
509: For each `batch_XXXX`, check:
510: 
511: - prompt sent to Ollama: `prompt_relabel.batch_XXXX.txt`
512: - raw Ollama response: `raw_proposed_relabels.batch_XXXX.txt`
513: - parse/sanitize summary: `proposed_relabels.batch_XXXX.validation.json`
514: - rejected rows: `proposed_relabels.batch_XXXX.rejected_items.csv`
515: - sanitized proposals used for training:
516:   `proposed_relabels.batch_XXXX.csv`
517: 
518: Example (batch `0000`).
519: 
520: ```bash
521: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
522: BATCH_ID="0000"
523: 
524: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc "
525: set -e
526: DIR=\"/model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/relabel/batches\"
527: 
528: echo '--- prompt ---'
529: sed -n '1,200p' \"\$DIR/prompt_relabel.batch_${BATCH_ID}.txt\"
530: 
531: echo '--- raw response ---'
532: sed -n '1,200p' \"\$DIR/raw_proposed_relabels.batch_${BATCH_ID}.txt\"
533: 
534: echo '--- validation summary ---'
535: cat \"\$DIR/proposed_relabels.batch_${BATCH_ID}.validation.json\" | head -n 200
536: 
537: echo '--- rejected items (head) ---'
538: if [ -f \"\$DIR/proposed_relabels.batch_${BATCH_ID}.rejected_items.csv\" ]; then
539:   head -n 50 \"\$DIR/proposed_relabels.batch_${BATCH_ID}.rejected_items.csv\"
540: else
541:   echo 'no rejected items file'
542: fi
543: "
544: ```
545: 
546: ### Merge-Level ETL Validation
547: 
548: ```bash
549: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
550: 
551: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc "
552: cat /model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/relabel/merge_relabel.validation.json | head -n 200
553: "
554: ```
555: 
556: ### Inspect Misclassified Input
557: 
558: The relabel worker consumes `/model/misclassified.csv`.
559: 
560: ```bash
561: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc '
562: echo "--- misclassified.csv (head) ---"
563: head -n 30 /model/misclassified.csv
564: '
565: ```
566: 
567: ## Augment Only (Debugging the Data ETL)
568: 
569: ### Option A: CLI (best for "augment matching a prior relabel run")
570: 
571: ```bash
572: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID_FROM_RELABEL"
573: ./scripts/demo.sh augment --run-id "$RUN_ID"
574: ```
575: 
576: ### Option B: API (Gateway)
577: 
578: Start augment job (creates a new `run_id`).
579: 
580: ```bash
581: RESP="$(curl -s -X POST "$GATEWAY_URL/api/refine/augment" -H "Content-Type: application/json")"
582: echo "$RESP"
583: echo "$RESP" | python3 -m json.tool
584: ```
585: 
586: From the response, capture `JOB_ID` and `RUN_ID`.
587: 
588: Stream job events.
589: 
590: ```bash
591: JOB_ID="PASTE_JOB_ID"
592: curl -sN "$GATEWAY_URL/api/refine/augment/events/$JOB_ID"
593: ```
594: 
595: ### Verify Candidate vs Base (Before/After Metrics)
596: 
597: ```bash
598: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
599: 
600: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc "
601: set -e
602: BEFORE='/model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/metrics_before.json'
603: AFTER='/model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/augment/metrics_augment_candidate.json'
604: 
605: echo \"Before: \$BEFORE\"
606: echo \"After:  \$AFTER\"
607: 
608: if cmp -s \"\$BEFORE\" \"\$AFTER\"; then
609:   echo 'metrics_before.json and metrics_augment_candidate.json are byte-identical'
610: else
611:   echo 'metrics differ:'
612:   diff -u \"\$BEFORE\" \"\$AFTER\" | head -n 200
613: fi
614: "
615: ```
616: 
617: ### ETL Artifacts Created Per Label
618: 
619: List augment label task output.
620: 
621: ```bash
622: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
623: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc "
624: ls -1 /model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/augment/labels
625: "
626: ```
627: 
628: For label `search`, example files include:
629: 
630: - `raw_augment.label_search.txt`
631: - `prompt_augment.label_search.txt`
632: - `augment.label_search.validation.json`
633: - `augment.label_search.rejected_items.csv`
634: - `proposed_examples.label_search.csv`
635: 
636: Example inspection.
637: 
638: ```bash
639: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
640: LABEL="search"
641: 
642: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc "
643: DIR=\"/model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/augment/labels\"
644: SAFE=\"$LABEL\"
645: echo '--- raw response ---'
646: sed -n '1,200p' \"\$DIR/raw_augment.label_${SAFE}.txt\"
647: 
648: echo '--- validation summary ---'
649: cat \"\$DIR/augment.label_${SAFE}.validation.json\" | head -n 200
650: 
651: echo '--- rejected items (head) ---'
652: if [ -f \"\$DIR/augment.label_${SAFE}.rejected_items.csv\" ]; then
653:   head -n 50 \"\$DIR/augment.label_${SAFE}.rejected_items.csv\"
654: fi
655: "
656: ```
657: 
658: ### Merge-Level ETL Validation
659: 
660: ```bash
661: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
662: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc "
663: cat /model/refine_runs/$RUN_ID/augment/merge_augment.validation.json | head -n 200
664: # Expects counts: input_rows_count, accepted_rows_count, invalid_rows_count,
665: # duplicate_existing_count, fuzzy_duplicate_count
666: "
667: ```
668: 
669: ## Promote (Apply Candidate if Metrics Improve)
670: 
671: ### CLI
672: 
673: ```bash
674: ./scripts/demo.sh promote
675: ```
676: 
677: ### Option B: API (Gateway)
678: 
679: Promote a specific run:
680: 
681: ```bash
682: RUN_ID="PASTE_RUN_ID"
683: curl -s -X POST "$GATEWAY_URL/api/refine/promote" \
684:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
685:   -d \"{\\\"run_id\\\": \\\"$RUN_ID\\\"}\" | python3 -m json.tool
686: ```
687: 
688: If `run_id` is omitted, it uses legacy behavior (expects the most recent
689: `train_candidate.csv` in `/model`).
690: 
691: ```bash
692: curl -s -X POST "$GATEWAY_URL/api/refine/promote" \
693:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
694:   -d '{}' | python3 -m json.tool
695: ```
696: 
697: ### Verify Promotion Artifacts
698: 
699: If promotion succeeded and updated the model:
700: 
701: ```bash
702: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc '
703: ls -la /model/model.joblib /model/metrics.json
704: echo "--- /model/metrics.json ---"
705: cat /model/metrics.json
706: '
707: ```
708: 
709: Restart `ai_router` to use the newly promoted model:
710: 
711: ```bash
712: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" restart ai_router
713: ```
714: 
715: ## Redis Job Debugging (Optional but Useful)
716: 
717: This is helpful when your job appears to hang or your artifacts are missing.
718: 
719: ### List Job Keys
720: 
721: ```bash
722: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T redis redis-cli keys 'job:*'
723: ```
724: 
725: ### Inspect a Specific Job State (Relabel)
726: 
727: ```bash
728: JOB_ID="PASTE_JOB_ID"
729: 
730: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc "
731: python3 - <<'PY'
732: from app.redis_client import get_job_state
733: job_id='$JOB_ID'
734: key='job:refine:relabel:' + job_id
735: st=get_job_state(key)
736: print('key:', key)
737: print('present:', st is not None)
738: print('status:', (st or {}).get('status'))
739: print('run_id:', ((st or {}).get('result') or {}).get('run_id'))
740: print('result_keys:', sorted(((st or {}).get('result') or {}).keys()))
741: PY
742: "
743: ```
744: 
745: ### Inspect a Specific Job State (Augment)
746: 
747: ```bash
748: JOB_ID="PASTE_JOB_ID"
749: 
750: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T training-api sh -lc "
751: python3 - <<'PY'
752: from app.redis_client import get_job_state
753: job_id='$JOB_ID'
754: key='job:refine:augment:' + job_id
755: st=get_job_state(key)
756: print('key:', key)
757: print('present:', st is not None)
758: print('status:', (st or {}).get('status'))
759: print('run_id:', ((st or {}).get('result') or {}).get('run_id'))
760: print('result_keys:', sorted(((st or {}).get('result') or {}).keys()))
761: PY
762: "
763: ```
764: 
765: ## Common Failure Modes and What to Check
766: 
767: ### "Before and After metrics are identical"
768: 
769: Check:
770: 
771: 1. Whether relabel/augment changed the candidate dataset.
772: 2. Whether the ETL rejected almost everything (look at
773:    `*.validation.json` and `*.rejected_items.csv`).
774: 3. Whether the only accepted proposal produced no effective label change.
775: 
776: Use the "Verify Candidate vs Base" and "ETL Artifacts" sections above.
777: 
778: ### "Only 0 to 1 proposals/examples appear"
779: 
780: For relabel:
781: 
782: 1. Count misclassified rows vs batch output rows.
783: 2. Inspect `raw_proposed_relabels.batch_*.txt` and
784:    `proposed_relabels.batch_*.validation.json`.
785: 
786: For augment:
787: 
788: 1. Inspect `augment.label_*.validation.json` (check `verified_count`,
789:    `verification_rejected_count` when verification is on) and
790:    `augment.label_*.rejected_items.csv`.
791: 2. Inspect `merge_augment.validation.json` (including `fuzzy_duplicate_count`).
792: 3. Confirm `train.csv` has enough rows per label for seeds when using
793:    `REFINER_AUGMENT_SEED_EXAMPLES`.
794: 
795: ### "Job appears stuck"
796: 
797: 1. Tail `training-api` logs and `refiner`/Ollama logs.
798: 2. Inspect Redis job state using the Redis section above.
799: 3. Check whether output files exist under `/model/refine_runs/<run_id>/...`.
````

## File: scripts/promote.sh
````bash
 1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
 2: # Promote train_candidate.csv to train.csv only if retraining improves metrics.
 3: # Uses the canonical training-api promote flow (same behavior as the Refine UI
 4: # "Promote" button).
 5: #
 6: # Prerequisites: Run the refine pipeline first (via UI relabel + augment, or
 7: # via CLI: ./scripts/demo.sh refine) so that train_candidate.csv and metrics
 8: # artifacts exist for the most recent run.
 9: # After promotion, restart ai_router to use the new model.
10: set -euo pipefail
11: COMPOSE_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
12: COMPOSE_FILE="${COMPOSE_DIR}/compose/docker-compose.yaml"
13: CONFIG_ENV="${CONFIG_ENV:-dev}"
14: if [[ ! "$CONFIG_ENV" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ ]]; then
15:   echo "Invalid CONFIG_ENV: $CONFIG_ENV" >&2
16:   exit 1
17: fi
18: ensure_env() {
19:   local env_file="${COMPOSE_DIR}/env/.env.${CONFIG_ENV}"
20:   if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
21:     echo "Generating $env_file from config/PROJECT_CONFIG.yaml (CONFIG_ENV=${CONFIG_ENV})..."
22:     python3 "${COMPOSE_DIR}/scripts/generate_env.py" "${CONFIG_ENV}"
23:   fi
24: }
25: cd "$COMPOSE_DIR"
26: ensure_env
27: export ENV="${CONFIG_ENV}"
28: docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" --profile refine run --rm training-api promote
29: echo ""
30: echo "If promoted, restart ai_router to use the new model:"
31: echo "  docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml restart ai_router"
````

````python
  1: """Tests for relabel and augment enqueue/process via Redis Streams."""
  2: from __future__ import annotations
  3: import json
  4: from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

  6: import pytest
  7: from import_training_api import training_api_imported
  8: with training_api_imported():
  9:     from app.refine import relabel as relabel_mod
 10:     from app.refine import augment as augment_mod
 11:     from app.refine.config import RefineConfig
 12:     from app.refine.relabel import (
 13:         RelabelTask,
 14:         enqueue_relabel_batches,
 15:         run_relabel_workers,
 16:         _handle_task as handle_relabel_task,
 17:         merge_relabel_outputs,
 18:     )
 19:     from app.refine.parser import parse_json_response as _parse_json_response
 20:     from app.refine.augment import (
 21:         enqueue_augment_tasks,
 22:         run_augment_workers,
 23:     )
 24:     from app.refine.ollama_pool import OllamaPool
 25: def _cfg(tmp_path) -> RefineConfig:
 26:     return RefineConfig(
 27:         runs_root=tmp_path / "runs",
 28:         relabel_batch_size=2,
 29:         relabel_max_parallel_batches=1,
 30:         augment_n_per_label=3,
 31:         augment_max_parallel_labels=1,
 32:         augment_verify_labels=False,
 33:         augment_verify_min_confidence=0.8,
 34:         augment_max_text_length=100,
 35:         augment_seed_examples=3,
 36:         promote_accuracy_tolerance=0.01,
 37:         ollama_urls=["http://ollama1:11434"],
 38:         ollama_model="phi3:mini",
 39:         ollama_timeout_seconds=10,
 40:         ollama_max_inflight_per_instance=2,
 41:         ollama_num_ctx=512,
 42:         ollama_num_predict=128,
 43:         refiner_temperature=0.1,
 44:         refiner_seed=42,
 45:         structured_output_enabled=True,
 46:         relabel_min_confidence=0.0,
 47:         relabel_tasks_stream="test:relabel:tasks",
 48:         augment_tasks_stream="test:augment:tasks",
 49:         relabel_consumer_group="relabel-workers",
 50:         augment_consumer_group="augment-workers",
 51:         events_channel_prefix="test:events:",
 52:     )
 53: def _train_df_for_augment() -> pd.DataFrame:
 54:     return pd.DataFrame(
 55:         [
 56:             {"text": "find restaurants nearby", "label": "search"},
 57:             {"text": "compare cloud providers", "label": "search"},
 58:         ]
 59:     )
 60: # --- enqueue_relabel_batches ---
 61: def test_enqueue_relabel_batches_creates_correct_number(tmp_path):
 62:     cfg = _cfg(tmp_path)
 63:     rows = [
 64:         {"text": f"t{i}", "true_label": "search", "pred_label": "ops"}
 65:         for i in range(5)
 66:     ]
 67:     with patch.object(relabel_mod, "stream_add") as mock_add:
 68:         count = enqueue_relabel_batches(cfg, run_id="r1", misclassified_rows=rows)
 69:     # batch_size=2, 5 rows -> 3 batches (2+2+1)
 70:     assert count == 3
 71:     assert mock_add.call_count == 3
 72: def test_enqueue_relabel_batches_uses_correct_stream(tmp_path):
 73:     cfg = _cfg(tmp_path)
 74:     rows = [{"text": "t1", "true_label": "search", "pred_label": "ops"}]
 75:     with patch.object(relabel_mod, "stream_add") as mock_add:
 76:         enqueue_relabel_batches(cfg, run_id="r1", misclassified_rows=rows)
 77:     assert mock_add.call_args[0][0] == "test:relabel:tasks"
 78: def test_enqueue_relabel_batches_fields_contain_run_id(tmp_path):
 79:     cfg = _cfg(tmp_path)
 80:     rows = [{"text": "t1", "true_label": "search", "pred_label": "ops"}]
 81:     with patch.object(relabel_mod, "stream_add") as mock_add:
 82:         enqueue_relabel_batches(cfg, run_id="r1", misclassified_rows=rows)
 83:     fields = mock_add.call_args[0][1]

 85:     assert "batch_id" in fields
 86:     assert "rows" in fields

 90:         count = enqueue_relabel_batches(cfg, run_id="r1", misclassified_rows=[])
 91:     assert count == 0

## File: services/training-api/app/refine/augment.py
````python
  1: from __future__ import annotations
  2: import csv
  3: import json
  4: import os
  5: import random
  6: import threading
  7: from collections import defaultdict
  8: from dataclasses import dataclass
  9: from pathlib import Path
 10: from typing import Any, Callable
 11: import pandas as pd  # pylint: disable=import-error
 12: import requests  # pylint: disable=import-error
 13: from app.redis_client import (
 14:     publish_event,
 15:     stream_ack,
 16:     stream_add,
 17:     stream_group_create,
 18:     stream_auto_claim_pending,
 19:     stream_get_delivery_count,
 20:     stream_read_group,
 21: )
 22: from app.refine.config import RefineConfig
 23: from app.refine.ollama_pool import OllamaPool, get_ollama_pool
 24: from app.refine.prompts import (
 25:     LABELS,
 26:     SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS,
 27:     augment_examples,
 28:     relabel_misclassified_batch,
 29: )
 30: from app.refine.training import train_candidate
 31: from app.refine.parser import parse_json_response
 32: MIN_TEXT_LENGTH = 3
 33: FUZZY_DEDUP_JACCARD_THRESHOLD = 0.85
 34: @dataclass(frozen=True)
 35: class AugmentTask:
 36:     run_id: str
 37:     label: str
 38:     n: int
 39: def _char_trigrams(text: str) -> set[str]:
 40:     t = text.strip()
 41:     if len(t) < 3:
 42:         return {t} if t else set()
 43:     return {t[i : i + 3] for i in range(len(t) - 2)}
 44: def _trigram_jaccard(a: set[str], b: set[str]) -> float:
 45:     if not a and not b:
 46:         return 1.0
 47:     if not a or not b:
 48:         return 0.0
 49:     inter = len(a & b)
 50:     union = len(a | b)
 51:     return inter / union if union else 0.0
 52: def _is_fuzzy_duplicate(
 53:     text: str, reference_sets: list[set[str]], threshold: float = FUZZY_DEDUP_JACCARD_THRESHOLD
 54: ) -> bool:
 55:     tset = _char_trigrams(text)
 56:     for ref in reference_sets:
 57:         if _trigram_jaccard(tset, ref) > threshold:
 58:             return True
 59:     return False
 60: def _compute_augment_counts(
 61:     train_df: pd.DataFrame,
 62:     labels: list[str],
 63:     base_n: int,
 64: ) -> dict[str, int]:
 65:     """Weight per-label N by class frequency: rarer classes get more synthetic rows."""
 66:     counts: dict[str, int] = {}
 67:     if train_df.empty or "label" not in train_df.columns:
 68:         return {lab: base_n for lab in labels}
 69:     vc = train_df["label"].astype(str).str.strip().value_counts()
 70:     max_c = int(vc.max()) if len(vc) else 1
 71:     max_c = max(max_c, 1)
 72:     cap = 3 * base_n
 73:     for lab in labels:
 74:         this_c = int(vc.get(lab, 0)) if lab in vc.index else 0
 75:         denom = max(this_c, 1)
 76:         n = int(round(base_n * (max_c / denom)))
 77:         n = max(base_n, min(cap, n))
 78:         counts[lab] = n
 79:     return counts
 80: def _sample_seed_texts(
 81:     train_df: pd.DataFrame,
 82:     label: str,
 83:     k: int,
 84:     rng: random.Random,
 85: ) -> list[str]:
 86:     if k <= 0:
 87:         return []
 88:     sub = train_df[train_df["label"].astype(str).str.strip() == label]
 89:     texts = [str(x).strip() for x in sub["text"].astype(str).tolist() if str(x).strip()]
 90:     if not texts:
 91:         return []
 92:     if len(texts) <= k:
 93:         return texts
 94:     return rng.sample(texts, k)
 95: def _write_csv(path: Path, rows: list[dict[str, Any]], fieldnames: list[str]) -> None:
 96:     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 97:     with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8", newline="") as f:
 98:         w = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames)
 99:         w.writeheader()
100:         for r in rows:
101:             w.writerow({k: r.get(k, "") for k in fieldnames})
102: def _write_json(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
103:     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
104:     with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
105:         json.dump(payload, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
106: def _write_text(path: Path, content: str) -> None:
107:     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
108:     with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
109:         f.write(content or "")
110: def _read_csv_rows(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
111:     if not path.exists():
112:         return []
113:     with open(path, encoding="utf-8", newline="") as f:
114:         return list(csv.DictReader(f))
115: def _read_train_csv(promote_target_dir: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
116:     train_path = os.path.join(promote_target_dir, "train.csv")
117:     if not os.path.exists(train_path):
118:         raise FileNotFoundError(f"train.csv not found at {train_path}")
119:     df = pd.read_csv(train_path)
120:     if "text" not in df.columns or "label" not in df.columns:
121:         raise ValueError("train.csv missing required columns: text,label")
122:     return df
123: def _read_metrics(artifacts_dir: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
124:     path = os.path.join(artifacts_dir, "metrics.json")
125:     if not os.path.exists(path):
126:         return {}
127:     try:
128:         with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
129:             return json.load(f)
130:     except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
131:         return {}
132: def _labels_to_augment(metrics: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
133:     # Keep the baseline behavior: augment any label present in metrics report
134:     # with low recall (<0.75). If metrics missing, augment all labels.
135:     report = metrics.get("classification_report", {})
136:     labels: list[str] = []
137:     for label in LABELS:
138:         if label in report and isinstance(report[label], dict):
139:             recall = report[label].get("recall")
140:             if recall is not None and float(recall) < 0.75:
141:                 labels.append(label)
142:     return labels or LABELS
143: def enqueue_augment_tasks(
144:     cfg: RefineConfig,
145:     *,
146:     run_id: str,
147:     labels: list[str],
148:     label_counts: dict[str, int] | None = None,
149: ) -> int:
150:     for label in labels:
151:         n = (
152:             int(label_counts.get(label, cfg.augment_n_per_label))
153:             if label_counts
154:             else cfg.augment_n_per_label
155:         )
156:         stream_add(
157:             cfg.augment_tasks_stream,
158:             {"run_id": run_id, "label": label, "n": str(max(1, n))},
159:         )
160:     return len(labels)
161: def _label_output_path(cfg: RefineConfig, run_id: str, label: str) -> Path:
162:     safe = label.replace("/", "_")
163:     return cfg.augment_dir(run_id) / "labels" / f"proposed_examples.label_{safe}.csv"
164: def _label_raw_output_path(cfg: RefineConfig, run_id: str, label: str) -> Path:
165:     safe = label.replace("/", "_")
166:     return cfg.augment_dir(run_id) / "labels" / f"raw_augment.label_{safe}.txt"
167: def _label_prompt_output_path(cfg: RefineConfig, run_id: str, label: str) -> Path:
168:     safe = label.replace("/", "_")
169:     return cfg.augment_dir(run_id) / "labels" / f"prompt_augment.label_{safe}.txt"
170: def _label_validation_output_path(cfg: RefineConfig, run_id: str, label: str) -> Path:
171:     safe = label.replace("/", "_")
172:     return cfg.augment_dir(run_id) / "labels" / f"augment.label_{safe}.validation.json"
173: def _label_rejected_output_path(cfg: RefineConfig, run_id: str, label: str) -> Path:
174:     safe = label.replace("/", "_")
175:     return cfg.augment_dir(run_id) / "labels" / f"augment.label_{safe}.rejected_items.csv"
176: def _parse_json_array(raw: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]] | None:
177:     data = parse_json_response(raw)
178:     if not data:
179:         return None
180:     out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
181:     for item in data:
182:         if not isinstance(item, dict):
183:             continue
184:         t = str(item.get("text") or "").strip()
185:         l = str(item.get("label") or "").strip()
186:         if len(t) < MIN_TEXT_LENGTH or "\n" in t or l not in LABELS:
187:             continue
188:         out.append({"text": t, "label": l, "source_pattern": "augmentation"})
189:     return out
190: def _parse_json_array_etl(
191:     *,
192:     raw: str,
193:     expected_label: str,
194:     max_text_length: int,
195: ) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]] | None, list[dict[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
196:     data = parse_json_response(raw)
197:     if data is None:
198:         return (
199:             None,
200:             [],
201:             {
202:                 "error_code": "INVALID_JSON_ARRAY",
203:                 "error_message": "Invalid JSON array from Ollama",
204:             },
205:         )
206:     accepted: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
207:     rejected: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
208:     for idx, item in enumerate(data):
209:         if not isinstance(item, dict):
210:             rejected.append({"idx": idx, "error_code": "INVALID_ITEM_TYPE"})
211:             continue
212:         t = str(item.get("text") or "").strip()
213:         l = str(item.get("label") or "").strip()
214:         # Preserve current acceptance semantics (order of checks matters).
215:         if len(t) < MIN_TEXT_LENGTH:
216:             rejected.append(
217:                 {"idx": idx, "error_code": "TEXT_TOO_SHORT", "text": t, "label": l}
218:             )
219:             continue
220:         if "\n" in t:
221:             rejected.append(
222:                 {
223:                     "idx": idx,
224:                     "error_code": "TEXT_CONTAINS_NEWLINE",
225:                     "text": t,
226:                     "label": l,
227:                 }
228:             )
229:             continue
230:         if l not in LABELS:
231:             rejected.append(
232:                 {"idx": idx, "error_code": "INVALID_LABEL", "text": t, "label": l}
233:             )
234:             continue
235:         if l != expected_label:
236:             rejected.append(
237:                 {
238:                     "idx": idx,
239:                     "error_code": "LABEL_MISMATCH",
240:                     "text": t,
241:                     "label": l,
242:                 }
243:             )
244:             continue
245:         if len(t) > max_text_length:
246:             rejected.append(
247:                 {
248:                     "idx": idx,
249:                     "error_code": "TEXT_TOO_LONG",
250:                     "text": t,
251:                     "label": l,
252:                 }
253:             )
254:             continue
255:         accepted.append({"text": t, "label": l, "source_pattern": "augmentation"})
256:     validation: dict[str, Any] = {
257:         "error_code": None,
258:         "parsed_items_count": int(len(data)),
259:         "accepted_items_count": int(len(accepted)),
260:         "rejected_items_count": int(len(rejected)),
261:         "rejected_items": rejected,
262:     }
263:     return accepted, rejected, validation
264: def _verify_augmented_examples(
265:     cfg: RefineConfig,
266:     *,
267:     intended_label: str,
268:     accepted: list[dict[str, Any]],
269:     pool: OllamaPool,
270: ) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]], dict[str, int]]:
271:     """Re-label generated rows; keep rows that match intended_label with enough confidence."""
272:     if not cfg.augment_verify_labels or not accepted:
273:         return accepted, [], {"verified_count": len(accepted), "verification_rejected_count": 0}
274:     verified: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
275:     rejected: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
276:     batch_size = max(1, cfg.relabel_batch_size)
277:     for i in range(0, len(accepted), batch_size):
278:         chunk = accepted[i : i + batch_size]
279:         rows = [
280:             {"text": ex["text"], "true_label": intended_label, "pred_label": intended_label}
281:             for ex in chunk
282:         ]
283:         prompt = relabel_misclassified_batch(rows)
284:         raw = pool.generate(prompt=prompt, system=SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS)
285:         parsed = parse_json_response(raw)
286:         if not parsed or len(parsed) != len(chunk):
287:             for ex in chunk:
288:                 rejected.append(
289:                     {
290:                         **ex,
291:                         "error_code": "VERIFICATION_FAILED",
292:                         "reason": "invalid_verify_response",
293:                     }
294:                 )
295:             continue
296:         for ex, obj in zip(chunk, parsed):
297:             sugg = str(obj.get("suggested_label") or "").strip()
298:             conf_raw = obj.get("confidence", 0.0)
299:             try:
300:                 conf = float(conf_raw)
301:             except (TypeError, ValueError):
302:                 conf = 0.0
303:             if sugg != intended_label or conf < cfg.augment_verify_min_confidence:
304:                 rejected.append(
305:                     {
306:                         **ex,
307:                         "error_code": "VERIFICATION_FAILED",
308:                         "suggested_label": sugg,
309:                         "confidence": conf,
310:                     }
311:                 )
312:             else:
313:                 verified.append(ex)
314:     stats = {
315:         "verified_count": len(verified),
316:         "verification_rejected_count": len(rejected),
317:     }
318:     return verified, rejected, stats
319: def run_augment_workers(
320:     cfg: RefineConfig,
321:     *,
322:     run_id: str,
323:     expected_labels: int,
324:     train_df: pd.DataFrame,
325:     progress: Callable[[dict[str, Any]], None] | None = None,
326: ) -> None:
327:     group = f"{cfg.augment_consumer_group}:{run_id}"
328:     stream_group_create(cfg.augment_tasks_stream, group)
329:     def _mk_pool() -> OllamaPool:
330:         return OllamaPool(
331:             cfg.ollama_urls,
332:             model=cfg.ollama_model,
333:             timeout_seconds=cfg.ollama_timeout_seconds,
334:             max_inflight_per_instance=cfg.ollama_max_inflight_per_instance,
335:             num_ctx=cfg.ollama_num_ctx,
336:             num_predict=cfg.ollama_num_predict,
337:             temperature=cfg.refiner_temperature,
338:             seed=cfg.refiner_seed,
339:             structured_output_enabled=cfg.structured_output_enabled,
340:         )
341:     pool = get_ollama_pool(_mk_pool)
342:     rng = random.Random(cfg.refiner_seed)
343:     done_lock = threading.Lock()
344:     done_labels: set[str] = set()
345:     max_retries = int(os.environ.get("REFINER_AUGMENT_MAX_RETRIES", "3"))
346:     def _worker(worker_idx: int) -> None:
347:         consumer = f"{run_id[:8]}-{worker_idx}"
348:         while True:
349:             items = stream_read_group(
350:                 cfg.augment_tasks_stream,
351:                 group,
352:                 consumer,
353:                 count=1,
354:                 block_ms=1000,
355:             )
356:             if not items:
357:                 # Nothing new; attempt to claim any idle pending entries so that
358:                 # timeouts or crashed workers do not leave the job stuck.
359:                 pending = stream_auto_claim_pending(
360:                     cfg.augment_tasks_stream,
361:                     group,
362:                     consumer,
363:                     min_idle_ms=cfg.ollama_timeout_seconds * 1000,
364:                     count=1,
365:                 )
366:                 if not pending:
367:                     with done_lock:
368:                         if len(done_labels) >= expected_labels:
369:                             return
370:                     continue
371:                 items = pending
372:             entry_id, fields = items[0]
373:             try:
374:                 if fields.get("run_id") != run_id:
375:                     continue
376:                 label = str(fields.get("label") or "").strip()
377:                 n = int(str(fields.get("n") or cfg.augment_n_per_label))
378:                 if label not in LABELS:
379:                     raise ValueError(f"Invalid label: {label}")
380:                 seeds = _sample_seed_texts(
381:                     train_df, label, cfg.augment_seed_examples, rng
382:                 )
383:                 prompt = augment_examples(label, n, seeds)
384:                 raw = pool.generate(prompt=prompt, system=SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS)
385:                 _write_text(_label_raw_output_path(cfg, run_id, label), raw)
386:                 _write_text(_label_prompt_output_path(cfg, run_id, label), prompt)
387:                 accepted, rejected, validation = _parse_json_array_etl(
388:                     raw=raw,
389:                     expected_label=label,
390:                     max_text_length=cfg.augment_max_text_length,
391:                 )
392:                 validation.update({"label": label, "n": int(n)})
393:                 if accepted is None:
394:                     raise ValueError("Invalid JSON array from Ollama")
395:                 verify_rejected: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
396:                 if accepted:
397:                     verified, verify_rejected, vstats = _verify_augmented_examples(
398:                         cfg,
399:                         intended_label=label,
400:                         accepted=accepted,
401:                         pool=pool,
402:                     )
403:                     accepted = verified
404:                     validation.update(vstats)
405:                 else:
406:                     validation.update(
407:                         {"verified_count": 0, "verification_rejected_count": 0}
408:                     )
409:                 all_rejected = list(rejected) + verify_rejected
410:                 _write_json(_label_validation_output_path(cfg, run_id, label), validation)
411:                 if all_rejected:
412:                     rej_fields = [
413:                         "idx",
414:                         "error_code",
415:                         "text",
416:                         "label",
417:                         "reason",
418:                         "suggested_label",
419:                         "confidence",
420:                     ]
421:                     norm_rejected = [
422:                         {
423:                             "idx": r.get("idx", ""),
424:                             "error_code": r.get("error_code", ""),
425:                             "text": r.get("text", ""),
426:                             "label": r.get("label", ""),
427:                             "reason": r.get("reason", ""),
428:                             "suggested_label": r.get("suggested_label", ""),
429:                             "confidence": r.get("confidence", ""),
430:                         }
431:                         for r in all_rejected
432:                     ]
433:                     _write_csv(
434:                         _label_rejected_output_path(cfg, run_id, label),
435:                         norm_rejected,
436:                         fieldnames=rej_fields,
437:                     )
438:                 out_path = _label_output_path(cfg, run_id, label)
439:                 _write_csv(
440:                     out_path, accepted, fieldnames=["text", "label", "source_pattern"]
441:                 )
442:                 stream_ack(cfg.augment_tasks_stream, group, entry_id)
443:                 with done_lock:
444:                     done_labels.add(label)
445:                     completed = len(done_labels)
446:                 evt = {
447:                     "status": "progress",
448:                     "phase": "augment",
449:                     "detail": f"Augment {label} {completed}/{expected_labels} complete",
450:                     "label": label,
451:                 }
452:                 if progress:
453:                     progress(evt)
454:                 publish_event(cfg.events_channel(run_id), evt)
455:             except (ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError, requests.RequestException) as e:
456:                 err_evt = {
457:                     "status": "progress",
458:                     "phase": "augment",
459:                     "detail": f"Augment task failed: {e}",
460:                     "error": str(e),
461:                     "entry_id": entry_id,
462:                     "label": fields.get("label"),
463:                 }
464:                 if progress:
465:                     progress(err_evt)
466:                 publish_event(cfg.events_channel(run_id), err_evt)
467:                 deliveries = stream_get_delivery_count(
468:                     cfg.augment_tasks_stream,
469:                     group,
470:                     entry_id,
471:                 )
472:                 if deliveries is not None and deliveries >= max_retries:
473:                     # Give up on this label so the overall job can complete.
474:                     stream_ack(cfg.augment_tasks_stream, group, entry_id)
475:                     with done_lock:
476:                         label_done = str(fields.get("label") or "").strip()
477:                         if label_done:
478:                             done_labels.add(label_done)
479:     threads = []
480:     max_workers = min(cfg.augment_max_parallel_labels, max(1, expected_labels))
481:     for i in range(max_workers):
482:         t = threading.Thread(target=_worker, args=(i,), daemon=True)
483:         threads.append(t)
484:         t.start()
485:     for t in threads:
486:         t.join()
487: def merge_augment_outputs(
488:     cfg: RefineConfig,
489:     *,
490:     run_id: str,
491:     train_df: pd.DataFrame,
492: ) -> pd.DataFrame:
493:     labels_dir = cfg.augment_dir(run_id) / "labels"
494:     if not labels_dir.exists():
495:         return train_df
496:     example_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
497:     for p in sorted(labels_dir.glob("proposed_examples.label_*.csv")):
498:         with open(p, encoding="utf-8", newline="") as f:
499:             example_rows.extend(list(csv.DictReader(f)))
500:     refs_by_label: dict[str, list[set[str]]] = defaultdict(list)
501:     for _, row in train_df.iterrows():
502:         lab = str(row.get("label") or "").strip()
503:         tx = str(row.get("text") or "").strip()
504:         if lab in LABELS and tx:
505:             refs_by_label[lab].append(_char_trigrams(tx))
506:     # Dedupe against existing texts; keep first occurrence.
507:     existing = set(train_df["text"].astype(str).str.strip())
508:     merged: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
509:     invalid_rows_count = 0
510:     duplicate_existing_count = 0
511:     fuzzy_duplicate_count = 0
512:     for r in example_rows:
513:         text = str(r.get("text") or "").strip()
514:         label = str(r.get("label") or "").strip()
515:         if not text or label not in LABELS:
516:             invalid_rows_count += 1
517:             continue
518:         if text in existing:
519:             duplicate_existing_count += 1
520:             continue
521:         refs = refs_by_label.get(label, [])
522:         if _is_fuzzy_duplicate(text, refs):
523:             fuzzy_duplicate_count += 1
524:             continue
525:         existing.add(text)
526:         merged.append(
527:             {
528:                 "text": text,
529:                 "label": label,
530:                 "source_pattern": r.get("source_pattern") or "augmentation",
531:             }
532:         )
533:         refs_by_label[label].append(_char_trigrams(text))
534:     _write_json(
535:         cfg.augment_dir(run_id) / "merge_augment.validation.json",
536:         {
537:             "input_rows_count": int(len(example_rows)),
538:             "accepted_rows_count": int(len(merged)),
539:             "invalid_rows_count": int(invalid_rows_count),
540:             "duplicate_existing_count": int(duplicate_existing_count),
541:             "fuzzy_duplicate_count": int(fuzzy_duplicate_count),
542:         },
543:     )
544:     _write_csv(
545:         cfg.augment_merged_csv(run_id), merged, fieldnames=["text", "label", "source_pattern"]
546:     )
547:     result = pd.concat(
548:         [train_df, pd.DataFrame([{"text": r["text"], "label": r["label"]} for r in merged])],
549:         ignore_index=True,
550:     )
551:     cfg.augment_dir(run_id).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
552:     result.to_csv(cfg.augment_candidate_csv(run_id), index=False)
553:     return result
554: def run_augment_phase(
555:     cfg: RefineConfig,
556:     *,
557:     model_artifacts_path: str,
558:     promote_target_path: str,
559:     run_id: str,
560:     progress: Callable[[dict[str, Any]], None] | None = None,
561: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
562:     cfg.ensure_run_dir(run_id)
563:     cfg.augment_dir(run_id).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
564:     metrics_before = _read_metrics(model_artifacts_path)
565:     cfg.metrics_before_path(run_id).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
566:     with open(cfg.metrics_before_path(run_id), "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
567:         json.dump(metrics_before, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
568:     labels = _labels_to_augment(metrics_before)
569:     train_df = _read_train_csv(promote_target_path)
570:     label_counts = _compute_augment_counts(train_df, labels, cfg.augment_n_per_label)
571:     # Create the consumer group BEFORE enqueueing so workers don't miss entries.
572:     stream_group_create(
573:         cfg.augment_tasks_stream, f"{cfg.augment_consumer_group}:{run_id}"
574:     )
575:     total = enqueue_augment_tasks(
576:         cfg, run_id=run_id, labels=labels, label_counts=label_counts
577:     )
578:     start_evt = {
579:         "status": "progress",
580:         "phase": "augment",
581:         "detail": f"Enqueued {total} augmentation label tasks",
582:         "labels": labels,
583:         "label_counts": label_counts,
584:     }
585:     if progress:
586:         progress(start_evt)
587:     publish_event(cfg.events_channel(run_id), start_evt)
588:     run_augment_workers(
589:         cfg,
590:         run_id=run_id,
591:         expected_labels=total,
592:         train_df=train_df,
593:         progress=progress,
594:     )
595:     candidate_df = merge_augment_outputs(cfg, run_id=run_id, train_df=train_df)
596:     compose_working_dir = os.environ.get("COMPOSE_WORKING_DIR", ".")
597:     metrics_after = train_candidate(
598:         compose_working_dir=compose_working_dir,
599:         candidate_csv=cfg.augment_candidate_csv(run_id),
600:         metrics_out=cfg.augment_candidate_metrics_json(run_id),
601:         timeout_seconds=int(os.environ.get("RUN_TRAIN_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "3600")),
602:     )
603:     return {
604:         "run_id": run_id,
605:         "labels": labels,
606:         "train_augment_candidate_rows": int(len(candidate_df)),
607:         "metrics_before": metrics_before,
608:         "metrics_after": metrics_after,
609:         "proposed_examples": _read_csv_rows(cfg.augment_merged_csv(run_id)),
610:     }
````

## File: README.md
````markdown
  1: # Local AI Microservice Mesh
  2: 
  3: A locally runnable, multi-container microservice system where an AI classifier
  4: selects the backend service for each incoming request. The browser UI displays
  5: the exact path taken (application-level tracing) per action.
  6: 
  7: ## Overview
  8: 
  9: - **Gateway** serves the static UI and main API (`POST /api/request`)
 10: - **AI Router** classifies request text and returns route, confidence, and
 11:   explanation
 12: - **Backends** (search, image, ops) simulate domain-specific responses
 13: - **Trainer** (optional profile) trains the classifier and writes the model
 14:   artifact
 15: - **Refiner** (optional profile) improves the dataset via local LLM (Ollama);
 16:   promotes when retrained accuracy meets the configured tolerance versus the
 17:   previous model
 18: - **Redis** and **Training API** (refine profile) provide job state and HTTP
 19:   endpoints for the Train/Refine UI; see `services/training-api/README.md`
 20:   for env (REDIS_URL or REDIS_HOST/REDIS_PORT). Both run on the internal
 21:   network only (no public ports); the gateway proxies to the training-api.
 22:   **Promote** can be run from the UI or via CLI: `./scripts/promote.sh` or
 23:   `demo.sh promote`. **Refine** requires Ollama (start with refine profile).
 24: 
 25: All services run in Python (FastAPI) with Docker Compose. No external
 26: observability stack is required; tracing is application-level.
 27: 
 28: ## Quick Start
 29: 
 30: **Prerequisites:** Docker and Docker Compose.
 31: 
 32: ```bash
 33: # Generate env file for selected environment (defaults to ENV=dev from PROJECT_CONFIG.yaml)
 34: python scripts/generate_env.py
 35: 
 36: # Build and start
 37: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml up --build -d
 38: 
 39: # Browser UI: http://localhost:8000
 40: # Health: http://localhost:8000/health
 41: ```
 42: 
 43: Alternatively, `./scripts/demo.sh run` generates env and starts the stack
 44: automatically instead of the steps above.
 45: 
 46: **Train the model** (optional, uses pre-built model by default):
 47: 
 48: ```bash
 49: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml --profile train run --rm trainer
 50: ```
 51: 
 52: **Refine the dataset** (optional, after training; requires Ollama):
 53: 
 54: Use the Refine UI at `http://localhost:8000/refine` to run relabeling and
 55: augmentation independently, then promote. Or use the CLI:
 56: 
 57: ```bash
 58: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml --profile refine run --rm training-api relabel
 59: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml --profile refine run --rm training-api augment
 60: ./scripts/promote.sh
 61: ```
 62: 
 63: See [docs/auxiliary/demo/DEMO.md](docs/auxiliary/demo/DEMO.md) for full runbook,
 64: scaling, and failure demos.
 65: 
 66: ## Compose Files
 67: 
 68: - **Base:** `compose/docker-compose.yaml` defines the full stack (gateway,
 69:   ai_router, backends, optional trainer/refiner/redis/training-api via
 70:   profiles). Services run from built images with default commands.
 71: - **Dev overlay:** `compose/docker-compose.dev.yaml` overrides the five app
 72:   services with bind-mounted source and `uvicorn --reload` so code changes
 73:   apply without rebuilding. Use both files together:
 74: 
 75: ```bash
 76: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml -f compose/docker-compose.dev.yaml up --build
 77: ```
 78: 
 79: Or use `./scripts/demo.sh run --dev`. See
 80: [docs/auxiliary/architecture/TECHNICAL.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/TECHNICAL.md)
 81: (Section 16.2) for details.
 82: 
 83: ## Documentation
 84: 
 85: | Document | Description |
 86: | --- | --- |
 87: | [docs/auxiliary/requirements/PRD.md](docs/auxiliary/requirements/PRD.md) | Product requirements and functional specs |
 88: | [docs/auxiliary/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md) | System design, components, request flow |
 89: | [docs/auxiliary/architecture/TECHNICAL.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/TECHNICAL.md) | Training pipeline, routing policy, model details |
 90: | [docs/auxiliary/architecture/CONFIGURATION.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/CONFIGURATION.md) | Centralized configuration and env generation |
 91: | [docs/auxiliary/demo/DEMO.md](docs/auxiliary/demo/DEMO.md) | Build, run, stop, scaling, and failure demos |
 92: | [docs/auxiliary/requirements/ACCEPTANCE.md](docs/auxiliary/requirements/ACCEPTANCE.md) | Acceptance criteria and verification |
 93: | [docs/auxiliary/troubleshooting/DEBUG.md](docs/auxiliary/troubleshooting/DEBUG.md) | Debug runbook and common failures |
 94: | [docs/auxiliary/planning/PROJECT_PLAN.md](docs/auxiliary/planning/PROJECT_PLAN.md) | Project overview and technical requirements |
 95: | [docs/auxiliary/planning/TASKS.md](docs/auxiliary/planning/TASKS.md) | Actionable implementation tasks |
 96: | [docs/auxiliary/planning/PERFORMANCE_IMPROVEMENTS.md](docs/auxiliary/planning/PERFORMANCE_IMPROVEMENTS.md) | Refinement performance tuning |
 97: | [docs/auxiliary/planning/AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md](docs/auxiliary/planning/AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md) | Augment quality gates and promotion tolerance |
 98: | [docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PLAN.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PLAN.md) | Refiner conceptual overview |
 99: | [docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_TECHNICAL.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_TECHNICAL.md) | Refiner technical specification |
100: | [docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_FLOW.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_FLOW.md) | Refiner end-to-end flow |
101: | [docs/auxiliary/planning/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PLAN.md](docs/auxiliary/planning/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PLAN.md) | Train and Refine GUI plan |
102: | [docs/auxiliary/architecture/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH.md) | Train and Refine GUI technical spec |
103: | [docs/auxiliary/requirements/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PRD.md](docs/auxiliary/requirements/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_PRD.md) | Train and Refine GUI requirements |
104: 
105: ## Testing
106: 
107: **Prerequisites:** Python 3.10+, pytest, and service dependencies
108: (install per-service `requirements.txt`).
109: 
110: ```bash
111: # Run all tests
112: pytest
113: 
114: # Run tests for a specific service
115: pytest services/gateway/tests/ -v
116: pytest services/ai_router/tests/ -v
117: pytest services/trainer/tests/ -v
118: pytest services/training-api/tests/ -v
119: 
120: # Run backend service tests
121: pytest services/search_service/tests/ services/image_service/tests/ services/ops_service/tests/ -v
122: 
123: # Run compose validation tests
124: pytest compose/tests/ -v
125: 
126: # Run integration and e2e tests
127: pytest tests/ -v
128: 
129: # Run by marker
130: pytest -m unit
131: pytest -m integration
132: pytest -m e2e
133: 
134: # With coverage
135: pytest --cov --cov-report=html
136: ```
137: 
138: The demo script also runs gateway and ai_router tests:
139: 
140: ```bash
141: ./scripts/demo.sh test
142: ```
143: 
144: ## Project Structure
145: 
146: ```text
147: compose/          # Docker Compose definitions
148: config/           # Authoritative PROJECT_CONFIG.yaml (see CONFIGURATION.md)
149: env/              # Generated .env.<env> files for Compose
150: services/         # Microservices (gateway, ai_router, search_service, etc.)
151: scripts/          # Demo and load-test scripts
152: docs/             # Project documentation (auxiliary/ for detailed docs)
153: ```
````

## File: docs/auxiliary/architecture/TECHNICAL.md
````markdown
   1: # TECHNICAL BREAKDOWN
   2: 
   3: Technical specification for the AI router: training pipeline, artifacts,
   4: routing policy, and explanation tokens. Training code lives in
   5: `services/trainer/train.py`; review for any additions or changes.
   6: 
   7: <!-- CROSS-REF: Each ## section includes a "TASKS:" marker listing
   8:      task IDs from TASKS.md that it implements. Search for "TASKS:"
   9:      to jump between sections and tasks. Section 21 has the full
  10:      bidirectional mapping. In TASKS.md, the "Tech spec" column
  11:      points back to sections here using "TECH-{N}" format. -->
  12: 
  13: ## 1. Dependencies (ai-router runtime)
  14: <!-- TASKS: 1.4, 2.2 -->
  15: 
  16: ```text
  17: fastapi==0.115.6
  18: uvicorn[standard]==0.32.1
  19: joblib==1.4.2
  20: ```
  21: 
  22: ## 2. Training Data and Commands
  23: <!-- TASKS: 2.1, 2.2 -->
  24: 
  25: ### Data format
  26: 
  27: - **File:** `train.csv` with columns `text` and `label`.
  28: - **Labels:** `search`, `image`, `ops`, `unknown` (must match exactly; order in
  29:   code is `["search", "image", "ops", "unknown"]`).
  30: - **Balance:** Aim for at least ~160 examples per label for stable training
  31:   (TASKS 2.1). Run a quick count per label; top up any class below that before
  32:   relying on metrics.
  33: 
  34: ### Running training
  35: 
  36: From `services/trainer/`:
  37: 
  38: ```bash
  39: python train.py --data ./train.csv --out ./model.joblib --metrics ./metrics.json
  40: ```
  41: 
  42: Optional: export misclassified validation samples:
  43: 
  44: ```bash
  45: python train.py --data ./train.csv --out ./model.joblib --metrics ./metrics.json --misclassified ./misclassified.csv
  46: ```
  47: 
  48: Use `--no-misclassified` to disable misclassified export.
  49: 
  50: ## 3. Training Outputs: What They Are and What You Do
  51: <!-- TASKS: 2.2, 2.3, 2.4 -->
  52: 
  53: ### 3.1 model.joblib
  54: 
  55: **What it is:** The trained artifact the ai-router service loads at runtime.
  56: Contains: TF-IDF vectorizer, LogisticRegression model, label list, and
  57: training metadata.
  58: 
  59: **What you do:**
  60: 
  61: 1. Keep it at `services/ai_router/model/model.joblib` (or load from path).
  62: 2. In ai-router startup: `artifact = joblib.load("model.joblib")`.
  63: 3. In `/classify`: vectorize text, call `predict_proba`, then apply your
  64:    threshold/margin policy to choose route or unknown.
  65: 
  66: This is the model you ship.
  67: 
  68: ### 3.2 metrics.json
  69: 
  70: **What it is:** Evaluation metrics on the held-out validation set: accuracy,
  71: per-class precision/recall/F1, and confusion matrix.
  72: 
  73: **What you do:** Use it to sanity-check the model. Ensure `unknown` recall is
  74: reasonable (otherwise 404 will rarely happen), and that `ops` is not often
  75: confused with `search`. If a class is weak, add more examples for that class.
  76: 
  77: ### 3.3 misclassified.csv (optional)
  78: 
  79: **What it is:** Rows from the validation set where `pred_label != true_label`.
  80: Columns: `text`, `true_label`, `pred_label`, `pred_confidence`, `probs_json`.
  81: 
  82: **What you do:** Use it to improve the dataset and tune routing policy.
  83: 
  84: **Workflow:**
  85: 
  86: 1. Open `misclassified.csv`.
  87: 2. For each row, classify the cause and act:
  88: 
  89:    - **Bad label:** Fix or remove the row in `train.csv`.
  90:    - **Ambiguous text:** Add clearer examples for both labels, or move some to
  91:      `unknown` for strict routing.
  92:    - **Missing pattern:** Add 5–20 short, varied examples with that wording.
  93:    - **Too confident but wrong (e.g. confidence > 0.75):** Add counterexamples
  94:      and distinctive tokens for the intended label.
  95:    - **Low confidence and wrong (e.g. < 0.55):** Rely on thresholding to send
  96:      to `unknown`; consider raising threshold or adding a margin rule.
  97: 
  98: 3. Retrain.
  99: 4. Repeat until misclassifications are acceptable.
 100: 
 101: Use misclassifications to tune gateway policy: if many wrong routes occur at
 102: 0.50–0.65 confidence, raise `T_route`; if two classes are often close, add
 103: `T_margin`.
 104: 
 105: **Minimum practical usage:** Keep `model.joblib`, glance at `metrics.json`, and
 106: optionally ignore `misclassified.csv`. For a solid router, use
 107: `misclassified.csv` to refine data and thresholds. For automated refinement
 108: via a local LLM, see [REFINER_PLAN.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PLAN.md)
 109: and [REFINER_TECHNICAL.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_TECHNICAL.md). For
 110: train/refine/promote (training-api), see
 111: [TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH.md](TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH.md).
 112: 
 113: ## 4. Iteration Cycle (Data → Retrain → Evaluate → Policy)
 114: <!-- TASKS: 6.11 -->
 115: 
 116: Refining data without retraining has no effect; the model is static.
 117: 
 118: **Correct loop:**
 119: 
 120: 1. **Edit the dataset:** Fix wrong labels, add missing patterns, move ambiguous
 121:    samples to `unknown`, remove misleading examples.
 122: 2. **Rerun training:** Run `train.py` again; new `model.joblib`, updated
 123:    metrics and misclassifications.
 124: 3. **Evaluate:** Check `metrics.json`, review `misclassified.csv`, verify
 125:    confidence distributions.
 126: 4. **Tune policy:** Adjust `T_route` and optional `T_margin` in the gateway
 127:    only; do not change gateway logic, only thresholds.
 128: 5. **Repeat** until high-confidence misroutes are rare and most uncertainty goes
 129:    to `unknown`.
 130: 
 131: **What changes between runs:** Model weights, decision boundaries, probability
 132: calibration, and unknown behavior. Nothing else in the system needs to change.
 133: 
 134: **Practical workflow:** Keep `train.csv` in git; do not commit `model.joblib`
 135: until satisfied; then commit the final artifact. Docker build uses that
 136: artifact. For automated refinement (refiner + promote), see
 137: [REFINER_FLOW.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_FLOW.md).
 138: 
 139: ## 5. Threshold and Margin: Two Different Loops
 140: <!-- TASKS: 4.3, 8.6 -->
 141: 
 142: ### 5.1 Rerunning training (data-driven refinement)
 143: 
 144: This changes the **model**. Do it when you fix labels, add/remove examples,
 145: improve coverage, or adjust class balance (especially `unknown`). Effect:
 146: weights and decision boundaries change, probabilities recalibrate. Mechanism:
 147: edit `train.csv`, run trainer, produce new `model.joblib`, restart ai-router.
 148: 
 149: ### 5.2 Threshold and margin tuning (policy refinement)
 150: 
 151: This does **not** retrain. It changes how the gateway **interprets**
 152: probabilities. Use when the model is broadly correct but too aggressive, or
 153: you want fewer wrong routes and more `unknown`. Parameters: `T_route` (min
 154: confidence to route), `T_margin` (min gap between top-1 and top-2). Effect:
 155: same probabilities, different routing decisions, more or fewer 404s.
 156: 
 157: **Order of operations:** (1) Train; (2) inspect metrics and misclassified;
 158: (3) fix data; (4) retrain; (5) repeat until misclassifications are mostly
