 159: low-confidence; (6) then tune thresholds/margin; (7) restart ai-router after
 160: changes. Do not tune thresholds to compensate for bad data; do not retrain
 161: only to change routing strictness.
 162: 
 163: **Summary:** Training improves what the model knows; thresholds control how
 164: willing the system is to act on that knowledge.
 165: 
 166: ## 6. Where and How to Implement Thresholds
 167: <!-- TASKS: 4.3, 8.6 -->
 168: 
 169: **Where:** In the **gateway**, not in the model or backends. The gateway is the
 170: policy enforcement point.
 171: 
 172: **What to implement:**
 173: 
 174: - `T_ROUTE`: minimum confidence to route to a backend.
 175: - `T_MARGIN`: minimum gap between top-1 and top-2 among non-unknown routes
 176:   (optional but recommended).
 177: 
 178: Routing uses only non-unknown routes for "guessing"; otherwise fall back to
 179: unknown (404).
 180: 
 181: **Implementation steps:**
 182: 
 183: 1. **Env vars (in compose):** `T_ROUTE=0.60`, `T_MARGIN=0.10`. Parse as
 184:    floats in gateway startup and validate in [0.0, 1.0]. Code fallback defaults
 185:    are `T_ROUTE=0.55` and `T_MARGIN=0.10`.
 186: 2. **ai-router response:** Gateway must receive full probability distribution
 187:    (or at least best_route among non-unknown, `p_best`, `p_second`, `p_unknown`).
 188:    Best practice: return full probability map from ai-router.
 189: 3. **Policy logic:**
 190: 
 191:    - Best among `{search, image, ops}` → `best_route`, `p_best`.
 192:    - Second-highest → `p_second`.
 193:    - If `p_best >= T_ROUTE` and `(p_best - p_second) >= T_MARGIN`: route to
 194:      `best_route`.
 195:    - Else: route = `unknown`, return 404.
 196: 
 197: 4. **Trace:** Add metadata for unknown: e.g. `policy: low_confidence` or
 198:    `policy: low_margin`.
 199: 5. **Tuning:** Use `misclassified.csv` and real prompts: raise `T_ROUTE` if
 200:    wrong routes at 0.55–0.65; raise `T_MARGIN` if two routes flip; lower
 201:    thresholds if too many valid prompts become unknown. Restart gateway after
 202:    env changes; no retraining needed.
 203: 
 204: **Note:** Threshold and margin checks are implemented in
 205: `services/gateway/app/main.py`. `train.py` and ai-router produce
 206: probabilities; the gateway applies decision policy with `T_ROUTE` and
 207: `T_MARGIN` and returns 404 for unknown/low-confidence/low-margin cases.
 208: 
 209: **Example gateway policy (pseudocode):**
 210: 
 211: ```python
 212: T_ROUTE = float(os.getenv("T_ROUTE", 0.55))
 213: T_MARGIN = float(os.getenv("T_MARGIN", 0.10))
 214: 
 215: non_unknown = {k: v for k, v in probs.items() if k != "unknown"}
 216: sorted_routes = sorted(non_unknown.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
 217: best_route, p_best = sorted_routes[0]
 218: p_second = sorted_routes[1][1]
 219: 
 220: if p_best >= T_ROUTE and (p_best - p_second) >= T_MARGIN:
 221:     route = best_route
 222: else:
 223:     route = "unknown"
 224: ```
 225: 
 226: If `route == "unknown"`: do not proxy; return HTTP 404 with trace and policy
 227: reason.
 228: 
 229: ## 7. Trainer Service
 230: <!-- TASKS: 6.7, 6.8, 6.9, 6.10, 6.11 -->
 231: 
 232: Dedicated one-shot container for retraining the model, separate from
 233: ai-router so inference stays small and stable. The trainer runs on demand,
 234: writes `model.joblib` to a shared volume, then exits. Prefer this over a
 235: long-running `POST /train` API: one-shot is simpler, avoids memory leaks,
 236: and fits the data-then-retrain loop described in Section 4.
 237: 
 238: ### 7.1 Directory Structure
 239: 
 240: ```text
 241: services/trainer/
 242:   Dockerfile
 243:   requirements.txt
 244:   train.py
 245:   train.csv
 246: ```
 247: 
 248: The trainer uses `train.py` (baked into the image) and `train.csv`
 249: (mounted from the host at runtime). Both live in `services/trainer/`.
 250: Edits to `train.csv` take effect without rebuilding the trainer image.
 251: 
 252: ### 7.2 Trainer Dockerfile
 253: 
 254: Single-stage image; no runtime server, just Python with training deps.
 255: 
 256: ```dockerfile
 257: ARG PY_IMAGE=python:3.12-slim-bookworm
 258: FROM ${PY_IMAGE}
 259: WORKDIR /train
 260: 
 261: COPY requirements.txt .
 262: RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
 263: 
 264: COPY train.py .
 265: 
 266: ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
 267: ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
 268: 
 269: CMD ["python", "train.py", \
 270:     "--data", "./train.csv", \
 271:     "--out", "/model/model.joblib", \
 272:     "--metrics", "/model/metrics.json", \
 273:     "--misclassified", "/model/misclassified.csv"]
 274: ```
 275: 
 276: `requirements.txt` for the trainer (training deps only, no FastAPI):
 277: 
 278: ```text
 279: scikit-learn==1.5.2
 280: numpy==2.1.3
 281: joblib==1.4.2
 282: ```
 283: 
 284: The image contains everything needed to train. At runtime it writes
 285: artifacts (`model.joblib`, `metrics.json`, `misclassified.csv`) into
 286: `/model` (the shared volume mount point).
 287: 
 288: ### 7.3 Compose Configuration
 289: 
 290: Add to `compose/docker-compose.yaml`:
 291: 
 292: ```yaml
 293: volumes:
 294:   model_artifacts:
 295: 
 296: services:
 297:   trainer:
 298:     build:
 299:       context: ../services/trainer
 300:       dockerfile: Dockerfile
 301:     profiles:
 302:       - train
 303:     volumes:
 304:       - ../services/trainer/train.csv:/train/train.csv:ro
 305:       - model_artifacts:/model
 306:     environment:
 307:       <<: *common-env
 308:     logging:
 309:       <<: *common-logging
 310: ```
 311: 
 312: Update `ai_router` to mount the shared volume so it can load the
 313: trainer-produced artifact:
 314: 
 315: ```yaml
 316:   ai_router:
 317:     # ... existing config ...
 318:     volumes:
 319:       - model_artifacts:/model:ro
 320:     environment:
 321:       <<: *common-env
 322:       MODEL_PATH: /model/model.joblib
 323: ```
 324: 
 325: Key points:
 326: 
 327: - **Profile `train`:** The trainer only runs when explicitly requested;
 328:   it is excluded from `docker compose up` by default.
 329: - **Named volume `model_artifacts`:** Trainer writes, ai-router reads.
 330:   Survives container restarts; artifacts persist until the volume is
 331:   removed.
 332: - **Code and data:** `train.py` is baked into the image; `train.csv` is
 333:   mounted from the host at runtime. To change training data, edit
 334:   `services/trainer/train.csv` and run the trainer again (no rebuild).
 335: 
 336: ### 7.4 Running the Trainer
 337: 
 338: **Trigger training:**
 339: 
 340: ```bash
 341: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml \
 342:   --profile train run --rm trainer
 343: ```
 344: 
 345: The container runs `train.py`, writes three files into the
 346: `model_artifacts` volume (`model.joblib`, `metrics.json`,
 347: `misclassified.csv`), prints accuracy, and exits.
 348: 
 349: **Reload the model in ai-router:**
 350: 
 351: ```bash
 352: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml restart ai_router
 353: ```
 354: 
 355: ai-router re-reads `MODEL_PATH` on startup and begins serving the new
 356: model. No image rebuild is needed; only a container restart.
 357: 
 358: ### 7.5 ai-router Model Loading
 359: 
 360: At startup, ai-router loads the model from the path given by the
 361: `MODEL_PATH` env var (default: built-in artifact from the Docker build
 362: stage). If `MODEL_PATH` is set and the file exists, load from there;
 363: otherwise fall back to the build-time artifact. Fail fast if neither exists.
 364: 
 365: ```python
 366: import os
 367: import joblib
 368: 
 369: MODEL_PATH = os.getenv("MODEL_PATH", "/app/model/model.joblib")
 370: 
 371: def load_model():
 372:     if not os.path.exists(MODEL_PATH):
 373:         raise RuntimeError(f"Model not found: {MODEL_PATH}")
 374:     artifact = joblib.load(MODEL_PATH)
 375:     return artifact["vectorizer"], artifact["model"], artifact["labels"]
 376: ```
 377: 
 378: Call `load_model()` once in the FastAPI `lifespan` or module-level init.
 379: Store the vectorizer, model, and labels in app state.
 380: 
 381: ### 7.6 Optional: Hot-Reload Without Restart
 382: 
 383: For faster iteration, add a `POST /reload-model` endpoint to ai-router
 384: instead of restarting the container. This is optional; restart is the
 385: simplest correct approach.
 386: 
 387: ```python
 388: import threading
 389: 
 390: _model_lock = threading.Lock()
 391: 
 392: @app.post("/reload-model")
 393: def reload_model():
 394:     with _model_lock:
 395:         new = joblib.load(MODEL_PATH)
 396:         app.state.vectorizer = new["vectorizer"]
 397:         app.state.model = new["model"]
 398:         app.state.labels = new["labels"]
 399:     return {"status": "reloaded", "path": MODEL_PATH}
 400: ```
 401: 
 402: Guard with a lock so in-flight `/classify` calls finish with the old model
 403: before the swap. Do not expose this endpoint externally; it is an internal
 404: admin action. If reload is not needed, skip this and use `restart`.
 405: 
 406: ### 7.7 Full Retrain-and-Reload Workflow
 407: 
 408: Complete loop integrating Sections 4, 7.4, and 7.5:
 409: 
 410: 1. Edit `services/trainer/train.csv` on the host.
 411: 2. Run the trainer (no rebuild needed):
 412: 
 413:    ```bash
 414:    docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml \
 415:      --profile train run --rm trainer
 416:    ```
 417: 
 418: 3. Inspect outputs on the shared volume (optional):
 419: 
 420:    ```bash
 421:    docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml \
 422:      --profile train run --rm --entrypoint cat trainer /model/metrics.json
 423:    ```
 424: 
 425: 4. Reload ai-router:
 426: 
 427:    ```bash
 428:    docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml restart ai_router
 429:    ```
 430: 
 431: 5. Test with a curl request to verify the new model:
 432: 
 433:    ```bash
 434:    curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/request \
 435:      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
 436:      -d '{"text": "compare nginx ingress vs traefik"}' | python -m json.tool
 437:    ```
 438: 
 439: 6. Repeat until `metrics.json` accuracy and `misclassified.csv` errors are
 440:    acceptable, then tune thresholds as described in Section 5.2.
 441: 
 442: ### 7.8 When to Use the Trainer vs Local Training
 443: 
 444: | Scenario | Use |
 445: | ---- | ----- |
 446: | First build or CI/CD pipeline | Trainer service, then rebuild ai_router image |
 447: | Iterating on `train.csv` locally | Trainer service (no rebuild) |
 448: | Shipping a final model to production | Trainer service, then rebuild ai_router image |
 449: | Quick experiment with hyperparams | Trainer service with CLI overrides |
 450: 
 451: The trainer produces `model.joblib` which can be used two ways:
 452: (1) copy to `services/ai_router/model/` and rebuild the ai_router
 453: image (artifact baked in), or (2) write to the shared volume and
 454: restart ai_router (no rebuild). Choose based on whether you want the
 455: artifact in the image or loaded from a volume.
 456: 
 457: **Refiner workflow:** After training, run the refiner to analyze
 458: `misclassified.csv` and produce `train_candidate.csv`; then run
 459: `scripts/promote.sh` to promote when accuracy is within tolerance of the
 460: baseline. See [REFINER_FLOW.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_FLOW.md),
 461: [AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md](../planning/AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md),
 462: and [REFINER_TECHNICAL.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_TECHNICAL.md).
 463: 
 464: ## 8. Explanation Token Extraction
 465: <!-- TASKS: 2.5 -->
 466: 
 467: **What it means:** When the model routes a request, return a short
 468: human-readable reason such as `["kubectl", "pod", "crashloop"]`: the TF-IDF
 469: features (tokens or n-grams) that most strongly pushed the classifier toward
 470: the predicted label. This is deterministic "top contributing features" from
 471: model weights, not full interpretability.
 472: 
 473: **Where:** In the **ai-router service**, inside `/classify`. The ai-router has
 474: the vectorizer, vocabulary, LogisticRegression coefficients, and vectorized
 475: input; the gateway should stay policy and proxy only.
 476: 
 477: **How (concept):** For LogisticRegression, per-class score is
 478: `score_c = intercept_c + sum_j (x_j * coef_cj)`. For explanation, use
 479: `contrib_j = x_j * coef_predicted_class_j`, then sort by contribution and map
 480: feature indices to token strings via `vectorizer.get_feature_names_out()`;
 481: return top N (e.g. 6).
 482: 
 483: **Drop-in helper (ai-router, `services/ai_router/app/main.py`):**
 484: 
 485: ```python
 486: from __future__ import annotations
 487: 
 488: from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
 489: import numpy as np
 490: 
 491: def top_contributing_tokens(
 492:     text: str,
 493:     vectorizer,
 494:     model,
 495:     labels: List[str],
 496:     top_n: int = 6,
 497: ) -> Tuple[str, float, Dict[str, float], List[str]]:
 498:     """
 499:     Returns:
 500:       predicted_label, confidence, probs_map, top_tokens
 501:     """
 502:     X = vectorizer.transform([text])
 503: 
 504:     probs = model.predict_proba(X)[0]
 505:     pred_idx = int(np.argmax(probs))
 506:     pred_label = labels[pred_idx]
 507:     confidence = float(probs[pred_idx])
 508:     probs_map = {labels[i]: float(probs[i]) for i in range(len(labels))}
 509: 
 510:     feature_names = vectorizer.get_feature_names_out()
 511:     class_coef = model.coef_[pred_idx]
 512: 
 513:     indices = X.indices
 514:     values = X.data
 515: 
 516:     if indices.size == 0:
 517:         return pred_label, confidence, probs_map, []
 518: 
 519:     contrib = values * class_coef[indices]
 520:     order = np.argsort(contrib)[::-1]
 521: 
 522:     top_tokens: List[str] = []
 523:     for i in order[:top_n]:
 524:         top_tokens.append(str(feature_names[indices[i]]))
 525: 
 526:     return pred_label, confidence, probs_map, top_tokens
 527: ```
 528: 
 529: Call this in `/classify` and return probabilities, explanation tokens, and
 530: optionally a short explanation string. With bigrams (`ngram_range=(1,2)`),
 531: tokens may be phrases like `"crashloop backoff"`. Add the result to trace
 532: metadata (e.g. `meta: {route, confidence, top_tokens}`) and show in the UI.
 533: 
 534: ## 9. Recommended Starting Policy and Cross-Reference
 535: <!-- TASKS: 4.3, 8.1 -->
 536: 
 537: **Starting values:** `T_route = 0.60`, `T_margin = 0.10`. Confident →
 538: route to backend; not confident or ambiguous → `unknown` → 404.
 539: 
 540: **Alignment with TASKS.md:**
 541: 
 542: - **Batch 2 (AI router):** train.csv format and labels (Section 2), train.py
 543:   and commands (Section 2), model.joblib and metrics (Section 3), build-time
 544:   artifact and ai-router `/classify` with route, confidence, explanation
 545:   (Sections 3.1, 6, 8).
 546: - **Batch 4 (Gateway):** Threshold and margin in gateway (Section 6), env
 547:   vars, policy logic, 404 for unknown, trace and timings (Section 6).
 548: - **Batch 6/7:** Trainer service Dockerfile, Compose config, shared volume,
 549:   retrain-and-reload workflow, hot-reload option (Section 7).
 550: 
 551: Training code and artifact format are correct; threshold and explanation logic
 552: must be implemented in gateway and ai-router respectively as described above.
 553: 
 554: ## 10. Service Communication
 555: <!-- TASKS: 4.2, 4.4 -->
 556: 
 557: ### 10.1 Internal HTTP Client
 558: 
 559: The gateway makes async HTTP calls to ai-router and backends using
 560: `httpx.AsyncClient`. Add `httpx` to the gateway `requirements.txt`:
 561: 
 562: ```text
 563: httpx>=0.27.0
 564: ```
 565: 
 566: Create a single `AsyncClient` in the FastAPI `lifespan` so connections
 567: are pooled and the client is cleaned up on shutdown:
 568: 
 569: ```python
 570: from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
 571: import httpx
 572: 
 573: @asynccontextmanager
 574: async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
 575:     app.state.http = httpx.AsyncClient(
 576:         timeout=httpx.Timeout(30.0),
 577:         limits=httpx.Limits(
 578:             max_connections=100,
 579:             max_keepalive_connections=0,
 580:         ),
 581:     )
 582:     yield
 583:     await app.state.http.aclose()
 584: 
 585: app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)
 586: ```
 587: 
 588: Setting `max_keepalive_connections=0` forces fresh DNS resolution per
 589: request, which is needed for round-robin distribution across scaled
 590: replicas (Section 18). If latency matters more than even distribution,
 591: keep the default and accept slightly uneven load.
 592: 
 593: ### 10.2 Service URL Resolution
 594: 
 595: Inside Compose, services resolve by name on the default bridge network.
 596: The gateway reads backend URLs from environment variables with defaults
 597: matching Compose service names:
 598: 
 599: ```python
 600: import os
 601: 
 602: AI_ROUTER_URL = os.getenv("AI_ROUTER_URL", "http://ai_router:8000")
 603: ROUTE_MAP = {
 604:     "search": os.getenv(
 605:         "SEARCH_SERVICE_URL", "http://search_service:8000"
 606:     ),
 607:     "image": os.getenv(
 608:         "IMAGE_SERVICE_URL", "http://image_service:8000"
 609:     ),
 610:     "ops": os.getenv(
 611:         "OPS_SERVICE_URL", "http://ops_service:8000"
 612:     ),
 613: }
 614: ```
 615: 
 616: Labels from the model (`search`, `image`, `ops`) map directly to these
 617: keys. If a label is not in `ROUTE_MAP` or is `unknown`, the gateway
 618: does not proxy.
 619: 
 620: ### 10.3 Timeout Configuration
 621: 
 622: Read `REQUEST_TIMEOUT` from the environment (set by `x-common-env` in
 623: Compose). Use it to configure the `httpx` client timeout. The default
 624: is 30 seconds:
 625: 
 626: ```python
 627: TIMEOUT = float(os.getenv("REQUEST_TIMEOUT", "30"))
 628: ```
 629: 
 630: ## 11. Request ID and Trace Protocol
 631: <!-- TASKS: 3.1, 3.5, 4.1, 4.5 -->
 632: 
 633: ### 11.1 Request ID Generation and Propagation
 634: 
 635: The gateway generates a UUID v4 if the client does not supply one.
 636: Propagate it to downstream services via both the JSON body
 637: (`request_id` field) and the `X-Request-ID` HTTP header:
 638: 
 639: ```python
 640: import uuid
 641: 
 642: def ensure_request_id(body: dict) -> str:
 643:     return body.get("request_id") or str(uuid.uuid4())
 644: ```
 645: 
 646: ### 11.2 Trace Entry Schema
 647: 
 648: **Authoritative schema:** Each trace entry has `service` (string),
 649: `event` (string), `ts` (ISO 8601 datetime string, UTC), and optional
 650: `meta` (object). Every service appends exactly one trace entry per
 651: request. Use a shared Pydantic model so all services produce consistent
 652: structure:
 653: 
 654: ```python
 655: from datetime import datetime, timezone
 656: from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
 657: from pydantic import BaseModel
 658: 
 659: class TraceEntry(BaseModel):
 660:     service: str
 661:     event: str
 662:     ts: str
 663:     meta: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
 664: 
 665: def make_trace_entry(
 666:     service: str,
 667:     event: str,
 668:     meta: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
 669: ) -> TraceEntry:
 670:     return TraceEntry(
 671:         service=service,
 672:         event=event,
 673:         ts=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(
 674:             timespec="milliseconds"
 675:         ),
 676:         meta=meta,
 677:     )
 678: ```
 679: 
 680: This helper can live in a shared utility module or be duplicated in
 681: each service (it is small enough that duplication is acceptable for
 682: a project with no shared library).
 683: 
 684: ### 11.3 Trace Aggregation
 685: 
 686: The gateway builds the trace array by collecting `trace_append` entries
 687: from each downstream call and merging them with its own entries:
 688: 
 689: 1. **Client trace** (optional): if the request body includes a `trace`
 690:    array (e.g. the web UI sends `{service: "web", event: "submit"}`),
 691:    start from that array.
 692: 2. **Gateway received**: append when `/api/request` handler begins.
 693: 3. **AI router classified**: append `trace_append` from the `/classify`
 694:    response.
 695: 4. **Backend handled**: append `trace_append` from the `/handle`
 696:    response (only if a backend is called).
 697: 5. **Gateway responded**: append just before returning the final
 698:    response.
 699: 
 700: ### 11.4 Timing Capture
 701: 
 702: Wrap each outbound HTTP call in a timer:
 703: 
 704: ```python
 705: import time
 706: 
 707: async def timed_post(client, url, json_body, headers):
 708:     t0 = time.monotonic()
 709:     resp = await client.post(url, json=json_body, headers=headers)
 710:     elapsed_ms = round((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000)
 711:     return resp, elapsed_ms
 712: ```
 713: 
 714: The gateway returns three timing values in `timings_ms`:
 715: 
 716: - `classify`: wall-clock time for the ai-router `/classify` call.
 717: - `proxy`: wall-clock time for the backend `/handle` call (0 if route
 718:   is unknown).
 719: - `total`: wall-clock from gateway receipt to response.
 720: 
 721: ## 12. AI Router `/classify` Endpoint
 722: <!-- TASKS: 2.4, 2.5 -->
 723: 
 724: Wire the `top_contributing_tokens` helper from Section 8 into a FastAPI
 725: endpoint. The ai-router loads the model once at startup and uses it
 726: for every request.
 727: 
 728: ### 12.1 Pydantic Models
 729: 
 730: ```python
 731: from pydantic import BaseModel
 732: from typing import Any, Dict, List
 733: 
 734: class ClassifyRequest(BaseModel):
 735:     request_id: str
 736:     text: str
 737: 
 738: class ClassifyResponse(BaseModel):
 739:     route: str
 740:     confidence: float
 741:     probabilities: Dict[str, float]
 742:     explanation: str
 743:     top_tokens: List[str]
 744:     trace_append: Dict[str, Any]
 745: ```
 746: 
 747: ### 12.2 Startup Model Loading
 748: 
 749: Use the `load_model()` function from Section 7.5 in the FastAPI
 750: lifespan:
 751: 
 752: ```python
 753: @asynccontextmanager
 754: async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
 755:     vec, mdl, labels = load_model()
 756:     app.state.vectorizer = vec
 757:     app.state.model = mdl
 758:     app.state.labels = labels
 759:     yield
 760: 
 761: app = FastAPI(title="AI Router", lifespan=lifespan)
 762: ```
 763: 
 764: ### 12.3 Endpoint Implementation
 765: 
 766: ```python
 767: @app.post("/classify", response_model=ClassifyResponse)
 768: async def classify(req: ClassifyRequest):
 769:     pred_label, confidence, probs_map, tokens = (
 770:         top_contributing_tokens(
 771:             req.text,
 772:             app.state.vectorizer,
 773:             app.state.model,
 774:             app.state.labels,
 775:             top_n=6,
 776:         )
 777:     )
 778: 
 779:     explanation = (
 780:         f"top tokens: {', '.join(tokens)}" if tokens else ""
 781:     )
 782: 
 783:     trace = make_trace_entry(
 784:         service="ai-router",
 785:         event="classified",
 786:         meta={
 787:             "route": pred_label,
 788:             "confidence": round(confidence, 4),
 789:         },
 790:     )
 791: 
 792:     return ClassifyResponse(
 793:         route=pred_label,
 794:         confidence=round(confidence, 4),
 795:         probabilities={
 796:             k: round(v, 4) for k, v in probs_map.items()
 797:         },
 798:         explanation=explanation,
 799:         top_tokens=tokens,
 800:         trace_append=trace.model_dump(),
 801:     )
 802: ```
 803: 
 804: The response includes the full probability distribution so the gateway
 805: can apply its own threshold and margin policy (Section 6) without
 806: the ai-router making routing decisions.
 807: 
 808: ## 13. Backend Services `/handle` Pattern
 809: <!-- TASKS: 1.5, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4 -->
 810: 
 811: All three backends (search, image, ops) share the same endpoint
 812: contract. Each returns a service-specific simulated payload and a
 813: single trace entry.
 814: 
 815: ### 13.1 Shared Request and Response Models
 816: 
 817: ```python
 818: from pydantic import BaseModel
 819: from typing import Any, Dict
 820: 
 821: class HandleRequest(BaseModel):
 822:     request_id: str
 823:     text: str
 824: 
 825: class HandleResponse(BaseModel):
 826:     payload: Dict[str, Any]
 827:     trace_append: Dict[str, Any]
 828: ```
 829: 
 830: ### 13.2 Endpoint Pattern
 831: 
 832: All three backends follow the same structure. Example for
 833: `search_service`:
 834: 
 835: ```python
 836: import socket
 837: 
 838: @app.post("/handle", response_model=HandleResponse)
 839: async def handle(req: HandleRequest):
 840:     payload = {
 841:         "service": "search-service",
 842:         "result": f"Search results for: {req.text}",
 843:         "instance": socket.gethostname(),
 844:     }
 845: 
 846:     trace = make_trace_entry(
 847:         service="search-service",
 848:         event="handled",
 849:         meta={
 850:             "status": 200,
 851:             "instance": socket.gethostname(),
 852:         },
 853:     )
 854: 
 855:     return HandleResponse(
 856:         payload=payload,
 857:         trace_append=trace.model_dump(),
 858:     )
 859: ```
 860: 
 861: ### 13.3 Per-Service Response Variations
 862: 
 863: Each backend varies the `service` name and `result` content:
 864: 
 865: - **search_service**: returns mock lookup/research response. Payload
 866:   includes a summary string and optionally a list of mock result
 867:   titles.
 868: - **image_service**: returns mock image processing metadata (e.g.
 869:   detected labels, dimensions, format).
 870: - **ops_service**: returns mock troubleshooting steps or diagnostic
 871:   output (e.g. a list of suggested commands or status checks).
 872: 
 873: Include `socket.gethostname()` in both the payload and trace so
 874: scaled replicas are distinguishable in the trace visualization
 875: (the hostname is the container ID prefix in Docker).
 876: 
 877: ## 14. Gateway Service
 878: <!-- TASKS: 1.3, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5, 4.6, 5.7 -->
 879: 
 880: ### 14.1 Dependencies
 881: 
 882: Gateway `requirements.txt`:
 883: 
 884: ```text
 885: fastapi>=0.109.0
 886: gunicorn>=21.0.0
 887: uvicorn[standard]>=0.27.0
 888: httpx>=0.27.0
 889: ```
 890: 
 891: ### 14.2 Pydantic Models
 892: 
 893: ```python
 894: from pydantic import BaseModel
 895: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
 896: 
 897: class ApiRequest(BaseModel):
 898:     request_id: Optional[str] = None
 899:     text: str
 900:     trace: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None
 901: 
 902: class ApiResponse(BaseModel):
 903:     request_id: str
 904:     route: str
 905:     confidence: float
 906:     explanation: str
 907:     trace: List[Dict[str, Any]]
 908:     backend_response: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
 909:     timings_ms: Dict[str, int]
 910: 
 911: class UnknownResponse(BaseModel):
 912:     request_id: str
 913:     route: str
 914:     confidence: float
 915:     message: str
 916:     trace: List[Dict[str, Any]]
 917: ```
 918: 
 919: ### 14.3 `/api/request` Implementation
 920: 
 921: Core orchestration logic (pseudocode-level; see Sections 10-11 for
 922: helpers):
 923: 
 924: ```python
 925: from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
 926: 
 927: @app.post("/api/request")
 928: async def api_request(req: ApiRequest):
 929:     request_id = req.request_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
 930:     trace: List[Dict] = list(req.trace or [])
 931:     t_start = time.monotonic()
 932:     http = app.state.http
 933:     headers = {"X-Request-ID": request_id}
 934: 
 935:     trace.append(
 936:         make_trace_entry("gateway", "received").model_dump()
 937:     )
 938: 
 939:     # 1. Classify
 940:     classify_body = {
 941:         "request_id": request_id,
 942:         "text": req.text,
 943:     }
 944:     classify_resp, classify_ms = await timed_post(
 945:         http, f"{AI_ROUTER_URL}/classify",
 946:         classify_body, headers,
 947:     )
 948:     classify_resp.raise_for_status()
 949:     cdata = classify_resp.json()
 950:     trace.append(cdata["trace_append"])
 951: 
 952:     route = cdata["route"]
 953:     confidence = cdata["confidence"]
 954:     probabilities = cdata.get("probabilities", {})
 955:     explanation = cdata.get("explanation", "")
 956: 
 957:     # 2. Apply routing policy (Section 6)
 958:     effective_route = _apply_policy(route, probabilities)
 959: 
 960:     # 3a. Unknown - return 404 without proxy
 961:     if effective_route == "unknown":
 962:         trace.append(make_trace_entry(
 963:             "gateway", "responded",
 964:             meta={"status": 404},
 965:         ).model_dump())
 966:         total_ms = round(
 967:             (time.monotonic() - t_start) * 1000
 968:         )
 969:         return JSONResponse(
 970:             status_code=404,
 971:             content=UnknownResponse(
 972:                 request_id=request_id,
 973:                 route="unknown",
 974:                 confidence=confidence,
 975:                 message=(
 976:                     "Unable to determine a suitable backend"
 977:                     " for this request"
 978:                 ),
 979:                 trace=trace,
 980:             ).model_dump(),
 981:         )
 982: 
 983:     # 3b. Proxy to backend
 984:     backend_url = ROUTE_MAP.get(effective_route)
 985:     handle_body = {
 986:         "request_id": request_id,
 987:         "text": req.text,
 988:     }
 989:     handle_resp, proxy_ms = await timed_post(
 990:         http, f"{backend_url}/handle",
 991:         handle_body, headers,
 992:     )
 993:     handle_resp.raise_for_status()
 994:     hdata = handle_resp.json()
 995:     trace.append(hdata["trace_append"])
 996:     backend_response = hdata.get("payload")
 997: 
 998:     trace.append(make_trace_entry(
 999:         "gateway", "responded",
1000:         meta={"status": 200},
1001:     ).model_dump())
1002:     total_ms = round(
1003:         (time.monotonic() - t_start) * 1000
1004:     )
1005: 
1006:     return ApiResponse(
1007:         request_id=request_id,
1008:         route=effective_route,
1009:         confidence=confidence,
1010:         explanation=explanation,
1011:         trace=trace,
1012:         backend_response=backend_response,
1013:         timings_ms={
1014:             "classify": classify_ms,
1015:             "proxy": proxy_ms,
1016:             "total": total_ms,
1017:         },
1018:     )
1019: ```
1020: 
1021: ### 14.4 Routing Policy Helper
1022: 
1023: Combines threshold and margin logic from Section 6 into a single
1024: function called inside `/api/request`:
1025: 
1026: ```python
1027: def _apply_policy(
1028:     raw_route: str,
1029:     probabilities: Dict[str, float],
1030: ) -> str:
1031:     if raw_route == "unknown":
1032:         return "unknown"
1033: 
1034:     non_unknown = {
1035:         k: v for k, v in probabilities.items()
1036:         if k != "unknown"
1037:     }
1038:     if not non_unknown:
1039:         return "unknown"
1040: 
1041:     sorted_routes = sorted(
1042:         non_unknown.items(),
1043:         key=lambda x: x[1],
1044:         reverse=True,
1045:     )
1046:     best_route, p_best = sorted_routes[0]
1047:     p_second = (
1048:         sorted_routes[1][1] if len(sorted_routes) > 1 else 0.0
1049:     )
1050: 
1051:     if p_best < T_ROUTE:
1052:         return "unknown"
1053:     if (p_best - p_second) < T_MARGIN:
1054:         return "unknown"
1055: 
1056:     return best_route
1057: ```
1058: 
1059: ### 14.5 Static UI Serving
1060: 
1061: Serve the web UI from the gateway using FastAPI's `StaticFiles` and a
1062: `FileResponse` for the root path:
1063: 
1064: ```python
1065: from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
1066: from fastapi.responses import FileResponse
1067: 
1068: STATIC_DIR = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "static")
1069: 
1070: app.mount(
1071:     "/static",
1072:     StaticFiles(directory=STATIC_DIR),
1073:     name="static",
1074: )
1075: 
1076: @app.get("/")
1077: async def root():
1078:     return FileResponse(
1079:         os.path.join(STATIC_DIR, "index.html")
1080:     )
1081: ```
1082: 
1083: Static files live in `services/gateway/app/static/`:
1084: 
1085: ```text
1086: services/gateway/app/static/
1087:   base.css          # shared base styles
1088:   index.html        # query page (default)
1089:   query.js          # query page logic
1090:   query.css         # query page styles
1091:   train.html        # train page
1092:   train.js          # train page logic
1093:   train.css         # train page styles
1094:   refine.html       # refine page
1095:   refine.js         # refine page logic
1096:   refine.css        # refine page styles
1097:   utils.js          # shared utilities (escapeHtml, buildMetricsDOM)
1098: ```
1099: 
1100: Mount `/static` before defining API routes so asset requests
1101: are served directly. The `GET /` route returns `index.html`
1102: (query page); `train.html` and `refine.html` are served at
1103: their respective paths. `utils.js` is shared by both the
1104: train and refine pages for metrics rendering.
1105: 
1106: ### 14.6 `/routes` Endpoint
1107: 
1108: Returns the mapping of route labels to backend URLs so the UI or
1109: operators can inspect available routes:
1110: 
1111: ```python
1112: @app.get("/routes")
1113: async def routes():
1114:     return {
1115:         "routes": [
1116:             {"label": k, "backend_url": v}
1117:             for k, v in ROUTE_MAP.items()
1118:         ]
1119:     }
1120: ```
1121: 
1122: ## 15. AI Router Dockerfile
1123: <!-- TASKS: 2.3, 2.4, 6.2, 6.3 -->
1124: 
1125: The ai_router Dockerfile is a standard two-stage build. It copies a
1126: pre-built `model.joblib` from the build context; training is handled
1127: separately by the trainer service (Section 7).
1128: 
1129: ```dockerfile
1130: ARG PY_IMAGE=python:3.12-slim-bookworm
1131: 
1132: # Stage 1: Build wheels
1133: FROM ${PY_IMAGE} AS builder
1134: WORKDIR /build
1135: COPY requirements.txt .
1136: RUN pip wheel --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt -w /wheels
1137: 
1138: # Stage 2: Runtime
1139: FROM ${PY_IMAGE} AS runtime
1140: ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
1141: 
1142: RUN groupadd -r appgroup \
1143:     && useradd -r -g appgroup -m -d /home/appuser \
1144:        -s /usr/sbin/nologin appuser
1145: 
1146: WORKDIR /app
1147: 
1148: COPY --from=builder /wheels /wheels
1149: COPY requirements.txt .
1150: RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --no-index \
1151:     --find-links=/wheels -r requirements.txt \
1152:     && rm -rf /wheels
1153: 
1154: COPY model/model.joblib ./model/
1155: 
1156: ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
1157: ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
1158: 
1159: COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup app/ ./app/
1160: 
1161: USER appuser
1162: EXPOSE 8000
1163: 
1164: HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s \
1165:     --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
1166:     CMD python -c \
1167:     "import sys,urllib.request; \
1168:      urllib.request.urlopen( \
1169:        'http://127.0.0.1:8000/health', timeout=2 \
1170:      ); sys.exit(0)"
1171: 
1172: CMD ["gunicorn", "app.main:app", \
1173:     "--bind", "0.0.0.0:8000", \
1174:     "--workers", "2", \
1175:     "--worker-class", "uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker", \
1176:     "--access-logfile", "-", \
1177:     "--error-logfile", "-"]
1178: ```
1179: 
1180: `COPY model/model.joblib ./model/` copies the pre-built artifact into
1181: the runtime image at `/app/model/model.joblib`. When the trainer
1182: service is used instead (Section 7), the `MODEL_PATH` env var
1183: overrides this path.
1184: 
1185: ## 16. Docker Compose: Dependencies and Dev Overlay
1186: <!-- TASKS: 1.2, 1.6, 6.4, 6.5 -->
1187: 
1188: ### 16.1 Service Dependencies
1189: 
1190: Add `depends_on` to the gateway so it waits for healthy downstreams
1191: before accepting traffic:
1192: 
1193: ```yaml
1194: services:
1195:   gateway:
1196:     depends_on:
1197:       ai_router:
1198:         condition: service_healthy
1199:       search_service:
1200:         condition: service_healthy
1201:       image_service:
1202:         condition: service_healthy
1203:       ops_service:
1204:         condition: service_healthy
1205:     environment:
1206:       <<: *common-env
1207:       AI_ROUTER_URL: "http://ai_router:8000"
1208:       SEARCH_SERVICE_URL: "http://search_service:8000"
1209:       IMAGE_SERVICE_URL: "http://image_service:8000"
1210:       OPS_SERVICE_URL: "http://ops_service:8000"
1211:       T_ROUTE: "0.60"
1212:       T_MARGIN: "0.10"
1213: ```
1214: 
1215: Service URL env vars are not strictly required (the defaults in code
1216: match Compose service names), but making them explicit aids clarity
1217: and allows overriding in non-Compose environments.
1218: 
1219: Centralized configuration for paths, Redis URL, Ollama pool, and refiner
1220: parameters lives in `config/PROJECT_CONFIG.yaml` and is materialized into
1221: flat env files (for example `env/.env.<env>`) via `scripts/generate_env.py`;
1222: Docker Compose references these files with `env_file`. See
1223: `CONFIGURATION.md` for the configuration workflow.
1224: 
1225: ### 16.2 Dev Overlay
1226: 
1227: `compose/docker-compose.dev.yaml` overrides the CMD for hot reload
1228: and adds bind mounts so source changes take effect without rebuilding:
1229: 
1230: ```yaml
1231: services:
1232:   gateway:
1233:     volumes:
1234:       - ../services/gateway/app:/app/app:ro
1235:     command:
1236:       - uvicorn
1237:       - app.main:app
1238:       - --host
1239:       - "0.0.0.0"
1240:       - --port
1241:       - "8000"
1242:       - --reload
1243:     user: root
1244: 
1245:   ai_router:
1246:     volumes:
1247:       - ../services/ai_router/app:/app/app:ro
1248:     command:
1249:       - uvicorn
1250:       - app.main:app
1251:       - --host
1252:       - "0.0.0.0"
1253:       - --port
1254:       - "8000"
1255:       - --reload
1256:     user: root
1257: 
1258:   search_service:
1259:     volumes:
1260:       - ../services/search_service/app:/app/app:ro
1261:     command:
1262:       - uvicorn
1263:       - app.main:app
1264:       - --host
1265:       - "0.0.0.0"
1266:       - --port
1267:       - "8000"
1268:       - --reload
1269:     user: root
1270: 
1271:   image_service:
1272:     volumes:
1273:       - ../services/image_service/app:/app/app:ro
1274:     command:
1275:       - uvicorn
1276:       - app.main:app
1277:       - --host
1278:       - "0.0.0.0"
1279:       - --port
1280:       - "8000"
1281:       - --reload
1282:     user: root
1283: 
1284:   ops_service:
1285:     volumes:
1286:       - ../services/ops_service/app:/app/app:ro
1287:     command:
1288:       - uvicorn
1289:       - app.main:app
1290:       - --host
1291:       - "0.0.0.0"
1292:       - --port
1293:       - "8000"
1294:       - --reload
1295:     user: root
1296: ```
1297: 
1298: Launch with dev overlay:
1299: 
1300: ```bash
1301: docker compose \
1302:   -f compose/docker-compose.yaml \
1303:   -f compose/docker-compose.dev.yaml up --build
1304: ```
1305: 
1306: The `user: root` override is needed because bind mounts may have
1307: different ownership than the `appuser` created in the Dockerfile.
1308: uvicorn `--reload` watches for file changes and restarts the worker.
1309: 
1310: ## 17. Web UI
1311: <!-- TASKS: 5.1, 5.2, 5.3, 5.4, 5.5, 5.6, 5.7 -->
1312: 
1313: ### 17.1 File Structure
1314: 
1315: Static files in `services/gateway/app/static/`:
1316: 
1317: - `index.html` - query page layout, input form, result display.
1318: - `query.js` - query fetch logic, trace rendering, hop diagram.
1319: - `query.css` - query page styles.
1320: - `train.html` - train page layout and metrics display.
1321: - `train.js` - train trigger and metrics rendering.
1322: - `train.css` - train page styles.
1323: - `refine.html` - refine page (relabel, augment, promote).
1324: - `refine.js` - refine trigger and comparison rendering.
1325: - `refine.css` - refine page styles.
1326: - `base.css` - shared base styles across all pages.
1327: - `utils.js` - shared utilities (`escapeHtml`, `buildMetricsDOM`)
1328:   used by both train and refine pages.
1329: 
1330: ### 17.2 Core JavaScript
1331: 
1332: The UI generates a `request_id`, sends the request, and renders the
1333: response:
1334: 
1335: ```javascript
1336: async function submitRequest(text) {
1337:   const requestId = crypto.randomUUID();
1338:   const body = {
1339:     request_id: requestId,
1340:     text: text,
1341:     trace: [{
1342:       service: "web",
1343:       event: "submit",
1344:       ts: new Date().toISOString(),
1345:     }],
1346:   };
1347: 
1348:   const resp = await fetch("/api/request", {
1349:     method: "POST",
1350:     headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
1351:     body: JSON.stringify(body),
1352:   });
1353: 
1354:   const data = await resp.json();
1355:   renderResult(data, resp.status);
1356: }
1357: ```
1358: 
1359: Handle both 200 (successful route) and 404 (unknown) responses. The
1360: JSON body is present in both cases; the 404 body uses the
1361: `UnknownResponse` schema (Section 14.2).
1362: 
1363: ### 17.3 Rendering Components
1364: 
1365: Display sections (see PROJECT_PLAN Section 7.1 for requirements):
1366: 
1367: - **Route card**: route label, confidence bar (0.0 - 1.0), explanation
1368:   tokens as tags or inline text.
1369: - **Trace timeline**: ordered list of trace entries with service name,
1370:   event, timestamp, and metadata. Highlight the active hop.
1371: - **Hop diagram**: text or SVG rendering of the service chain, e.g.
1372:   `web -> gateway -> ai-router -> gateway -> ops-service -> gateway`.
1373:   Build from the `trace` array by extracting service names in order.
1374: - **Backend response**: formatted JSON payload (hidden when route is
1375:   unknown).
1376: - **Timings**: classify, proxy, and total in milliseconds.
1377: - **Unknown handling**: when `route == "unknown"` or HTTP 404, show the
1378:   `message` field, hide backend payload, and indicate no backend hop in
1379:   the diagram.
1380: 
1381: ## 18. Scaling and DNS Round-Robin
1382: <!-- TASKS: 6.6, 7.2 -->
1383: 
1384: ### 18.1 How It Works
1385: 
1386: When a backend is scaled with `docker compose up --scale
1387: search_service=3`, Compose registers multiple container IPs under the
1388: same DNS name (`search_service`). Docker's embedded DNS resolves the
1389: name to all IPs in round-robin order, so
1390: `httpx.AsyncClient.post("http://search_service:8000/handle", ...)`
1391: distributes requests across replicas automatically.
1392: 
1393: No gateway-side load balancing logic is required.
1394: 
1395: ### 18.2 Identifying Replicas in Traces
1396: 
1397: Each backend includes `socket.gethostname()` in its trace entry and
1398: payload (Section 13.2). In a scaled deployment, each container gets a
1399: unique hostname (the container ID prefix), making it visible in the
1400: trace which replica handled the request.
1401: 
1402: ### 18.3 Scaling Command
1403: 
1404: ```bash
1405: docker compose -f compose/docker-compose.yaml \
1406:   up -d --scale search_service=3
1407: ```
1408: 
1409: Then send multiple requests and observe the `instance` field in traces
1410: cycling through different container hostnames.
1411: 
1412: ## 19. Error Handling: Unknown vs Backend Failure
1413: <!-- TASKS: 4.3, 8.2, 8.4, 8.5 -->
1414: 
1415: The gateway produces two distinct error responses. The trace shape
1416: and HTTP status differ so the UI can distinguish them.
1417: 
1418: ### 19.1 Unknown Classification (AI Decision)
1419: 
1420: - **Cause**: model returns `unknown`, or confidence below `T_ROUTE`,
1421:   or margin below `T_MARGIN`.
1422: - **Gateway behavior**: does not proxy to any backend.
1423: - **HTTP status**: 404.
1424: - **Trace shape**: gateway-received, ai-router-classified,
1425:   gateway-responded. No backend hop.
1426: - **Response body**: `UnknownResponse` (Section 14.2) with `message`.
1427: 
1428: ### 19.2 Backend Failure (Infrastructure)
1429: 
1430: - **Cause**: ai-router classifies to a known route, but the backend
1431:   is down, unreachable, or times out.
1432: - **Gateway behavior**: catches the proxy error.
1433: - **HTTP status**: 502.
1434: - **Trace shape**: gateway-received, ai-router-classified,
1435:   gateway-responded with `meta.error` and `meta.status: 502`. No
1436:   backend-handled entry.
1437: - **Response body**: includes `route` (the intended backend), the
1438:   classification trace, and an error message about unavailability.
1439: 
1440: Implementation in the proxy section of `/api/request`:
1441: 
1442: ```python
1443: try:
1444:     handle_resp, proxy_ms = await timed_post(
1445:         http, f"{backend_url}/handle",
1446:         handle_body, headers,
1447:     )
1448:     handle_resp.raise_for_status()
1449: except (
1450:     httpx.ConnectError,
1451:     httpx.TimeoutException,
1452: ) as exc:
1453:     trace.append(make_trace_entry(
1454:         "gateway", "responded",
1455:         meta={"status": 502, "error": str(exc)},
1456:     ).model_dump())
1457:     total_ms = round(
1458:         (time.monotonic() - t_start) * 1000
1459:     )
1460:     return JSONResponse(
1461:         status_code=502,
1462:         content={
1463:             "request_id": request_id,
1464:             "route": effective_route,
1465:             "confidence": confidence,
1466:             "message": (
1467:                 f"Backend {effective_route} unavailable"
1468:             ),
1469:             "trace": trace,
1470:             "timings_ms": {
1471:                 "classify": classify_ms,
1472:                 "proxy": proxy_ms,
1473:                 "total": total_ms,
1474:             },
1475:         },
1476:     )
1477: ```
1478: 
1479: ### 19.3 AI Router Failure
1480: 
1481: If the ai-router itself is unreachable, the gateway cannot classify
1482: and should return HTTP 503:
1483: 
1484: ```python
1485: try:
1486:     classify_resp, classify_ms = await timed_post(
1487:         http, f"{AI_ROUTER_URL}/classify",
1488:         classify_body, headers,
1489:     )
1490:     classify_resp.raise_for_status()
1491: except (
1492:     httpx.ConnectError,
1493:     httpx.TimeoutException,
1494: ) as exc:
1495:     trace.append(make_trace_entry(
1496:         "gateway", "responded",
1497:         meta={
1498:             "status": 503,
1499:             "error": "AI router unavailable",
1500:         },
1501:     ).model_dump())
1502:     return JSONResponse(
1503:         status_code=503,
1504:         content={
1505:             "request_id": request_id,
1506:             "route": None,
1507:             "message": (
1508:                 "Classification service unavailable"
1509:             ),
1510:             "trace": trace,
1511:         },
1512:     )
1513: ```
1514: 
1515: ### 19.4 Summary Table
1516: 
1517: | Scenario | HTTP | Trace backend hop | Cause |
1518: | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- |
1519: | Unknown classification | 404 | None | AI decision |
1520: | Low confidence / low margin | 404 | None | Policy decision |
1521: | Backend unreachable | 502 | None (attempted) | Infrastructure |
1522: | AI router unreachable | 503 | None | Infrastructure |
1523: | Successful route | 200 | Yes | Normal flow |
1524: 
1525: ## 20. Structured Logging
1526: <!-- TASKS: 8.3 -->
1527: 
1528: ### 20.1 Logger Configuration
1529: 
1530: Use Python `logging` with JSON-structured output so Docker log drivers
1531: can parse fields. A minimal config shared across services:
1532: 
1533: ```python
1534: import json
1535: import logging
1536: import sys
1537: 
1538: class JsonFormatter(logging.Formatter):
1539:     def format(self, record):
1540:         obj = {
1541:             "ts": self.formatTime(record, self.datefmt),
1542:             "level": record.levelname,
1543:             "service": record.name,
1544:             "msg": record.getMessage(),
1545:         }
1546:         if hasattr(record, "request_id"):
1547:             obj["request_id"] = record.request_id
1548:         return json.dumps(obj)
1549: 
1550: def setup_logging(
1551:     service_name: str, level: str = "INFO",
1552: ):
1553:     handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
1554:     handler.setFormatter(JsonFormatter())
1555:     logger = logging.getLogger(service_name)
1556:     logger.setLevel(
1557:         getattr(logging, level.upper(), logging.INFO)
1558:     )
1559:     logger.addHandler(handler)
1560:     return logger
1561: ```
1562: 
1563: ### 20.2 Usage in Endpoints
1564: 
1565: Pass `request_id` as an extra field so it appears in every log line:
1566: 
1567: ```python
1568: logger = setup_logging("gateway")
1569: 
1570: logger.info(
1571:     "classify route=%s confidence=%.4f",
1572:     route,
1573:     confidence,
1574:     extra={"request_id": request_id},
1575: )
1576: ```
1577: 
1578: Read `LOG_LEVEL` from the environment (set via `x-common-env` in
1579: Compose) to control verbosity at runtime without code changes.
1580: 
1581: ### 20.3 Log Correlation
1582: 
1583: Every service logs `request_id` on every significant action (request
1584: received, classification done, handle processed, response sent). To
1585: follow a single request through the system:
1586: 
1587: ```bash
1588: docker compose logs -f | grep "request_id.*c7a8"
1589: ```
1590: 
1591: This gives the same end-to-end visibility as the in-response trace,
1592: but via log aggregation rather than the API response.
1593:

1676: 
1677: ### 21.3 Refiner Cross-References
1678: 
1679: The refiner service (offline dataset improvement via local LLM) is documented
1680: separately. See:
1681: 
1682: - [REFINER_PLAN.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PLAN.md) - Conceptual overview
1683: - [REFINER_PRD.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PRD.md) - Requirements

1685: specification
1686: - [REFINER_FLOW.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_FLOW.md) - End-to-end flow
1687: - [AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md](../planning/AUGMENTATION_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md)
1688: Augment quality and promotion tolerance
1689: - [REFINER_TASKS.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_TASKS.md) - Implementation tasks

## File: docs/auxiliary/planning/PROJECT_PLAN.md
````markdown
   1: # Project Plan: Local AI Microservice Mesh
   2: 
   3: ## 1. Project Overview
   4: 
   5: ### 1.1 Goal
   6: 
   7: Build a locally runnable, multi-container microservice system where an AI classifier
   8: selects the backend service for each request. The browser UI displays the exact
   9: path taken (application-level tracing) per action, demonstrating Docker Compose
  10: advanced features and AI-driven routing.
  11: 
  12: ### 1.2 Demo Surface
  13: 
  14: The project must be demonstrable via:
  15: 
  16: - **Browser UI** (primary): Single-page application (Query, Train, Refine) showing
  17:   routing decisions, request traces, and train/refine job results via SSE
  18: - **Terminal commands**: curl-based API testing with trace visibility
  19: - **Scaling scenarios**: Demonstrate load distribution across scaled services
  20: - **Failure scenarios**: Show fallback behavior when backends fail
  21: 
  22: ### 1.3 Technical Requirements
  23: 
  24: - **Platform**: Cross-platform (Linux, Mac, Windows)
  25: - **Language**: Python (FastAPI for all services)
  26: - **AI Model**: scikit-learn (TF-IDF + Logistic Regression) - see Section 5 for
  27: details
  28: - **Tracing**: Application-level tracing (no external observability stack) - see
  29: Section 2.4
  30: - **Docker**: Multi-stage Dockerfiles with pre-built model
  31:   artifacts - see Section 8
  32: - **Docker Compose**: Advanced features including fragments, anchors, profiles,
  33: health checks - see Section 9
  34: 
  35: ## 2. System Architecture
  36: 
  37: ### 2.1 Services
  38: 
  39: 1. **gateway** (FastAPI)
  40:    - Serves static UI at `/` (Query | Train | Refine; Section 7.3)
  41:    - Main API entrypoint: `POST /api/request`
  42:    - Calls `ai-router` to classify requests; proxies to backends and
  43:      training-api. See [ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md).
  44:    - Aggregates and returns full request trace
  45:    - Implements confidence thresholding and fallback logic (see Section 2.3)
  46: 
  47: 2. **ai-router** (FastAPI)
  48:    - Endpoint: `POST /classify`
  49:    - Runs lightweight text classifier (see Section 5 for model details)
  50:    - Returns route, confidence score, and explanation (top weighted features)
  51:    - Loads pre-built model artifact at startup
  52: 
  53: 3. **search-service** (FastAPI)
  54:    - Endpoint: `POST /handle`
  55:    - Simulates information lookup/research responses
  56: 
  57: 4. **image-service** (FastAPI)
  58:    - Endpoint: `POST /handle`
  59:    - Simulates image-related request handling
  60: 
  61: 5. **ops-service** (FastAPI)
  62:    - Endpoint: `POST /handle`
  63:    - Simulates DevOps/infrastructure troubleshooting responses
  64: 
  65: 6. **trainer** (one-shot container, no FastAPI)
  66:    - Invoked by training-api or directly via profile. See Section 8.3,
  67:      Section 9.3, and [ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md).
  68: 
  69: 7. **refiner** (one-shot container, no FastAPI)
  70:    - Invoked by training-api or directly via profile; promotion via
  71:      training-api or `scripts/promote.sh`. See
  72:      [REFINER_PLAN.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_PLAN.md),
  73:      [REFINER_TECHNICAL.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_TECHNICAL.md),
  74:      [REFINER_FLOW.md](docs/auxiliary/refiner/REFINER_FLOW.md).
  75: 
  76: 8. **training-api** (FastAPI)
  77:    - Train, refine, promote (HTTP + CLI). See
  78:      [TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH.md),
  79:      [ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md).
  80: 
  81: 9. **Redis**
  82:    - Job state and Pub/Sub for training-api. See
  83:      [TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/TRAIN_AND_REFINE_GUI_PAGES_TECH.md),
  84:      [ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/auxiliary/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md).
  85: 
  86: ### 2.2 Request Flow
  87: 
  88: 1. User submits request via UI or curl → `gateway /api/request`
  89: 2. Gateway calls → `ai-router /classify` with request text
  90: 3. AI router returns → `{route, confidence, explanation, trace_append}`
  91: 4. Gateway evaluates confidence threshold:
  92:    - If route is `unknown` → return 404-equivalent response
  93:    - If confidence below threshold → treat as `unknown`
  94:    - Otherwise → proxy to selected backend `/handle`
  95: 5. Backend returns → `{payload, trace_append}`
  96: 6. Gateway aggregates trace and returns → complete response with routing metadata
  97: 
  98: ### 2.3 Routing Model
  99: 
 100: **Routes (4 labels):**
 101: 
 102: - `search-service`: Lookup, explanation, comparison queries
 103: - `image-service`: Image-related intents (detection, processing, etc.)
 104: - `ops-service`: DevOps, infrastructure, troubleshooting queries
 105: - `unknown`: Out-of-scope, unclear, or irrelevant requests → returns 404-equivalent
 106: 
 107: **Routing Policy:**
 108: 
 109: - Model produces probabilities for all 4 labels
 110: - Gateway uses confidence threshold (`T_route`; runtime default 0.60 from
 111:   generated env config,
 112:   code fallback 0.55) for non-unknown routes
 113: - Optional margin check: if difference between top two routes < threshold → treat
 114: as unknown
 115: - If route is `unknown` or confidence too low → return 404 without proxying to backend
 116: 
 117: ### 2.4 Application-Level Tracing
 118: 
 119: #### 2.4.1 Trace Contract
 120: 
 121: - `request_id` (UUID) generated by gateway if not provided, propagated end-to-end
 122: via headers and body
 123: - Each service appends exactly one trace entry per request
 124: - Gateway aggregates all entries into single `trace` array
 125: - Trace returned to UI/client in gateway response
 126: 
 127: #### 2.4.2 Trace Entry Schema
 128: 
 129: ```json
 130: {
 131:   "service": "gateway",
 132:   "event": "received",
 133:   "ts": "2026-02-11T19:12:01.210Z",
 134:   "meta": {
 135:     "detail": "...",
 136:     "route": "ops-service",
 137:     "confidence": 0.87,
 138:     "status": 200
 139:   }
 140: }
 141: ```
 142: 
 143: #### 2.4.3 Gateway Response Schema
 144: 
 145: Trace entries follow the schema defined in Section 2.4.2.
 146: 
 147: ```json
 148: {
 149:   "request_id": "c7a8...",
 150:   "route": "ops-service",
 151:   "confidence": 0.87,
 152:   "explanation": "top tokens: kubectl, pod, crashloop",
 153:   "trace": [
 154:     {"service": "web", "event": "submit", "ts": "..."},
 155:     {"service": "gateway", "event": "received", "ts": "..."},
 156:     {"service": "ai-router", "event": "classified", "ts": "...", "meta": {"route": "ops-service", "confidence": 0.87}},
 157:     {"service": "ops-service", "event": "handled", "ts": "...", "meta": {"status": 200}},
 158:     {"service": "gateway", "event": "responded", "ts": "...", "meta": {"status": 200}}
 159:   ],
 160:   "backend_response": {
 161:     "payload": {}
 162:   },
 163:   "timings_ms": {
 164:     "classify": 12,
 165:     "proxy": 18,
 166:     "total": 35
 167:   }
 168: }
 169: ```
 170: 
 171: #### 2.4.4 Unknown Route Response (404-equivalent)
 172: 
 173: Trace entries follow the schema defined in Section 2.4.2. See Section 2.3 for
 174: routing policy.
 175: 
 176: ```json
 177: {
 178:   "request_id": "c7a8...",
 179:   "route": "unknown",
 180:   "confidence": 0.81,
 181:   "message": "Unable to determine a suitable backend for this request",
 182:   "trace": [
 183:     {"service": "web", "event": "submit", "ts": "..."},
 184:     {"service": "gateway", "event": "received", "ts": "..."},
 185:     {"service": "ai-router", "event": "classified", "ts": "...", "meta": {"route": "unknown", "confidence": 0.81}},
 186:     {"service": "gateway", "event": "responded", "ts": "...", "meta": {"status": 404}}
 187:   ]
 188: }
 189: ```
 190: 
 191: ## 3. Repository Structure
 192: 
 193: ```bash
 194: docker-compose-ai-gateway/
 195: ├── compose/
 196: │   ├── docker-compose.yaml          # Base configuration (uses env/.env.<env> via env_file)
